handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:561:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:42:**3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a TaskBankruptcy-style resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? (Must be rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`.)**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:563:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:46:*   Any other combination on a resolved market, such as `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)`, falls through to the wildcard arm which returns `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:581:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:116:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:582:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:118:*   **Scrutiny**: The `ResolutionRef` in `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1044` points to a single `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1964` checks if this `tx_id` corresponds to a `TaskBankruptcyTx` or a `FinalizeRewardTx`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:583:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:120:*   **Verdict**: **CHALLENGE**. This is not a blocker for TB-13, but the architectural roadmap for TB-15 must explicitly address how `ResolutionRef` will evolve to handle more complex resolution scenarios. The current schema correctly implements the narrow TB-13 mandate.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:1447:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:1457:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:72:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:1458:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:2096:handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md:19:**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:5925:    19	**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:7800:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4486:**RQ1 — Anti-forgery enforcement**: A malformed CompleteSetRedeemTx where wire `outcome` differs from `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` — does the dispatch arm reject pre-state-lookup? handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8036:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6537:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8039:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6540:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8082:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6596:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:9017:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:582:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:118:*   **Scrutiny**: The `ResolutionRef` in `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1044` points to a single `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1964` checks if this `tx_id` corresponds to a `TaskBankruptcyTx` or a `FinalizeRewardTx`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:9397:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:43:**Finding**: PASS. No. The dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1889-1899` contains an explicit `match` on `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`. A `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)` pair correctly falls through to the `InvalidResolutionRef` rejection path. This is confirmed by integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:9401:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:74:**Finding**: CHALLENGE. The current `ResolutionRef` model at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1120` and its validation logic in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` are tightly coupled to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). If TB-15+ introduces new resolution mechanisms (e.g., `ChallengeCourtRulingTx`, `OracleUpdateTx`), the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm will require modification to recognize these new transaction types. A more abstract and robust long-term design might involve a canonical `ResolutionsIndex` in `QState` that maps `EventId` to a resolved `OutcomeSide`, decoupling the redemption logic from the specific mechanism of resolution. The current implementation is not a bug but represents an architectural choice that incurs future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:10026:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:10029:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11008:handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md:169:  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11092:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11120:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:423:  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11916:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11965:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1563:   169	  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:12343:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:95:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:12745:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:119:*   **Finding**: The current model assumes a single, canonical resolution transaction for an event. It is not robust to scenarios with multiple valid-but-competing resolutions or partial resolutions. However, this is an explicit simplification for TB-13. The model is additive; extending the `match` in the sequencer to handle new resolution transaction types in TB-15 is straightforward. The risk is that the *concept* of a single `resolution_tx_id` may be insufficient, but this is a TB-15 design problem, not a TB-13 implementation flaw.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:12746:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:120:*   **Verdict**: **CHALLENGE**. This is not a blocker for TB-13, but the architectural roadmap for TB-15 must explicitly address how `ResolutionRef` will evolve to handle more complex resolution scenarios. The current schema correctly implements the narrow TB-13 mandate.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:13078:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:27:However, the audit identifies one non-blocking but significant architectural risk concerning the forward-compatibility of the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12), which warrants a **CHALLENGE**. The current model tightly couples redemption to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`), which may prove brittle in TB-15+ multi-resolver scenarios. While not a halting trigger for TB-13, this design choice introduces future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:13082:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:87:**Rationale**: The implementation of TB-13 is sound and passes all critical safety checks (Q1-Q9) in its remediated form. However, the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12) introduces a point of future architectural friction. While not a VETO-level flaw for TB-13, it is a significant enough design choice to warrant a CHALLENGE. The architect should acknowledge this future refactoring cost. The recommended "fix" is to add an OBS (observation) to the project backlog to track the redesign of the resolution mechanism for TB-15, rather than blocking the TB-13 ship. With this acknowledgment, the project can proceed.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:13876:- TB13-RQ5 CHALLENGE: `resolution_tx_id` is not validated, which is not a money exploit because `task_markets_t.state` is the live truth, but source docs still promise L4 resolution lookup. Either validate it or explicitly document it as opaque/non-authoritative. Refs: [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1705), [typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1124), [typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1163), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:86).

Either validate it or explicitly document it as opaque/non-authoritative. handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:25:| Art. III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:66:| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:67:| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:118:| SG-13.6   redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no                | ✓ pass (exact)      | `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (also covers symmetric Bankrupt → No path + 2 mismatch InvalidResolutionRef checks) |
handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:136:| G9 TransitionError variants: 5 NEW additive                       | ✓ pass     | InsufficientBalanceForMint / RedeemBeforeResolution / RedeemMoreThanOwned / InsufficientCollateral / InvalidResolutionRef. |

handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:42:**3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a TaskBankruptcy-style resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? (Must be rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`.)**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:46:*   Any other combination on a resolved market, such as `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)`, falls through to the wildcard arm which returns `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:116:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:118:*   **Scrutiny**: The `ResolutionRef` in `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1044` points to a single `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1964` checks if this `tx_id` corresponds to a `TaskBankruptcyTx` or a `FinalizeRewardTx`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:119:*   **Finding**: The current model assumes a single, canonical resolution transaction for an event. It is not robust to scenarios with multiple valid-but-competing resolutions or partial resolutions. However, this is an explicit simplification for TB-13. The model is additive; extending the `match` in the sequencer to handle new resolution transaction types in TB-15 is straightforward. The risk is that the *concept* of a single `resolution_tx_id` may be insufficient, but this is a TB-15 design problem, not a TB-13 implementation flaw.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:120:*   **Verdict**: **CHALLENGE**. This is not a blocker for TB-13, but the architectural roadmap for TB-15 must explicitly address how `ResolutionRef` will evolve to handle more complex resolution scenarios. The current schema correctly implements the narrow TB-13 mandate.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:27:However, the audit identifies one non-blocking but significant architectural risk concerning the forward-compatibility of the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12), which warrants a **CHALLENGE**. The current model tightly couples redemption to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`), which may prove brittle in TB-15+ multi-resolver scenarios. While not a halting trigger for TB-13, this design choice introduces future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:42:**3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a TaskBankruptcy-style resolution_ref bypass the outcome check?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:43:**Finding**: PASS. No. The dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1889-1899` contains an explicit `match` on `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`. A `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)` pair correctly falls through to the `InvalidResolutionRef` rejection path. This is confirmed by integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:73:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:74:**Finding**: CHALLENGE. The current `ResolutionRef` model at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1120` and its validation logic in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` are tightly coupled to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). If TB-15+ introduces new resolution mechanisms (e.g., `ChallengeCourtRulingTx`, `OracleUpdateTx`), the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm will require modification to recognize these new transaction types. A more abstract and robust long-term design might involve a canonical `ResolutionsIndex` in `QState` that maps `EventId` to a resolved `OutcomeSide`, decoupling the redemption logic from the specific mechanism of resolution. The current implementation is not a bug but represents an architectural choice that incurs future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:87:**Rationale**: The implementation of TB-13 is sound and passes all critical safety checks (Q1-Q9) in its remediated form. However, the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12) introduces a point of future architectural friction. While not a VETO-level flaw for TB-13, it is a significant enough design choice to warrant a CHALLENGE. The architect should acknowledge this future refactoring cost. The recommended "fix" is to add an OBS (observation) to the project backlog to track the redesign of the resolution mechanism for TB-15, rather than blocking the TB-13 ship. With this acknowledgment, the project can proceed.
handover/alignment/OBS_TB13_AUDIT_RESIDUAL_CHALLENGES_2026-05-03.md:22:- **TB13-RQ5** (Codex): `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` documented as
handover/alignment/OBS_TB13_AUDIT_RESIDUAL_CHALLENGES_2026-05-03.md:25:  declare `resolution_tx_id` OPAQUE traceability metadata, NOT
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:32:**3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a TaskBankruptcy-style resolution_ref bypass the outcome check?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:72:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.

codex
The RQ5 code path now has a single claim source: `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome` is in the signed payload and the dispatch arm compares only `(task_markets_t state, redeem.outcome)`. The SG-13.6 test still covers both mismatch directions.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! TB-13 Atom 0.5 — Legacy CPMM forward-fence + label ship-gate.
     2	//!
     3	//! TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 0.5 (architect 2026-05-03 ruling Part A §4.2;
     4	//! SG-13.0.1 / SG-13.0.2 / SG-13.0.3).
     5	//!
     6	//! These three tests enforce the forward-binding fence that NEW TB-13
     7	//! modules cannot import or reuse the legacy `src/prediction_market.rs`
     8	//! f64 CPMM scaffolding. Architect §4.2 halting triggers:
     9	//!
    10	//!   HALT if new TB-13 code imports legacy prediction_market.rs.
    11	//!   HALT if f64 appears in new CompleteSet / MarketSeed code.
    12	//!   HALT if any AMM / CPMM router function is introduced in TB-13.
    13	//!
    14	//! ## What is "TB-13 code"?
    15	//!
    16	//! A span of Rust source belongs to TB-13 iff it is a contiguous block
    17	//! of non-blank lines whose first non-blank line contains an authoring
    18	//! marker that identifies TB-13 as the contributing tracer-bullet (NOT
    19	//! a forward-reference from an earlier-TB doc-comment to TB-13's future
    20	//! work). Authoring markers:
    21	//!
    22	//!   - `TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 ` (TB-12 convention used by every shipped TB).
    23	//!   - A line that begins with `// TB-13 ` after stripping leading
    24	//!     whitespace + comment markers.
    25	//!   - A line that begins with `//! TB-13 ` (module-level doc).
    26	//!   - A line that begins with `/// TB-13 ` (item-level doc).
    27	//!
    28	//! A span ends at the next blank line OR end-of-file. Cross-references
    29	//! to TB-13 from inside a TB-12 (or earlier) span do NOT pull that span
    30	//! into TB-13 scope — only the *first non-blank line* of a span is
    31	//! checked for the authoring marker.
    32	//!
    33	//! ## File set in scope
    34	//!
    35	//! - `src/state/typed_tx.rs` — TB-13 typed-tx variant additions (Atom 1).
    36	//! - `src/state/q_state.rs` — TB-13 EconomicState extensions (Atom 2).
    37	//! - `src/state/sequencer.rs` — TB-13 dispatch-arm additions (Atom 2).
    38	//! - `src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs` — TB-13 conservation extensions (Atom 3).
    39	//! - `src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs` — TB-13 §14 dashboard rendering (Atom 4).
    40	//!
    41	//! At Atom 0.5 ship time, none of these files contain `TB-13` markers
    42	//! (TB-12 is the latest contributor). The fence passes trivially. As
    43	//! Atom 1..4 land, markers appear and the fence enforces the rule.
    44	
    45	use std::fs;
    46	use std::path::PathBuf;
    47	
    48	/// Statically-listed in-scope source files for the TB-13 forward-fence
    49	/// Layer 1 (unconditional whole-file scan for hard-banned imports).
    50	/// Codex round-2 CHALLENGE remediation 2026-05-03: this list is now
    51	/// a *floor* — `discover_tb_13_files()` walks `src/` for any additional
    52	/// file containing a TB-13 authoring marker and adds it to the
    53	/// effective scope.
    54	const FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR: &[&str] = &[
    55	    "src/state/typed_tx.rs",
    56	    "src/state/q_state.rs",
    57	    "src/state/sequencer.rs",
    58	    "src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs",
    59	    "src/bin/audit_dashboard.rs",
    60	    "src/runtime/verify.rs",
    61	];
    62	
    63	/// Tokens forbidden inside any TB-13-marker span (architect §4.2 halting
    64	/// triggers + §4.7 forbidden list).
    65	///
    66	/// Each entry is a literal substring that must NOT appear in TB-13 code.
    67	const FORBIDDEN_LEGACY_TOKENS: &[&str] = &[
    68	    // Direct legacy CPMM imports / type names.
    69	    "prediction_market::",
    70	    "BinaryMarket",
    71	    // Legacy CPMM API method names.
    72	    ".buy_yes(",
    73	    ".buy_no(",
    74	    "open_bounty_market",
    75	    "bounty_market",
    76	    "bounty_lp_seed",
    77	    "bounty_yes_price",
    78	    "resolve_bounty",
    79	    "market_ticker(",
    80	    "market_ticker_full(",
    81	    // f64 in money-path context (see SG-13.0.2 dedicated test for the
    82	    // primary check; this entry catches `f64` in any TB-13-marked span).
    83	    " f64",
    84	    "f64,",
    85	    "f64;",
    86	    "f64)",
    87	    // Trading / AMM / orderbook concepts forbidden in TB-13 (per §4.7).
    88	    "MarketOrderTx",
    89	    "MarketTradeTx",
    90	    "MarketBuyTx",
    91	    "MarketSellTx",
    92	    "AMM",
    93	    "CPMM",
    94	    "DPMM",
    95	    "orderbook",
    96	    // Price-as-truth concepts (deferred to TB-14 per §5).
    97	    "price_yes",
    98	    "price_no",
    99	    "PriceIndex",
   100	    "yes_price",
   101	    "no_price",
   102	    "RationalPrice",
   103	];
   104	
   105	fn workspace_root() -> PathBuf {
   106	    let manifest = env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR");
   107	    PathBuf::from(manifest)
   108	}
   109	
   110	/// Returns true if `line` is an authoring marker for TB-13 (i.e., the
   111	/// line declares that the following block is TB-13 code, NOT a forward-
   112	/// reference from an earlier-TB doc-comment to TB-13's future work).
   113	fn is_tb_13_authoring_marker(line: &str) -> bool {
   114	    if line.contains("TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 ") {
   115	        return true;
   116	    }
   117	    let trimmed = line.trim_start();
   118	    let body = trimmed
   119	        .strip_prefix("//! ")
   120	        .or_else(|| trimmed.strip_prefix("/// "))
   121	        .or_else(|| trimmed.strip_prefix("// "))
   122	        .unwrap_or("");
   123	    body.starts_with("TB-13 ")
   124	}
   125	
   126	/// Extract line ranges that belong to TB-13 additions. A span is a
   127	/// contiguous block of non-blank lines; it is in-scope iff the first
   128	/// non-blank line is an authoring marker per `is_tb_13_authoring_marker`.
   129	fn tb_13_spans(source: &str) -> Vec<(usize, String)> {
   130	    let mut out: Vec<(usize, String)> = Vec::new();
   131	    let lines: Vec<&str> = source.lines().collect();
   132	    let n = lines.len();
   133	    let mut i = 0;
   134	    while i < n {
   135	        if lines[i].trim().is_empty() {
   136	            i += 1;
   137	            continue;
   138	        }
   139	        let span_start = i;
   140	        let mut span_end = i;
   141	        while span_end < n && !lines[span_end].trim().is_empty() {
   142	            span_end += 1;
   143	        }
   144	        let span = &lines[span_start..span_end];
   145	        if is_tb_13_authoring_marker(span[0]) {
   146	            for (offset, line) in span.iter().enumerate() {
   147	                out.push((span_start + offset + 1, (*line).to_string()));
   148	            }
   149	        }
   150	        i = span_end;
   151	    }
   152	    out
   153	}
   154	
   155	/// Read a source file relative to the workspace root, returning its
   156	/// content as a String. Panics with a clear message if missing — fence
   157	/// requires every file in `FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR` to exist.
   158	fn read_scope_file(rel_path: &str) -> String {
   159	    let full = workspace_root().join(rel_path);
   160	    fs::read_to_string(&full)
   161	        .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!("TB-13 fence: failed to read {rel_path}: {e}"))
   162	}
   163	
   164	/// TB-13 type names — any non-comment use of one of these in `src/`
   165	/// marks the file as a TB-13 contributor. Codex round-3 RQ6 remediation
   166	/// 2026-05-03: the round-2 marker-only discovery caught files whose
   167	/// authors followed the `TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 ` / `// TB-13 ` convention,
   168	/// but a contributor could `use crate::state::typed_tx::CompleteSetMintTx;`
   169	/// in a fresh file without adding a marker — and the fence would miss
   170	/// it. The type-use walk closes that gap by checking for the distinctive
   171	/// TB-13-introduced symbol set itself; the marker discipline becomes a
   172	/// hint for human readers, not the only line of defense.
   173	///
   174	/// All names below are TB-13-introduced and have no pre-existing
   175	/// occurrence in `src/` outside `FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR`.
   176	const TB_13_TYPE_NAMES: &[&str] = &[
   177	    "CompleteSetMintTx",
   178	    "CompleteSetRedeemTx",
   179	    "MarketSeedTx",
   180	    "ConditionalCollateralIndex",
   181	    "ConditionalShareBalances",
   182	    "ShareSidePair",
   183	    "EventNotOpen",
   184	    "EventId",
   185	    "OutcomeSide",
   186	    "ShareAmount",
   187	];
   188	
   189	/// True iff `line` is a pure comment (line starts with `//`, `///`, or
   190	/// `//!` after leading whitespace). Used by type-use discovery to skip
   191	/// legacy doc-comment cross-references (e.g., TB-12 `kernel.rs` /// doc
   192	/// strings that mention `CompleteSetMintTx` as future work).
   193	fn is_pure_comment_line(line: &str) -> bool {
   194	    line.trim_start().starts_with("//")
   195	}
   196	
   197	/// Walk `walk_root` for any `.rs` file containing a TB-13 authoring
   198	/// marker on at least one line. Codex round-2 CHALLENGE remediation
   199	/// 2026-05-03: makes the fence self-extending against marked but
   200	/// not-yet-listed contributors.
   201	fn discover_by_marker(walk_root: &std::path::Path) -> Vec<String> {
   202	    let mut found: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   203	    walk_rs_files(walk_root, &mut |path| {
   204	        let rel = path
   205	            .strip_prefix(workspace_root())
   206	            .unwrap_or(path)
   207	            .to_string_lossy()
   208	            .into_owned();
   209	        let body = match fs::read_to_string(path) {
   210	            Ok(s) => s,
   211	            Err(_) => return,
   212	        };
   213	        if body.lines().any(is_tb_13_authoring_marker) {
   214	            found.push(rel);
   215	        }
   216	    });
   217	    found
   218	}
   219	
   220	/// Walk `walk_root` for any `.rs` file that USES a TB-13 type name
   221	/// (`TB_13_TYPE_NAMES`) on a non-comment line. Codex round-3 RQ6
   222	/// remediation 2026-05-03: catches contributors who imported TB-13
   223	/// types without remembering the authoring-marker convention.
   224	fn discover_by_type_use(walk_root: &std::path::Path) -> Vec<String> {
   225	    let mut found: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   226	    walk_rs_files(walk_root, &mut |path| {
   227	        let rel = path
   228	            .strip_prefix(workspace_root())
   229	            .unwrap_or(path)
   230	            .to_string_lossy()
   231	            .into_owned();
   232	        let body = match fs::read_to_string(path) {
   233	            Ok(s) => s,
   234	            Err(_) => return,
   235	        };
   236	        for line in body.lines() {
   237	            if is_pure_comment_line(line) {
   238	                continue;
   239	            }
   240	            if TB_13_TYPE_NAMES.iter().any(|t| line.contains(t)) {
   241	                found.push(rel);
   242	                return;
   243	            }
   244	        }
   245	    });
   246	    found
   247	}
   248	
   249	/// Discover every TB-13-contributing file in `src/`. Union of
   250	/// marker-walk (round-2) + type-use-walk (round-3 RQ6). Either path
   251	/// alone would leave a loophole; together they enforce the fence even
   252	/// when the human-followed marker convention slips.
   253	fn discover_tb_13_files() -> Vec<String> {
   254	    let src_root = workspace_root().join("src");
   255	    let mut set: std::collections::BTreeSet<String> = std::collections::BTreeSet::new();
   256	    for f in discover_by_marker(&src_root) {
   257	        set.insert(f);
   258	    }
   259	    for f in discover_by_type_use(&src_root) {
   260	        set.insert(f);
   260	        set.insert(f);
   261	    }
   262	    set.into_iter().collect()
   263	}
   264	
   265	fn walk_rs_files(dir: &std::path::Path, visitor: &mut dyn FnMut(&std::path::Path)) {
   266	    let entries = match fs::read_dir(dir) {
   267	        Ok(e) => e,
   268	        Err(_) => return,
   269	    };
   270	    for entry in entries.flatten() {
   271	        let path = entry.path();
   272	        if path.is_dir() {
   273	            walk_rs_files(&path, visitor);
   274	        } else if path.extension().map(|e| e == "rs").unwrap_or(false) {
   275	            visitor(&path);
   276	        }
   277	    }
   278	}
   279	
   280	/// Effective fence scope = FLOOR ∪ discovered. Deduplicated, sorted.
   281	fn effective_fence_scope() -> Vec<String> {
   282	    let mut set: std::collections::BTreeSet<String> = std::collections::BTreeSet::new();
   283	    for s in FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR {
   284	        set.insert((*s).to_string());
   285	    }
   286	    for s in discover_tb_13_files() {
   287	        set.insert(s);
   288	    }
   289	    set.into_iter().collect()
   290	}
   291	
   292	/// Hard-banned legacy CPMM imports — these strings MUST NOT appear in
   293	/// any FENCE_SCOPE file regardless of TB-13-marker discipline. Codex
   294	/// round-1 Q9 CHALLENGE remediation (2026-05-03): the marker-only fence
   295	/// could be bypassed by writing a legacy import outside a TB-13 doc-
   296	/// comment span. These tokens are unconditionally banned (a `use
   297	/// crate::prediction_market::BinaryMarket` anywhere in scope is an
   298	/// architectural regression even in non-TB-13 sections).
   299	const HARD_BANNED_LEGACY_IMPORTS: &[&str] = &[
   300	    "use crate::prediction_market::",
   301	    "use crate::prediction_market;",
   302	    "crate::prediction_market::BinaryMarket",
   303	    "crate::prediction_market::MarketError",
   304	];
   305	
   306	/// SG-13.0.1 — `legacy_cpm_api_not_imported_by_complete_set`.
   307	///
   308	/// Architect §4.2 halting trigger: HALT if NEW TB-13 code imports legacy
   309	/// `prediction_market.rs`. Two layers of enforcement:
   310	///
   311	/// **Layer 1 (unconditional, Codex round-1 Q9 remediation)**: scan every
   312	/// FENCE_SCOPE file for `HARD_BANNED_LEGACY_IMPORTS` regardless of
   313	/// TB-13-marker discipline. Catches any new use-statement or type
   314	/// reference that pulls legacy CPMM into a TB-13-scope module.
   315	///
   316	/// **Layer 2 (TB-13-marker-scoped)**: scan TB-13-marked spans for the
   317	/// broader `FORBIDDEN_LEGACY_TOKENS` set (API names, trading/AMM
   318	/// concepts). The marker discipline allows benign references in
   319	/// historical doc-comments while keeping new TB-13 code clean.
   320	#[test]
   321	fn legacy_cpm_api_not_imported_by_complete_set() {
   322	    let mut violations: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   323	    let scope = effective_fence_scope();
   324	
   325	    // Layer 1: unconditional whole-file scan for hard-banned imports.
   326	    for rel in &scope {
   327	        let source = read_scope_file(rel);
   328	        for (line_no, line) in source.lines().enumerate() {
   329	            for token in HARD_BANNED_LEGACY_IMPORTS {
   330	                if line.contains(token) {
   331	                    violations.push(format!(
   332	                        "{rel}:{}: hard-banned legacy import `{token}` — {line}",
   333	                        line_no + 1
   334	                    ));
   335	                }
   336	            }
   337	        }
   338	    }
   339	
   340	    // Layer 2: TB-13-marker-scoped scan for trading/AMM concepts.
   341	    for rel in &scope {
   342	        let source = read_scope_file(rel);
   343	        for (line_no, line) in tb_13_spans(&source) {
   344	            for token in FORBIDDEN_LEGACY_TOKENS {
   345	                // The `f64` family entries are checked in SG-13.0.2 — skip
   346	                // them here so the failure message is unambiguous.
   347	                if token.starts_with(" f64")
   348	                    || token.starts_with("f64,")
   349	                    || token.starts_with("f64;")
   350	                    || token.starts_with("f64)")
   351	                {
   352	                    continue;
   353	                }
   354	                if line.contains(token) {
   355	                    violations.push(format!(
   356	                        "{rel}:{line_no}: TB-13-marked span contains forbidden token `{token}` — {line}"
   357	                    ));
   358	                }
   359	            }
   360	        }
   361	    }
   362	    assert!(
   363	        violations.is_empty(),
   364	        "TB-13 SG-13.0.1 forward-fence violated (scope: {} files):\n{}",
   365	        scope.len(),
   366	        violations.join("\n")
   367	    );
   368	}
   369	
   370	/// SG-13.0.2 — `no_f64_in_complete_set_or_market_seed`. Now uses
   371	/// effective_fence_scope() (auto-discovers new TB-13 files).
   372	///
   373	/// Architect §4.2 halting trigger: HALT if `f64` appears in NEW
   374	/// CompleteSet / MarketSeed code. Money-path types must use integer
   375	/// `MicroCoin` / `ShareAmount`.
   376	#[test]
   377	fn no_f64_in_complete_set_or_market_seed() {
   378	    let mut violations: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   379	    let f64_tokens = [" f64", "f64,", "f64;", "f64)"];
   380	    for rel in &effective_fence_scope() {
   381	        let source = read_scope_file(rel);
   382	        for (line_no, line) in tb_13_spans(&source) {
   383	            for token in &f64_tokens {
   384	                if line.contains(token) {
   385	                    violations.push(format!(
   386	                        "{rel}:{line_no}: TB-13-marked span contains f64 (`{token}`) — {line}"
   387	                    ));
   388	                }
   389	            }
   390	        }
   391	    }
   392	    assert!(
   393	        violations.is_empty(),
   394	        "TB-13 SG-13.0.2 no-f64-in-money-path violated:\n{}",
   395	        violations.join("\n")
   396	    );
   397	}
   398	
   399	/// SG-13.0.3 — `prediction_market_legacy_quarantined`.
   400	///
   401	/// Architect §4.2 ship gate: legacy CPMM "must be clearly labeled". We
   402	/// enforce that `src/prediction_market.rs` carries the LEGACY module-
   403	/// header doc-comment with the four required tokens (`legacy`,
   404	/// `not constitutional`, `not RSP-M`, `not production market path`)
   405	/// AND that `src/kernel.rs` market-bearing fields carry the `LEGACY`
   406	/// label tying them to the migration path.
   407	#[test]
   408	fn prediction_market_legacy_quarantined() {
   409	    let pm = read_scope_file("src/prediction_market.rs");
   410	    let header = pm
   411	        .lines()
   412	        .take(60)
   413	        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
   414	        .join("\n");
   415	
   416	    let required_label_tokens = [
   417	        "LEGACY",
   418	        "not constitutional",
   419	        "not RSP-M",
   420	        "not production market path",
   421	    ];
   422	    for token in &required_label_tokens {
   423	        assert!(
   424	            header.contains(token),
   425	            "TB-13 SG-13.0.3: src/prediction_market.rs module header missing required \
   426	             label token `{token}`. Header:\n{header}"
   427	        );
   428	    }
   429	
   430	    // Architect §4.2 also requires the doc to name the migration path so
   431	    // future maintainers don't reintroduce the legacy API.
   432	    let migration_tokens = [
   433	        "TB-13",
   434	        "TB-14",
   435	        "CompleteSetMintTx",
   436	        "OBS_TB_12_LEGACY_CPMM_QUARANTINE",
   437	    ];
   438	    for token in &migration_tokens {
   439	        assert!(
   440	            header.contains(token),
   441	            "TB-13 SG-13.0.3: src/prediction_market.rs module header missing migration-path \
   442	             token `{token}`. Header:\n{header}"
   443	        );
   444	    }
   445	
   446	    // Defense-in-depth: kernel.rs market-bearing fields carry LEGACY.
   447	    let kernel = read_scope_file("src/kernel.rs");
   448	    let kernel_struct_idx = kernel
   449	        .find("pub struct Kernel {")
   450	        .expect("TB-13 SG-13.0.3: cannot locate `pub struct Kernel {` in src/kernel.rs");
   451	    let kernel_struct_end = kernel[kernel_struct_idx..]
   452	        .find("\n}\n")
   453	        .map(|off| kernel_struct_idx + off + 2)
   454	        .expect("TB-13 SG-13.0.3: cannot locate end of Kernel struct");
   455	    let kernel_struct_block = &kernel[kernel_struct_idx..kernel_struct_end];
   456	
   457	    for field in ["markets", "bounty_market", "bounty_lp_seed"] {
   458	        let field_marker = format!("pub {field}");
   459	        let field_idx = kernel_struct_block
   460	            .find(&field_marker)
   461	            .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("TB-13 SG-13.0.3: cannot locate field `{field}` in Kernel struct"));
   462	        // Look for `LEGACY` in the 600 chars preceding the field
   463	        // declaration (covers a multi-line doc-comment block).
   464	        let doc_window_start = field_idx.saturating_sub(600);
   465	        let doc_window = &kernel_struct_block[doc_window_start..field_idx];
   466	        assert!(
   467	            doc_window.contains("LEGACY"),
   468	            "TB-13 SG-13.0.3: Kernel.{field} missing LEGACY doc-comment label. \
   469	             Doc window:\n{doc_window}"
   470	        );
   471	    }
   472	}
   473	
   474	/// Round-5 RQ6 unit test: `discover_by_type_use` catches a fresh file
   475	/// that imports a TB-13 type without an authoring marker, and the
   476	/// pure-comment skip prevents a TB-12 doc-comment cross-reference
   477	/// from being misclassified as a TB-13 contributor.
   478	#[test]
   479	fn discover_by_type_use_catches_unmarked_imports_and_skips_doc_xref() {
   480	    use std::io::Write;
   481	    let tmp = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!(
   482	        "tb13_fence_discovery_test_{}",
   483	        std::process::id()
   484	    ));
   485	    let _ = fs::remove_dir_all(&tmp);
   486	    fs::create_dir_all(&tmp).expect("mkdir tmp");
   487	
   488	    // (1) An unmarked file that USES a TB-13 type → must be discovered.
   489	    let unmarked_path = tmp.join("unmarked_user.rs");
   490	    {
   491	        let mut f = fs::File::create(&unmarked_path).expect("create unmarked");
   492	        writeln!(
   493	            f,
   494	            "use crate::state::typed_tx::CompleteSetMintTx;\nfn touch() -> CompleteSetMintTx {{ CompleteSetMintTx::default() }}"
   495	        )
   496	        .unwrap();
   497	    }
   498	
   499	    // (2) A file with TB-13 type names ONLY in /// doc-comment lines →
   500	    // must NOT be discovered (TB-12 legacy doc-xref pattern).
   501	    let docxref_path = tmp.join("doc_xref_only.rs");
   502	    {
   503	        let mut f = fs::File::create(&docxref_path).expect("create docxref");
   504	        writeln!(
   505	            f,
   506	            "/// Replaced by TB-13 `CompleteSetMintTx` (canonical mint).\n//! see ConditionalShareBalances for the future shape.\npub struct Unrelated;"
   507	        )
   508	        .unwrap();
   509	    }
   510	
   511	    // (3) A control file with no TB-13 references → not discovered.
   512	    let neutral_path = tmp.join("neutral.rs");
   513	    {
   514	        let mut f = fs::File::create(&neutral_path).expect("create neutral");
   515	        writeln!(f, "pub fn add(a: i64, b: i64) -> i64 {{ a + b }}").unwrap();
   516	    }
   517	
   518	    let found = discover_by_type_use(&tmp);
   519	    let found_set: std::collections::BTreeSet<&str> =
   520	        found.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect();
   521	
   522	    let unmarked_str = unmarked_path.to_string_lossy().into_owned();
   523	    let docxref_str = docxref_path.to_string_lossy().into_owned();
   524	    let neutral_str = neutral_path.to_string_lossy().into_owned();
   525	
   526	    assert!(
   527	        found_set.contains(unmarked_str.as_str()),
   528	        "RQ6: unmarked TB-13 type-use file must be discovered. Got: {found:?}"
   529	    );
   530	    assert!(
   531	        !found_set.contains(docxref_str.as_str()),
   532	        "RQ6: doc-xref-only file must NOT be discovered. Got: {found:?}"
   533	    );
   534	    assert!(
   535	        !found_set.contains(neutral_str.as_str()),
   536	        "RQ6: neutral file must NOT be discovered. Got: {found:?}"
   537	    );
   538	
   539	    // Also assert that the marker walk alone would have missed (1) —
   540	    // proves type-use is the path that catches it.
   541	    let marker_only = discover_by_marker(&tmp);
   542	    let marker_set: std::collections::BTreeSet<&str> =
   543	        marker_only.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect();
   544	    assert!(
   545	        !marker_set.contains(unmarked_str.as_str()),
   546	        "RQ6: marker walk alone should NOT have caught the unmarked file (otherwise the type-use layer is redundant). Got: {marker_only:?}"
   547	    );
   548	
   549	    let _ = fs::remove_dir_all(&tmp);
   550	}

     1	//! TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ3 remediation: non-empty TB-13 chaintape
     2	//! replay smoke.
     3	//!
     4	//! ## Why this test exists
     5	//!
     6	//! Codex round-3 RQ3 found that the existing real-LLM regression smoke at
     7	//! `handover/evidence/tb_13_real_llm_smoke_2026-05-03/` proves that
     8	//! `EconomicState`'s 13-sub-field schema round-trips with **EMPTY** TB-13
     9	//! maps — the LLM-driven solver path doesn't submit `CompleteSetMint` /
    10	//! `CompleteSetRedeem` / `MarketSeed` (those are user-economic actions,
    11	//! not solver actions). So the smoke's chaintape contains zero TB-13
    12	//! entries, and `verify_chaintape`'s `economic_state_reconstructed: true`
    13	//! indicator only proves the schema-shape round-trip with empty maps.
    14	//!
    15	//! This deterministic non-LLM smoke closes that gap by:
    16	//!
    17	//! 1. Bootstrapping a chain-backed sequencer with `initial_q` containing
    18	//!    pre-seeded balances (alice = 100 Coin), an open task `task-MINT`
    19	//!    (so the Q13 mint gate passes), a finalized task `task-REDEEM` with
    20	//!    pre-seeded YES/NO shares + collateral (so the redeem gate passes).
    21	//! 2. Wiring a real `AgentKeypair` via `AgentKeypairRegistry` (writes
    22	//!    `agent_pubkeys.json` to runtime_repo_path) + `set_agent_pubkeys`
    23	//!    on the sequencer (closes submit-time Class 3 admission control).
    24	//! 3. Submitting a real signed `CompleteSetMintTx` against `task-MINT`
    25	//!    + a real signed `CompleteSetRedeemTx` against `task-REDEEM`. Both
    26	//!    flow through `submit_agent_tx` → driver → Git2LedgerWriter persist.
    27	//! 4. Shutting down the bundle (drains queue) + holding a clone of
    28	//!    `Arc<Sequencer>` to read the post-drain live `q_snapshot()`.
    29	//! 5. Asserting that pre-shutdown live `conditional_collateral_t` and
    30	//!    `conditional_share_balances_t` are NON-EMPTY (sanity).
    31	//! 6. Running `verify_chaintape` on the persisted runtime_repo + cas →
    32	//!    asserting all 7 indicators GREEN, l4_entries ≥ 2, and the
    33	//!    replay-reconstructed `final_state_root_hex` matches the live
    34	//!    `state_root_t` byte-for-byte. Because `state_root_t` is the
    35	//!    SHA-256 chain-fold over the full QState (including TB-13 sub-
    36	//!    fields), state-root equality is cryptographic proof that replay
    37	//!    reconstructed the non-empty TB-13 maps bit-equal to the live
    38	//!    state.
    39	//!
    40	//! ## What this proves
    41	//!
    42	//! - Non-empty `conditional_collateral_t` round-trip via verify_chaintape.
    43	//! - Non-empty `conditional_share_balances_t` round-trip via verify_chaintape.
    44	//! - Submit-time agent signature verification + replay-time Gate 4
    45	//!   coverage for all 3 TB-13 typed-tx variants.
    46	//! - Two-tx state-root chain (initial → mint → redeem) replays
    47	//!   deterministically end-to-end.
    48	//!
    49	//! TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 (Codex RQ3 remediation 2026-05-03;
    50	//! FC3-N1 chaintape replay determinism for non-empty TB-13 maps).
    51	
    52	use std::collections::BTreeMap;
    53	use std::sync::Arc;
    54	
    55	use tempfile::TempDir;
    56	
    57	use turingosv4::economy::money::MicroCoin;
    58	use turingosv4::runtime::agent_keypairs::AgentKeypairRegistry;
    59	use turingosv4::runtime::verify::{verify_chaintape, VerifyOptions};
    60	use turingosv4::runtime::{
    61	    build_chaintape_sequencer_with_initial_q, RuntimeChaintapeConfig,
    62	};
    63	use turingosv4::state::q_state::{
    64	    AgentId, QState, ShareSidePair, TaskId, TaskMarketEntry, TaskMarketState, TxId,
    65	};
    66	use turingosv4::state::sequencer::complete_set_mint_accept_state_root;
    67	use turingosv4::state::typed_tx::{
    68	    AgentSignature, CompleteSetMintTx, CompleteSetRedeemTx, EventId, OutcomeSide,
    69	    ShareAmount, TypedTx,
    70	};
    71	
    72	fn build_smoke_initial_q(
    73	    alice: &str,
    74	    mint_task: &str,
    75	    redeem_task: &str,
    76	    redeem_units: i64,
    77	) -> QState {
    78	    let mut q = QState::genesis();
    79	    let alice_id = AgentId(alice.into());
    80	
    81	    q.economic_state_t
    82	        .balances_t
    83	        .0
    84	        .insert(alice_id.clone(), MicroCoin::from_coin(100).unwrap());
    85	
    86	    let mut mint_entry = TaskMarketEntry::default();
    87	    mint_entry.state = TaskMarketState::Open;
    88	    q.economic_state_t
    89	        .task_markets_t
    90	        .0
    91	        .insert(TaskId(mint_task.into()), mint_entry);
    92	
    93	    let mut redeem_entry = TaskMarketEntry::default();
    94	    redeem_entry.state = TaskMarketState::Finalized;
    95	    q.economic_state_t
    96	        .task_markets_t
    97	        .0
    98	        .insert(TaskId(redeem_task.into()), redeem_entry);
    99	
   100	    // Pre-seed the redeem-task collateral + alice's YES/NO shares so the
   101	    // redeem gate passes. The MIN-balanced invariant holds at
   102	    // min(redeem_units, redeem_units) == collateral.
   103	    let redeem_event = EventId(TaskId(redeem_task.into()));
   104	    q.economic_state_t.conditional_collateral_t.0.insert(
   105	        redeem_event.clone(),
   106	        MicroCoin::from_micro_units(redeem_units),
   107	    );
   108	    let mut alice_shares: BTreeMap<EventId, ShareSidePair> = BTreeMap::new();
   109	    alice_shares.insert(
   110	        redeem_event,
   111	        ShareSidePair {
   112	            yes: ShareAmount::from_units(redeem_units as u128),
   113	            no: ShareAmount::from_units(redeem_units as u128),
   114	        },
   115	    );
   116	    q.economic_state_t
   117	        .conditional_share_balances_t
   118	        .0
   119	        .insert(alice_id, alice_shares);
   120	
   121	    q
   122	}
   123	
   124	#[tokio::test]
   125	async fn rq3_non_empty_tb13_chaintape_replays_with_state_root_match() {
   126	    let tmp = TempDir::new().expect("tempdir");
   127	    let cfg = RuntimeChaintapeConfig {
   128	        runtime_repo_path: tmp.path().join("runtime_repo"),
   129	        cas_path: tmp.path().join("cas"),
   130	        run_id: "rq3-tb13-smoke".to_string(),
   131	        queue_capacity: 16,
   132	    };
   133	
   134	    let alice = "alice";
   135	    let alice_id = AgentId(alice.into());
   136	    let mint_task = "task-rq3-mint";
   137	    let redeem_task = "task-rq3-redeem";
   138	    let mint_amount_micro: i64 = 2_000_000;
   139	    let redeem_units: i64 = 4_000_000;
   140	
   141	    let initial_q =
   142	        build_smoke_initial_q(alice, mint_task, redeem_task, redeem_units);
   143	    let bundle = build_chaintape_sequencer_with_initial_q(&cfg, initial_q)
   144	        .expect("bootstrap chaintape sequencer");
   145	
   146	    // Register alice in an AgentKeypairRegistry rooted at runtime_repo —
   147	    // this writes <runtime_repo>/agent_pubkeys.json which verify_chaintape
   148	    // Gate 4 reads on replay.
   149	    let mut reg = AgentKeypairRegistry::open(&cfg.runtime_repo_path)
   150	        .expect("open agent keypair registry");
   151	    reg.get_or_create(&alice_id)
   152	        .expect("generate alice keypair");
   153	    bundle
   154	        .sequencer
   155	        .set_agent_pubkeys(Arc::new(reg.manifest()))
   156	        .expect("set_agent_pubkeys must succeed once");
   157	
   158	    let initial_root = bundle
   159	        .sequencer
   160	        .q_snapshot()
   161	        .expect("initial q_snapshot")
   162	        .state_root_t;
   163	
   164	    // ── Build + sign mint tx (parent = initial_root) ────────────────────────
   165	    let mint_unsigned = CompleteSetMintTx {
   166	        tx_id: TxId("rq3-mint-1".into()),
   167	        parent_state_root: initial_root,
   168	        event_id: EventId(TaskId(mint_task.into())),
   169	        owner: alice_id.clone(),
   170	        amount: MicroCoin::from_micro_units(mint_amount_micro),
   171	        signature: AgentSignature::from_bytes([0u8; 64]),
   172	        timestamp_logical: 100,
   173	    };
   174	    let mint_digest = mint_unsigned
   175	        .to_signing_payload()
   176	        .canonical_digest();
   177	    let mint_sig = reg.sign(&alice_id, mint_digest).expect("sign mint");
   178	    let mint_tx = TypedTx::CompleteSetMint(CompleteSetMintTx {
   179	        signature: mint_sig,
   180	        ..mint_unsigned
   181	    });
   182	
   183	    // ── Pre-compute the post-mint state_root + build redeem at that parent ──
   184	    //
   185	    // Because the canonical state-root mutator is pure-deterministic in the
   186	    // tx fields, we can pre-compute the parent_state_root the redeem must
   187	    // carry without racing the driver. The dispatcher will compute the same
   188	    // hash when applying the mint; the redeem's parent_state_root then
   189	    // matches q.state_root_t at apply-time (no StaleParent rejection).
   190	    let after_mint_root =
   191	        complete_set_mint_accept_state_root(&initial_root, &mint_tx);
   192	
   193	    let redeem_unsigned = CompleteSetRedeemTx {
   194	        tx_id: TxId("rq3-redeem-1".into()),
   195	        parent_state_root: after_mint_root,
   196	        event_id: EventId(TaskId(redeem_task.into())),
   197	        owner: alice_id.clone(),
   198	        outcome: OutcomeSide::Yes,
   199	        share_amount: ShareAmount::from_units(redeem_units as u128),
   200	        signature: AgentSignature::from_bytes([0u8; 64]),
   201	        timestamp_logical: 101,
   202	    };
   203	    let redeem_digest = redeem_unsigned
   204	        .to_signing_payload()
   205	        .canonical_digest();
   206	    let redeem_sig = reg.sign(&alice_id, redeem_digest).expect("sign redeem");
   207	    let redeem_tx = TypedTx::CompleteSetRedeem(CompleteSetRedeemTx {
   208	        signature: redeem_sig,
   209	        ..redeem_unsigned
   210	    });
   211	
   212	    // ── Submit both; rely on driver+shutdown drain to apply in FIFO order ──
   213	    bundle
   214	        .sequencer
   215	        .submit_agent_tx(mint_tx)
   216	        .await
   217	        .expect("submit mint");
   218	    bundle
   219	        .sequencer
   220	        .submit_agent_tx(redeem_tx)
   220	        .submit_agent_tx(redeem_tx)
   221	        .await
   222	        .expect("submit redeem");
   223	
   224	    // Hold a clone of Arc<Sequencer> across shutdown so we can read live
   225	    // post-drain state. ChaintapeBundle::shutdown consumes self; the
   226	    // Arc keeps the Sequencer alive for our q_snapshot read below.
   227	    let seq_handle = bundle.sequencer.clone();
   228	    bundle.shutdown().await.expect("shutdown drain");
   229	
   230	    let live_q = seq_handle
   231	        .q_snapshot()
   232	        .expect("post-drain q_snapshot");
   233	    let live_state_root = live_q.state_root_t;
   234	
   235	    // Sanity — non-empty TB-13 maps. mint_task added a new collateral entry
   236	    // (size 2: pre-seeded redeem + new mint); alice has shares for both
   237	    // events post-redeem (yes side debited on redeem, no side preserved).
   238	    let collateral_count = live_q.economic_state_t.conditional_collateral_t.0.len();
   239	    let share_owner_count = live_q
   240	        .economic_state_t
   241	        .conditional_share_balances_t
   242	        .0
   243	        .len();
   244	    assert!(
   245	        collateral_count >= 2,
   246	        "expected ≥2 conditional_collateral_t entries (pre-seeded redeem task + mint task); got {collateral_count}"
   247	    );
   248	    assert!(
   249	        share_owner_count >= 1,
   250	        "expected alice in conditional_share_balances_t; got {share_owner_count} owner entries"
   251	    );
   252	
   253	    // Confirm both txs landed by chain-fold position.
   254	    let alice_balance_post = live_q
   255	        .economic_state_t
   256	        .balances_t
   257	        .0
   258	        .get(&alice_id)
   259	        .copied()
   260	        .unwrap()
   261	        .micro_units();
   262	    // Pre-test: 100 Coin = 100_000_000 micro.
   263	    // Post-mint: -2_000_000 (debited for mint).
   264	    // Post-redeem: +4_000_000 (credited for YES redeem).
   265	    // Net: 100_000_000 - 2_000_000 + 4_000_000 = 102_000_000.
   266	    assert_eq!(
   267	        alice_balance_post, 102_000_000,
   268	        "alice balance after mint+redeem must be 100M - 2M + 4M = 102M micro"
   269	    );
   270	
   271	    // ── Replay verification ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   272	    let report = verify_chaintape(
   273	        &cfg.runtime_repo_path,
   274	        &cfg.cas_path,
   275	        &VerifyOptions::default(),
   276	    )
   277	    .expect("verify_chaintape");
   278	
   279	    assert!(
   280	        report.l4_entries >= 2,
   281	        "expected ≥2 L4 entries (mint + redeem); got {}",
   282	        report.l4_entries
   283	    );
   284	    assert!(
   285	        report.all_indicators_pass(),
   286	        "all 7 indicators must pass; report = {report:?}"
   287	    );
   288	    assert!(
   289	        report.detail.initial_q_state_loaded_from_disk,
   290	        "initial_q_state.json must be loaded from disk for replay determinism"
   291	    );
   292	
   293	    // The crucial RQ3 check: replayed final_state_root matches live state_root.
   294	    // state_root is the SHA-256 chain-fold over the entire QState (incl. TB-13
   295	    // sub-fields), so equality is cryptographic proof that replay
   296	    // reconstructed the non-empty conditional_collateral_t and
   297	    // conditional_share_balances_t bit-equal to live state.
   298	    let live_state_root_hex: String = live_state_root
   299	        .0
   300	        .iter()
   301	        .map(|b| format!("{b:02x}"))
   302	        .collect();
   303	    let final_state_root_hex = report
   304	        .detail
   305	        .final_state_root_hex
   306	        .as_ref()
   307	        .expect("final_state_root_hex present after non-empty replay");
   308	    assert_eq!(
   309	        &live_state_root_hex, final_state_root_hex,
   310	        "RQ3: replay state_root must match live state_root → proves non-empty TB-13 maps reconstruct bit-equal"
   311	    );
   312	
   313	    // ── Persist evidence to canonical handover dir (best-effort) ────────────
   314	    //
   315	    // Mirrors TB-7 chain-backed smoke pattern. If the dir is unwritable
   316	    // (CI sandbox), the on-disk witness under TempDir is still authoritative
   317	    // for the test's correctness assertions — evidence dump is forensic.
   318	    let evidence_dir = std::path::Path::new(
   319	        "handover/evidence/tb_13_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-03",
   320	    );
   321	    if std::fs::create_dir_all(evidence_dir).is_ok() {
   322	        let report_json =
   323	            serde_json::to_string_pretty(&report).expect("serialize report");
   324	        let _ = std::fs::write(
   325	            evidence_dir.join("replay_report.json"),
   326	            report_json,
   327	        );
   328	        let agent_pubkeys_src = cfg.runtime_repo_path.join("agent_pubkeys.json");
   329	        if agent_pubkeys_src.exists() {
   330	            let _ = std::fs::copy(
   331	                &agent_pubkeys_src,
   332	                evidence_dir.join("agent_pubkeys.json"),
   333	            );
   334	        }
   335	        let _ = std::fs::write(
   336	            evidence_dir.join("README.md"),
   337	            format!(
   338	                "# TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — non-empty TB-13 chaintape replay smoke\n\
   339	                 \n\
   340	                 **Date**: 2026-05-03\n\
   341	                 **Source**: `tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs::rq3_non_empty_tb13_chaintape_replays_with_state_root_match`\n\
   342	                 **Trigger**: Codex round-3 RQ3 finding — the existing real-LLM smoke at `handover/evidence/tb_13_real_llm_smoke_2026-05-03/` proves EconomicState's 13-sub-field schema round-trips with EMPTY TB-13 maps; non-empty `conditional_collateral_t` / `conditional_share_balances_t` round-trip via `verify_chaintape` was not directly evidenced.\n\
   343	                 \n\
   344	                 ## Headline\n\
   345	                 \n\
   346	                 - L4 entries: {l4} (mint + redeem)\n\
   347	                 - L4.E entries: {l4e}\n\
   348	                 - All 7 ReplayReport indicators GREEN: {all_pass}\n\
   349	                 - Live `state_root_t` (post-drain): `{live_root}`\n\
   350	                 - Replay `final_state_root_hex`: `{replay_root}`\n\
   351	                 - Pre-shutdown `conditional_collateral_t` size: {coll_count}\n\
   352	                 - Pre-shutdown `conditional_share_balances_t` owner count: {owners}\n\
   353	                 \n\
   354	                 ## What this evidence proves (RQ3 closure)\n\
   355	                 \n\
   356	                 1. Two real signed TB-13 typed-tx (CompleteSetMint + CompleteSetRedeem) flow through the full production path: `submit_agent_tx` → driver → `Git2LedgerWriter` persist → on-disk L4 chain.\n\
   357	                 2. Pre-shutdown live state has non-empty TB-13 maps (sanity).\n\
   358	                 3. `verify_chaintape` reconstructs a `QState` from the persisted runtime_repo + cas + initial_q_state.json + agent_pubkeys.json + pinned_pubkeys.json whose `final_state_root_hex` matches the live `state_root_t` byte-for-byte. Because `state_root_t` is the SHA-256 chain-fold over the entire `QState` (including the TB-13 sub-fields), state-root equality is **cryptographic proof** that replay reconstructed the non-empty `conditional_collateral_t` and `conditional_share_balances_t` bit-equal to the live runtime state.\n\
   359	                 4. Submit-time + replay-time agent signature verification is exercised end-to-end for both `CompleteSetMint` and `CompleteSetRedeem` (Gate 4 covers both).\n\
   360	                 5. Two-tx state-root chain (initial → mint → redeem) replays deterministically.\n\
   361	                 \n\
   362	                 ## What is NOT in scope here\n\
   363	                 \n\
   364	                 - **`MarketSeedTx`**: not exercised in this smoke. Coverage lives in `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs::sg_13_3` / `sg_13_4` + canonical encode round-trip in `typed_tx.rs` U3. Adding seed to this smoke would not add chaintape-replay evidence beyond what mint already proves (seed mutates the same maps).\n\
   365	                 - **Resolution mid-test flip**: `task-REDEEM` is pre-seeded as `Finalized` in `initial_q` rather than flipped via a system-emitted `FinalizeReward` / `TaskBankruptcy` mid-test. The state-flip mechanism itself is exercised by TB-8 / TB-11 integration tests; here we focus on the TB-13 mint+redeem chaintape replay determinism.\n\
   366	                 - **Per-tactic decomposition**: per `feedback_chaintape_externalized_proposal`, ChainTape records what the system externalized via `submit_typed_tx`, not private CoT. 1 LLM call → 1 compound payload = 1 Attempt Node remains in effect.\n",
   367	                l4 = report.l4_entries,
   368	                l4e = report.l4e_entries,
   369	                all_pass = report.all_indicators_pass(),
   370	                live_root = live_state_root_hex,
   371	                replay_root = final_state_root_hex,
   372	                coll_count = collateral_count,
   373	                owners = share_owner_count,
   374	            ),
   375	        );
   376	    }
   377	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — non-empty TB-13 chaintape replay smoke
     2	
     3	**Date**: 2026-05-03
     4	**Source**: `tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs::rq3_non_empty_tb13_chaintape_replays_with_state_root_match`
     5	**Trigger**: Codex round-3 RQ3 finding — the existing real-LLM smoke at `handover/evidence/tb_13_real_llm_smoke_2026-05-03/` proves EconomicState's 13-sub-field schema round-trips with EMPTY TB-13 maps; non-empty `conditional_collateral_t` / `conditional_share_balances_t` round-trip via `verify_chaintape` was not directly evidenced.
     6	
     7	## Headline
     8	
     9	- L4 entries: 2 (mint + redeem)
    10	- L4.E entries: 0
    11	- All 7 ReplayReport indicators GREEN: true
    12	- Live `state_root_t` (post-drain): `25fe0968049d243cd6c3189f1dc83fe13f2eeb0f65b9d524f2063cec4af15dc5`
    13	- Replay `final_state_root_hex`: `25fe0968049d243cd6c3189f1dc83fe13f2eeb0f65b9d524f2063cec4af15dc5`
    14	- Pre-shutdown `conditional_collateral_t` size: 2
    15	- Pre-shutdown `conditional_share_balances_t` owner count: 1
    16	
    17	## What this evidence proves (RQ3 closure)
    18	
    19	1. Two real signed TB-13 typed-tx (CompleteSetMint + CompleteSetRedeem) flow through the full production path: `submit_agent_tx` → driver → `Git2LedgerWriter` persist → on-disk L4 chain.
    20	2. Pre-shutdown live state has non-empty TB-13 maps (sanity).
    21	3. `verify_chaintape` reconstructs a `QState` from the persisted runtime_repo + cas + initial_q_state.json + agent_pubkeys.json + pinned_pubkeys.json whose `final_state_root_hex` matches the live `state_root_t` byte-for-byte. Because `state_root_t` is the SHA-256 chain-fold over the entire `QState` (including the TB-13 sub-fields), state-root equality is **cryptographic proof** that replay reconstructed the non-empty `conditional_collateral_t` and `conditional_share_balances_t` bit-equal to the live runtime state.
    22	4. Submit-time + replay-time agent signature verification is exercised end-to-end for both `CompleteSetMint` and `CompleteSetRedeem` (Gate 4 covers both).
    23	5. Two-tx state-root chain (initial → mint → redeem) replays deterministically.
    24	
    25	## What is NOT in scope here
    26	
    27	- **`MarketSeedTx`**: not exercised in this smoke. Coverage lives in `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs::sg_13_3` / `sg_13_4` + canonical encode round-trip in `typed_tx.rs` U3. Adding seed to this smoke would not add chaintape-replay evidence beyond what mint already proves (seed mutates the same maps).
    28	- **Resolution mid-test flip**: `task-REDEEM` is pre-seeded as `Finalized` in `initial_q` rather than flipped via a system-emitted `FinalizeReward` / `TaskBankruptcy` mid-test. The state-flip mechanism itself is exercised by TB-8 / TB-11 integration tests; here we focus on the TB-13 mint+redeem chaintape replay determinism.
    29	- **Per-tactic decomposition**: per `feedback_chaintape_externalized_proposal`, ChainTape records what the system externalized via `submit_typed_tx`, not private CoT. 1 LLM call → 1 compound payload = 1 Attempt Node remains in effect.
