 succeeded in 0ms:
   112	/// architect-mandated indicators are top-level fields; richer detail
   113	/// (final state/ledger root hex, classification of any replay error) is
   114	/// captured under `detail` so downstream tooling can drill in without
   115	/// breaking the headline contract.
   116	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   117	pub struct ReplayReport {
   118	    /// Number of L4 entries (length of `refs/transitions/main`).
   119	    pub l4_entries: u64,
   120	    /// Number of L4.E entries (length of `rejections.jsonl`).
   121	    pub l4e_entries: u64,
   122	    /// True iff every entry's `parent_ledger_root` chains to the previous
   123	    /// `resulting_ledger_root` and the `append()` fold is byte-stable.
   124	    pub ledger_root_verified: bool,
   125	    /// True iff every entry's `system_signature` verifies against the
   126	    /// persisted pinned-pubkey manifest at the entry's epoch.
   127	    pub system_signatures_verified: bool,
   128	    /// True iff replay produced a `QState` (no `dispatch_transition` or
   129	    /// state-root divergence). Empty chain (`l4_entries == 0`) → `true`.
   130	    pub state_reconstructed: bool,
   131	    /// True iff the replayed `QState.economic_state_t` is consistent with
   132	    /// the chain (i.e., replay completed without error). Currently coupled
   133	    /// to `state_reconstructed`; future work may split when economic-only
   134	    /// replay paths are added (NodeMarket, RSP-M).
   135	    pub economic_state_reconstructed: bool,
   136	    /// True iff every L4 entry's `tx_payload_cid` was retrievable from CAS.
   137	    pub cas_payloads_retrievable: bool,
   138	    /// **TB-7 Atom 4 NEW**: True iff every L4 WorkTx / VerifyTx entry's
   139	    /// `AgentSignature` verifies against the per-run `agent_pubkeys.json`
   140	    /// manifest. Empty chain or chain with no Work/Verify entries → `true`
   141	    /// (no agent signatures to verify ≠ failure).
   142	    ///
   143	    /// This is the Gate 4 evidence (TB-7 charter §8): all WorkTx
   144	    /// signatures verify against agent_pubkeys.json. False on any
   145	    /// signature mismatch (tampering, key drift, unknown agent_id).
   146	    pub agent_signatures_verified: bool,
   147	    /// **TB-7 Atom 4 NEW**: True iff every L4 WorkTx entry's
   148	    /// `proposal_cid` resolves to a CAS-resident `ProposalTelemetry`
   149	    /// object. Empty chain or chain with zero Work entries → `true`.
   150	    ///
   151	    /// This is the Gate 5 evidence (TB-7 charter §8): every
   152	    /// `WorkTx.proposal_cid` resolves to a CAS `ProposalTelemetry`
   153	    /// object with the §4.5 schema. False if any WorkTx points to a
   154	    /// CID that's missing or decodes to non-ProposalTelemetry bytes.
   155	    pub proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable: bool,
   156	    /// Run-id from `pinned_pubkeys.json` manifest (echoed for forensics).
   157	    pub run_id: String,
   158	    /// Epoch from `pinned_pubkeys.json` manifest.
   159	    pub epoch: u64,
   160	    /// Detail block — non-blocking forensic data.
   161	    pub detail: ReplayReportDetail,
   162	}
   163	
   164	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: TB-6 Atom 4 — replay_report.json detail block.
   165	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   166	pub struct ReplayReportDetail {
   167	    pub final_state_root_hex: Option<String>,
   168	    pub final_ledger_root_hex: Option<String>,
   169	    /// Lowercase 40-char git commit OID at HEAD of `refs/transitions/main`,
   170	    /// or None if chain is empty.
   171	    pub head_commit_oid_hex: Option<String>,
   172	    /// L4.E chain hash at the end of `rejections.jsonl`, or `Hash::ZERO`
   173	    /// hex if empty.
   174	    pub l4e_last_hash_hex: String,
   175	    /// One-line classification of the replay error if replay failed.
   176	    pub replay_failure: Option<String>,
   177	    /// True iff `<runtime_repo>/initial_q_state.json` was found and loaded.
   178	    /// False when the verifier defaulted to `QState::genesis()`.
   179	    pub initial_q_state_loaded_from_disk: bool,
   180	}
   181	
   182	impl ReplayReport {
   183	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: TB-6 Atom 4 — ship-gate aggregator over the 5
   184	    /// architect-mandated boolean indicators. The CLI uses this to drive its
   185	    /// exit code (0 when all pass, 1 otherwise).
   186	    ///
   187	    /// True iff every architect-mandated boolean indicator is `true`.
   188	    /// **TB-7 Atom 4**: also checks the new `agent_signatures_verified` (Gate 4)
   189	    /// and `proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable` (Gate 5) indicators.
   190	    pub fn all_indicators_pass(&self) -> bool {
   191	        self.ledger_root_verified
   192	            && self.system_signatures_verified
   193	            && self.state_reconstructed
   194	            && self.economic_state_reconstructed
   195	            && self.cas_payloads_retrievable
   196	            && self.agent_signatures_verified
   197	            && self.proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable
   198	    }
   199	}
   200	
   201	// ── Verifier entry-points ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   202	
   203	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1: TB-6 Atom 4 — verify_chaintape options.
   204	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
   205	pub struct VerifyOptions {
   229	pub fn verify_chaintape(
   230	    runtime_repo_path: &Path,
   231	    cas_path: &Path,
   232	    options: &VerifyOptions,
   233	) -> Result<ReplayReport, VerifyError> {
   234	    // Step 1: pinned-pubkey manifest.
   235	    let manifest_path = runtime_repo_path.join(PINNED_PUBKEYS_FILENAME);
   236	    if !manifest_path.exists() {
   237	        return Err(VerifyError::PinnedPubkeysMissing(manifest_path));
   238	    }
   239	    let manifest_json = std::fs::read_to_string(&manifest_path)?;
   240	    let manifest: PinnedPubkeyManifest = serde_json::from_str(&manifest_json)
   241	        .map_err(|e| VerifyError::PinnedPubkeysParse(e.to_string()))?;
   242	    if let Some(expected) = options.expected_run_id.as_ref() {
   243	        if manifest.run_id != *expected {
   244	            return Err(VerifyError::PinnedPubkeysParse(format!(
   245	                "run_id mismatch: manifest={} expected={}",
   246	                manifest.run_id, expected
   247	            )));
   248	        }
   249	    }
   250	    let mut pinned = PinnedSystemPubkeys::new();
   251	    for entry in &manifest.pubkeys {
   252	        let bytes = decode_pubkey_hex(&entry.pubkey_hex)?;
   253	        let arr: [u8; 32] = bytes.as_slice().try_into().map_err(|_| {
   254	            VerifyError::PubkeyDecode(format!(
   255	                "expected 32-byte ed25519 pubkey, got {} bytes",
   256	                bytes.len()
   257	            ))
   258	        })?;
   259	        let pubkey = SystemPublicKey::from_bytes(arr);
   260	        pinned.insert(SystemEpoch::new(entry.epoch), pubkey);
   261	    }
   262	
   263	    // Step 2: initial QState.
   264	    let initial_q_path = runtime_repo_path.join(INITIAL_Q_STATE_FILENAME);
   265	    let (initial_q, initial_q_loaded_from_disk) = if initial_q_path.exists() {
   266	        let s = std::fs::read_to_string(&initial_q_path)?;
   267	        let q: QState = serde_json::from_str(&s)
   268	            .map_err(|e| VerifyError::InitialQStateParse(e.to_string()))?;
   269	        (q, true)
   270	    } else {
   271	        (QState::genesis(), false)
   272	    };
   273	
   274	    // Step 3: open ledger writer + read all entries.
   275	    let writer = Git2LedgerWriter::open(runtime_repo_path)?;
   276	    let l4_entries = writer.len();
   277	    let head_commit_oid_hex = writer.head_commit_oid_hex();
   278	    let entries: Vec<LedgerEntry> = (1..=l4_entries)
   279	        .map(|t| writer.read_at(t))
   280	        .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?;
   281	
   282	    // Step 4: open CAS.
   283	    let cas_store = CasStore::open(cas_path).map_err(|e| VerifyError::Cas(e.to_string()))?;
   284	
   285	    // Step 5: open + verify L4.E chain.
   286	    let rejections_path = runtime_repo_path.join(REJECTIONS_JSONL_FILENAME);
   287	    let l4e_writer = if rejections_path.exists() {
   288	        RejectionEvidenceWriter::open_jsonl(rejections_path).map_err(VerifyError::L4eOpen)?
   289	    } else {
   290	        RejectionEvidenceWriter::new()
   291	    };
   292	    let l4e_entries = l4e_writer.len() as u64;
   293	    let l4e_last_hash_hex = hash_to_hex(&l4e_writer.last_hash());
   294	
   295	    // Step 6: replay.
   296	    let predicate_registry = PredicateRegistry::new();
   297	    let tool_registry = ToolRegistry::new();
   298	    let replay_outcome = replay_full_transition(
   299	        &initial_q,
   300	        &entries,
   301	        &cas_store,
   302	        &pinned,
   303	        &predicate_registry,
   304	        &tool_registry,
   305	    );
   306	
   307	    let (
   308	        ledger_root_verified,
   309	        system_signatures_verified,
   310	        state_reconstructed,
   311	        economic_state_reconstructed,
   312	        cas_payloads_retrievable,
   313	        final_state_root_hex,
   314	        final_ledger_root_hex,
   315	        replay_failure,
   316	    ) = match replay_outcome {
   317	        Ok(final_q) => (
   318	            true,
   319	            true,
   320	            true,
   321	            true,
   322	            true,
   323	            Some(hash_to_hex(&final_q.state_root_t)),
   324	            Some(hash_to_hex(&final_q.ledger_root_t)),
   325	            None,
   326	        ),
   327	        Err(err) => classify_replay_error(&err),
   328	    };
   329	
   330	    // ── TB-7 Atom 4: agent signature verification (Gate 4) ──
   331	    //
   332	    // Walk every L4 entry; for WorkTx and VerifyTx variants, verify the
   333	    // AgentSignature against the per-run agent_pubkeys.json manifest.
   334	    // Empty chain or chain with no Work/Verify entries → trivially true
   335	    // (no agent signatures to fail).
   336	    let (agent_signatures_verified, proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable) =
   337	        verify_agent_artifacts(runtime_repo_path, &cas_store, &entries);
   338	
   339	    Ok(ReplayReport {
   340	        l4_entries,
   341	        l4e_entries,
   342	        ledger_root_verified,
   343	        system_signatures_verified,
   344	        state_reconstructed,
   345	        economic_state_reconstructed,
   346	        cas_payloads_retrievable,
   347	        agent_signatures_verified,
   348	        proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable,
   349	        run_id: manifest.run_id,
   350	        epoch: manifest.epoch,
   351	        detail: ReplayReportDetail {
   352	            final_state_root_hex,
   353	            final_ledger_root_hex,
   354	            head_commit_oid_hex,
   355	            l4e_last_hash_hex,
   356	            replay_failure,
   357	            initial_q_state_loaded_from_disk: initial_q_loaded_from_disk,
   358	        },
   359	    })
   360	}
   362	/// TRACE_MATRIX FC1-N14: TB-7 Atom 4 — verify Gate 4 + Gate 5 indicators by
   363	/// walking every L4 entry and (for WorkTx / VerifyTx variants) re-verifying
   364	/// agent signatures against the on-disk `agent_pubkeys.json` manifest, plus
   365	/// checking that every `WorkTx.proposal_cid` resolves to a CAS-resident
   366	/// ProposalTelemetry object.
   367	///
   368	/// Returns `(agent_signatures_verified, proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable)`.
   369	/// Both default to `true` when the manifest doesn't exist or when no
   370	/// Work/Verify entries are present (no signatures to verify ≠ failure).
   371	fn verify_agent_artifacts(
   372	    runtime_repo_path: &Path,
   373	    cas_store: &CasStore,
   374	    entries: &[LedgerEntry],
   375	) -> (bool, bool) {
   376	    use crate::bottom_white::ledger::transition_ledger::canonical_decode;
   377	    use crate::runtime::agent_keypairs::{verify_agent_signature, AgentPubkeyManifest};
   378	    use crate::runtime::proposal_telemetry::read_from_cas as read_telemetry;
   379	    use crate::state::typed_tx::TypedTx;
   380	
   381	    let manifest_path = runtime_repo_path.join("agent_pubkeys.json");
   382	    if !manifest_path.exists() {
   383	        // No agent_pubkeys.json (legacy / pre-Atom-1 chain). Both indicators
   384	        // trivially true since there are no agent-side artifacts to fail.
   385	        return (true, true);
   386	    }
   387	    let manifest = match AgentPubkeyManifest::load(&manifest_path) {
   388	        Ok(m) => m,
   389	        Err(_) => return (false, false), // manifest unparseable = both fail
   390	    };
   391	
   392	    let mut agent_signatures_verified = true;
   393	    let mut proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable = true;
   394	
   395	    for entry in entries {
   396	        // Get the typed payload from CAS.
   397	        let payload_bytes = match cas_store.get(&entry.tx_payload_cid) {
   398	            Ok(b) => b,
   399	            Err(_) => continue, // cas_payloads_retrievable already covers this
   400	        };
   401	        let typed_tx: TypedTx = match canonical_decode(&payload_bytes) {
   402	            Ok(tx) => tx,
   403	            Err(_) => continue, // payload decode error already covered upstream
   404	        };
   405	
   406	        match &typed_tx {
   407	            TypedTx::Work(work) => {
   408	                // Gate 4 — verify WorkTx signature.
   409	                let payload = work.to_signing_payload();
   410	                let digest = payload.canonical_digest();
   411	                let pubkey_opt = manifest.get(&work.agent_id);
   412	                match pubkey_opt {
   413	                    None => agent_signatures_verified = false,
   414	                    Some(pubkey) => {
   415	                        if verify_agent_signature(&work.signature, &digest, &pubkey).is_err() {
   416	                            agent_signatures_verified = false;
   417	                        }
   418	                    }
   419	                }
   420	                // Gate 5 — verify proposal_cid resolves to a ProposalTelemetry.
   421	                // Skip if proposal_cid is the zero-CID (legacy synthetic seed).
   422	                if work.proposal_cid.0 != [0u8; 32] {
   423	                    if read_telemetry(cas_store, &work.proposal_cid).is_err() {
   424	                        proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable = false;
   425	                    }
   426	                }
   427	            }
   428	            TypedTx::Verify(verify) => {
   429	                // Gate 4 — verify VerifyTx signature.
   430	                let payload = verify.to_signing_payload();
   431	                let digest = payload.canonical_digest();
   432	                let pubkey_opt = manifest.get(&verify.verifier_agent);
   433	                match pubkey_opt {
   434	                    None => agent_signatures_verified = false,
   435	                    Some(pubkey) => {
   436	                        if verify_agent_signature(&verify.signature, &digest, &pubkey).is_err() {
   437	                            agent_signatures_verified = false;
   438	                        }
   439	                    }
   440	                }
   441	            }
   442	            // TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 6 round-2 (Codex VETO TB13-V2
   443	            // remediation 2026-05-03): extend Gate 4 to cover the 3
   444	            // agent-signed TB-13 variants. The submit-time verification
   445	            // gap is codebase-wide (also affects Challenge/TaskOpen/
   446	            // EscrowLock); replay-time coverage is the existing TB-7
   447	            // ARCHITECT_RULING D3 model. TB-13 raises the bar to its
   448	            // own three variants because Class 3 money-mover.
   449	            TypedTx::CompleteSetMint(mint) => {
   450	                let payload = mint.to_signing_payload();
   451	                let digest = payload.canonical_digest();
   452	                let pubkey_opt = manifest.get(&mint.owner);
   453	                match pubkey_opt {
   454	                    None => agent_signatures_verified = false,
   455	                    Some(pubkey) => {
   456	                        if verify_agent_signature(&mint.signature, &digest, &pubkey).is_err() {
   457	                            agent_signatures_verified = false;
   458	                        }
   459	                    }
   460	                }
   461	            }
   462	            TypedTx::CompleteSetRedeem(redeem) => {
   463	                let payload = redeem.to_signing_payload();
   464	                let digest = payload.canonical_digest();
   465	                let pubkey_opt = manifest.get(&redeem.owner);
   466	                match pubkey_opt {
   467	                    None => agent_signatures_verified = false,
   468	                    Some(pubkey) => {
   469	                        if verify_agent_signature(&redeem.signature, &digest, &pubkey).is_err() {
   470	                            agent_signatures_verified = false;
   471	                        }
   472	                    }
   473	                }
   474	            }
   475	            TypedTx::MarketSeed(seed) => {
   476	                let payload = seed.to_signing_payload();
   477	                let digest = payload.canonical_digest();
   478	                let pubkey_opt = manifest.get(&seed.provider);
   479	                match pubkey_opt {
   480	                    None => agent_signatures_verified = false,
   481	                    Some(pubkey) => {
   482	                        if verify_agent_signature(&seed.signature, &digest, &pubkey).is_err() {
   483	                            agent_signatures_verified = false;
   484	                        }
   485	                    }
   486	                }
   487	            }
   488	            // Remaining tx variants (TaskOpen / EscrowLock / Challenge /
   489	            // ChallengeResolve / ReuseTx / FinalizeReward / TaskExpire /
   490	            // TerminalSummary / TaskBankruptcy) are not covered by Gate 4
   491	            // because:
   492	            // - Some are system-emitted (signature path is system, not agent;
   493	            //   covered by system_signatures_verified above).
   494	            // - Others are agent-emitted but their signing payloads need
   495	            //   per-variant signing helpers and are deferred to a future
   496	            //   codebase-wide CO P2.x AgentRegistry pass per `OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03`.
   497	            _ => {}
   498	        }
   499	    }
   500	
   501	    (
   502	        agent_signatures_verified,
   503	        proposal_telemetry_cas_retrievable,
   504	    )
   505	}
   506	
   507	// ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   508	
   509	fn decode_pubkey_hex(hex: &str) -> Result<Vec<u8>, VerifyError> {
   510	    if hex.len() % 2 != 0 {
   511	        return Err(VerifyError::PubkeyDecode(format!(
   512	            "odd-length hex string ({})",
   513	            hex.len()
   514	        )));
   515	    }
   516	    let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(hex.len() / 2);
   517	    for chunk in hex.as_bytes().chunks(2) {
   518	        let s = std::str::from_utf8(chunk)
   519	            .map_err(|e| VerifyError::PubkeyDecode(e.to_string()))?;
   520	        let byte = u8::from_str_radix(s, 16)
   521	            .map_err(|e| VerifyError::PubkeyDecode(e.to_string()))?;
   522	        out.push(byte);
   523	    }
   524	    Ok(out)
   525	}
   526	
   527	fn hash_to_hex(h: &Hash) -> String {
   528	    h.0.iter().map(|b| format!("{:02x}", b)).collect()
   529	}
   530

   414	/// 2. parent_state_root chain
   415	/// 3. parent_ledger_root chain (K2 transplant defense)
   416	/// 4. system_signature verifies via CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning + pinned pubkeys
   417	/// 5. CAS lookup of tx_payload_cid succeeds (CO1.4-extra cold-replay capability)
   418	/// 6. canonical_decode of payload bytes → TypedTx
   419	/// 6.5 (v1.1 C-3): decoded_typed_tx.tx_kind() MUST equal entry.tx_kind
   420	/// 7. dispatch_transition re-run produces (q_next, _signals)
   421	/// 8. q_next.state_root_t matches entry.resulting_state_root
   422	/// 9. resulting_ledger_root recomputed via append() matches stored
   423	///
   424	/// **v1.1 C-1 closure**: takes a full `genesis: &QState` (was `genesis_state_root`
   425	/// + `genesis_ledger_root` only). Caller provides the complete genesis state
   426	/// so dispatch_transition can read budget / registries / balances / task markets
   427	/// — fabricating `QState::genesis()` was dropping these fields.
   428	///
   429	/// **Stub-state caveat (CO1.7.5 unblocks)**: while `dispatch_transition`
   430	/// returns `NotYetImplemented` for every variant, replay errors at stage 7
   431	/// for any non-empty chain. Conformance tests exercising stages 1-6.5
   432	/// independently are `#[test]`-runnable now; full state_root reconstruction
   433	/// gates on CO1.7.5.
   434	pub fn replay_full_transition(
   435	    genesis: &crate::state::q_state::QState,
   436	    entries: &[LedgerEntry],
   437	    cas: &dyn LedgerCasView,
   438	    pinned_pubkeys: &crate::bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::PinnedSystemPubkeys,
   439	    predicate_registry: &crate::top_white::predicates::registry::PredicateRegistry,
   440	    tool_registry: &crate::bottom_white::tools::registry::ToolRegistry,
   441	) -> Result<crate::state::q_state::QState, ReplayError> {
   442	    use crate::bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::{
   443	        verify_system_signature, CanonicalMessage,
   444	    };
   445	    use crate::state::sequencer::dispatch_transition;
   446	
   447	    let mut q = genesis.clone();
   448	
   449	    for (i, entry) in entries.iter().enumerate() {
   450	        // Stage 1
   451	        let expected_logical_t = (i as u64) + 1;
   452	        if entry.logical_t != expected_logical_t {
   453	            return Err(ReplayError::LogicalTGap {
   454	                at: i,
   455	                expected: expected_logical_t,
   456	                got: entry.logical_t,
   457	            });
   458	        }
   459	        // Stage 2
   460	        if entry.parent_state_root != q.state_root_t {
   461	            return Err(ReplayError::ParentStateMismatch { at: i });
   462	        }
   463	        // Stage 3
   464	        if entry.parent_ledger_root != q.ledger_root_t {
   465	            return Err(ReplayError::ParentLedgerMismatch { at: i });
   466	        }
   467	
   468	        // Stage 4: system_signature verify (FullTransition mode only).
   469	        let signing_payload = entry.to_signing_payload();
   470	        let signing_digest = signing_payload.canonical_digest();
   471	        let canonical_msg = CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning(signing_digest.0);
   472	        if !verify_system_signature(
   473	            &entry.system_signature,
   474	            &canonical_msg,
   475	            entry.epoch,
   476	            pinned_pubkeys,
   477	        ) {
   478	            return Err(ReplayError::BadSignature { at: i });
   479	        }
   480	
   481	        // Stage 5: CAS lookup.
   482	        let payload_bytes = cas
   483	            .get_typed_payload(&entry.tx_payload_cid)
   484	            .map_err(|_| ReplayError::CasMissing { at: i })?;
   485	
   486	        // Stage 6: canonical_decode → TypedTx (v1.1 C-3-secondary: distinct
   487	        // error from CasMissing).
   488	        let typed_tx: crate::state::typed_tx::TypedTx =
   489	            canonical_decode(&payload_bytes).map_err(|e| ReplayError::PayloadDecode {
   490	                at: i,
   491	                reason: e.to_string(),
   492	            })?;
   493	
   494	        // Stage 6.5 (v1.1 C-3): tx_kind envelope vs decoded payload kind MUST match.
   495	        // Otherwise a signed envelope claiming `Work` could ride a CAS payload
   496	        // that decodes as `Verify` — sequencer would have written that
   497	        // mismatch but replay would have silently accepted it pre-v1.1.
   498	        let decoded_kind = typed_tx.tx_kind();
   499	        if decoded_kind != entry.tx_kind {
   500	            return Err(ReplayError::TxKindMismatch {
   501	                at: i,
   502	                envelope_kind: entry.tx_kind,
   503	                decoded_kind,
   504	            });
   505	        }
   506	
   507	        // Stage 7: re-run pure dispatch_transition.
   508	        let (q_next, _signals) =
   509	            dispatch_transition(&q, &typed_tx, predicate_registry, tool_registry)
   510	                .map_err(|inner| ReplayError::Transition { at: i, inner })?;
   511	
   512	        // Stage 8: state_root match.
   513	        if q_next.state_root_t != entry.resulting_state_root {
   514	            return Err(ReplayError::StateRootMismatch { at: i });
   515	        }
   516	
   517	        // Stage 9: ledger_root match (recompute via append).
   518	        let recomputed_ledger_root = append(&q.ledger_root_t, &signing_digest);
   519	        if recomputed_ledger_root != entry.resulting_ledger_root {
   520	            return Err(ReplayError::LedgerRootMismatch { at: i });
   521	        }
   522	
   523	        // Advance.
   524	        q = q_next;
   525	        q.ledger_root_t = entry.resulting_ledger_root;

   355	/// `build_chaintape_sequencer` delegates here with `QState::genesis()`.

   360	pub fn build_chaintape_sequencer_with_initial_q(

codex
One important issue is emerging in the RQ3 evidence: the TB-13 accept root mutators hash `(domain, previous state_root, canonical tx)`, not the full `QState`. That means `final_state_root_hex == live state_root_t` proves transaction-chain determinism, but it is not by itself cryptographic evidence that every TB-13 map byte reconstructed equal to live state.

   573	/// Halt: total_supply_micro must be unchanged across mint+redeem.
   574	#[tokio::test]
   575	async fn halt_total_supply_micro_unchanged_across_mint_redeem() {
   576	    // Use pre-baked post-mint state with task=Finalized; the post-mint
   577	    // QState reflects the would-be result of a successful mint into an
   578	    // Open task. Compare against the genesis (pre-mint balance) to
   579	    // verify total supply bit-equality. After redeem, alice's balance
   580	    // is restored.
   581	    let q_genesis = genesis_with_balances(&[("alice", 100)]);
   582	    let q0 = genesis_post_mint(
   583	        &[("alice", 100)],
   584	        "alice",
   585	        "task-H1",
   586	        7_000_000,
   587	        TaskMarketState::Finalized,
   588	    );
   589	    let mut h = fresh_harness(q0);
   590	    let parent = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap().state_root_t;
   591	    submit_and_apply(
   592	        &mut h,
   593	        build_redeem(parent, "alice", "task-H1", OutcomeSide::Yes, 7_000_000, 21),
   594	    )
   595	    .await
   596	    .expect("redeem");
   597	
   598	    let q = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap();
   599	    // q_genesis vs q (post-redeem) — both should have alice at 100 Coin
   600	    // (genesis 100; mint -7M; redeem +7M = 100). The pre-baked post-
   601	    // mint state had collateral, but post-redeem collateral is 0.
   602	    // Comparison must add task_markets_t state in pre as Finalized too,
   603	    // or we use a different baseline. Simpler: check 6-holding sum is
   604	    // the same as q_genesis.
   605	    let genesis_with_finalized_market = {
   606	        let mut g = q_genesis.clone();
   607	        seed_task_market(&mut g, "task-H1", TaskMarketState::Finalized);
   608	        g
   609	    };
   610	    assert_total_ctf_conserved(
   611	        &genesis_with_finalized_market.economic_state_t,
   612	        &q.economic_state_t,
   613	        &[],
   614	    )
   615	    .expect("total_supply_micro bit-equal pre/post (mint→redeem cancels out)");
   616	    assert_complete_set_balanced(&q.economic_state_t).expect("balanced");
   617	}
   618	
   619	/// Halt: shares are NOT counted as Coin (regression guard for SG-13.2).
   620	#[tokio::test]
   621	async fn halt_shares_not_counted_as_coin() {
   622	    let q0 = genesis_with_balances_and_open_task(&[("alice", 100)], "task-H2");
   623	    let mut h = fresh_harness(q0.clone());
   624	    let parent = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap().state_root_t;
   625	    submit_and_apply(&mut h, build_mint(parent, "alice", "task-H2", 9_876_543, 22))
   626	        .await
   627	        .expect("mint");
   628	    let q = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap();
   629	    assert_total_ctf_conserved(&q0.economic_state_t, &q.economic_state_t, &[])
   630	        .expect("shares not in total_supply");
   631	}

   633	/// Halt: MarketSeed with zero-balance provider rejected (regression
   634	/// guard for SG-13.3).
   635	#[tokio::test]
   636	async fn halt_market_seed_zero_balance_provider_rejected() {
   637	    let mut q0 = QState::genesis();
   638	    seed_task_market(&mut q0, "task-H3", TaskMarketState::Open);
   639	    let mut h = fresh_harness(q0);

   642	        .await
   643	        .expect_err("seed must fail");
   644	    assert!(err.contains("InsufficientBalanceForMint"));
   645	}

   647	/// Architect-mandated invariant: redeeming more shares than owned is
   648	/// rejected with `RedeemMoreThanOwned`.
   649	#[tokio::test]
   650	async fn halt_redeem_more_than_owned_rejected() {
   651	    let q0 = genesis_post_mint(
   652	        &[("alice", 100)],
   653	        "alice",
   654	        "task-H4",
   655	        1_000_000,
   656	        TaskMarketState::Finalized,
   657	    );
   658	    let mut h = fresh_harness(q0);
   659	    let parent = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap().state_root_t;
   660	    let err = submit_and_apply(
   661	        &mut h,
   662	        build_redeem(parent, "alice", "task-H4", OutcomeSide::Yes, 5_000_000, 25),
   663	    )
   664	    .await
   665	    .expect_err("over-redeem must fail");
   666	    assert!(
   667	        err.contains("RedeemMoreThanOwned"),
   668	        "expected RedeemMoreThanOwned, got: {err}"
   669	    );
   670	}
   671	
   672	/// Codex round-1 VETO TB13-V1 remediation: negative `MicroCoin` amount
   673	/// in CompleteSetMintTx must be rejected. `MicroCoin` is i64-backed and
   674	/// permits negative values at the type layer; the dispatch arm gates
   675	/// `<= 0` (not just `== 0`). Without this gate, a negative mint would
   676	/// credit balance + write negative collateral + cast to huge u128 shares.
   677	#[tokio::test]
   678	async fn halt_negative_mint_amount_rejected() {
   679	    // Pre-seed task as Open so the Q13 gate isn't the one firing — we
   680	    // want to verify the negative-amount check (Step 2) catches first.
   681	    let q0 = genesis_with_balances_and_open_task(&[("alice", 100)], "task-NEG");
   682	    let mut h = fresh_harness(q0);
   683	    let parent = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap().state_root_t;
   684	
   685	    let neg_mint = TypedTx::CompleteSetMint(CompleteSetMintTx {
   686	        tx_id: TxId("neg-mint-fixture".into()),
   687	        parent_state_root: parent,
   688	        event_id: EventId(TaskId("task-NEG".into())),
   689	        owner: AgentId("alice".into()),
   690	        amount: MicroCoin::from_micro_units(-1_000_000),
   691	        signature: AgentSignature::from_bytes([0u8; 64]),
   692	        timestamp_logical: 999,
   693	    });
   694	    let err = submit_and_apply(&mut h, neg_mint)
   695	        .await
   696	        .expect_err("negative mint must be rejected");
   697	    assert!(
   698	        err.contains("InsufficientBalanceForMint"),
   699	        "expected InsufficientBalanceForMint for negative amount, got: {err}"
   700	    );
   701	
   702	    // Verify alice balance unchanged.
   703	    let q = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap();
   704	    let alice_bal = q
   705	        .economic_state_t
   706	        .balances_t
   707	        .0
   708	        .get(&AgentId("alice".into()))
   709	        .copied()
   710	        .unwrap();
   711	    assert_eq!(
   712	        alice_bal.micro_units(),
   713	        100_i64 * 1_000_000,
   714	        "alice balance MUST be unchanged after negative-mint rejection"
   715	    );
   716	    // Verify no collateral written.
   717	    assert!(
   718	        q.economic_state_t
   719	            .conditional_collateral_t
   720	            .0
   721	            .get(&EventId(TaskId("task-NEG".into())))
   722	            .is_none(),
   723	        "no collateral must be written under negative-mint rejection"
   724	    );
   725	}
   726	
   727	/// Codex round-1 VETO TB13-V1 remediation: negative `MicroCoin`
   728	/// collateral_amount in MarketSeedTx must be rejected with
   729	/// `InsufficientCollateral`. Same attack vector as halt_negative_mint
   730	/// but via the seed path.
   731	#[tokio::test]
   732	async fn halt_negative_market_seed_collateral_rejected() {
   733	    // Pre-seed task as Open so the Q13 gate isn't the one firing — we
   734	    // want to verify the negative-collateral check (Step 1) catches first.
   735	    let q0 = genesis_with_balances_and_open_task(&[("provider", 50)], "task-NEGS");
   736	    let mut h = fresh_harness(q0);
   737	    let parent = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap().state_root_t;
   738	
   739	    let neg_seed = TypedTx::MarketSeed(MarketSeedTx {
   740	        tx_id: TxId("neg-seed-fixture".into()),
   741	        parent_state_root: parent,
   742	        event_id: EventId(TaskId("task-NEGS".into())),
   743	        provider: AgentId("provider".into()),
   744	        collateral_amount: MicroCoin::from_micro_units(-500_000),
   745	        signature: AgentSignature::from_bytes([0u8; 64]),
   746	        timestamp_logical: 998,
   747	    });
   748	    let err = submit_and_apply(&mut h, neg_seed)
   749	        .await
   750	        .expect_err("negative-collateral seed must be rejected");
   751	    assert!(
   752	        err.contains("InsufficientCollateral"),
   753	        "expected InsufficientCollateral for negative collateral, got: {err}"
   754	    );
   755	
   756	    let q = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap();
   757	    let provider_bal = q
   758	        .economic_state_t
   759	        .balances_t
   760	        .0
   761	        .get(&AgentId("provider".into()))
   762	        .copied()
   763	        .unwrap();
   764	    assert_eq!(
   765	        provider_bal.micro_units(),
   766	        50_i64 * 1_000_000,
   767	        "provider balance MUST be unchanged after negative-seed rejection"
   768	    );
   769	}

   771	/// Gemini round-2 CHALLENGE Q13 remediation: CompleteSetMintTx against
   772	/// a Finalized event must reject with `EventNotOpen` (closes the
   773	/// post-resolution griefing surface where an agent could mint shares
   774	/// against a closed event and immediately redeem winning side).
   775	#[tokio::test]
   776	async fn halt_q13_mint_against_finalized_event_rejected() {
   777	    let mut q0 = genesis_with_balances(&[("alice", 100)]);
   778	    seed_task_market(&mut q0, "task-Q13F", TaskMarketState::Finalized);
   779	    let mut h = fresh_harness(q0);
   780	    let parent = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap().state_root_t;
   781	
   782	    let err = submit_and_apply(
   783	        &mut h,
   784	        build_mint(parent, "alice", "task-Q13F", 1_000_000, 100),
   785	    )
   786	    .await
   787	    .expect_err("mint against Finalized event must be rejected");
   788	    assert!(
   789	        err.contains("EventNotOpen"),
   790	        "expected EventNotOpen, got: {err}"
   791	    );
   792	}
   793	
   794	/// Gemini round-2 CHALLENGE Q13 remediation: same gate against

   798	async fn halt_q13_seed_against_bankrupt_event_rejected() {

   816	/// Q13 gate: mint against an event with NO task_markets_t entry must
   817	/// reject with `TaskNotOpen` (the missing-event case; EventId is 1:1
   818	/// with TaskId in TB-13 so a task must exist for the event to be valid).
   819	#[tokio::test]
   820	async fn halt_q13_mint_against_missing_task_rejected() {
   821	    let q0 = genesis_with_balances(&[("alice", 100)]);
   822	    let mut h = fresh_harness(q0);
   823	    let parent = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap().state_root_t;
   824	
   825	    let err = submit_and_apply(
   826	        &mut h,
   827	        build_mint(parent, "alice", "task-NOEXIST", 1_000_000, 102),
   828	    )
   829	    .await
   830	    .expect_err("mint against missing task must be rejected");
   831	    assert!(
   832	        err.contains("TaskNotOpen"),
   833	        "expected TaskNotOpen, got: {err}"
   834	    );
   835	}

   837	/// Architect-mandated invariant: complete-set balanced post-seed.
   838	#[tokio::test]
   839	async fn halt_complete_set_balanced_post_seed() {
   840	    let q0 = genesis_with_balances_and_open_task(&[("provider", 50)], "task-H5");
   841	    let mut h = fresh_harness(q0);
   842	    let parent = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap().state_root_t;
   843	    submit_and_apply(&mut h, build_seed(parent, "provider", "task-H5", 3_141_592, 26))
   844	        .await
   845	        .expect("seed");
   846	    let q = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap();
   847	    assert_complete_set_balanced(&q.economic_state_t).expect("balanced after seed");
   848	    let collateral = q
   849	        .economic_state_t
   850	        .conditional_collateral_t
   851	        .0
   852	        .get(&EventId(TaskId("task-H5".into())))
   853	        .copied()
   854	        .unwrap();
   855	    assert_eq!(collateral.micro_units(), 3_141_592);
   856	    let pair = q
   857	        .economic_state_t
   858	        .conditional_share_balances_t
   859	        .0
   860	        .get(&AgentId("provider".into()))
   861	        .and_then(|m| m.get(&EventId(TaskId("task-H5".into()))))
   862	        .copied()
   863	        .unwrap();
   864	    assert_eq!(pair.yes.units, 3_141_592_u128);
   865	    assert_eq!(pair.no.units, 3_141_592_u128);
   866	}

   868	// Suppress unused import warnings — the harness types are referenced via
   869	// trait constraints + the helper signatures.
   870	#[allow(dead_code)]
   871	fn _suppress_unused() {
   872	    let _ = ConditionalCollateralIndex::default();
   873	    let _ = ConditionalShareBalances::default();
   874	    let _: BTreeMap<EventId, MicroCoin> = BTreeMap::new();
   875	    let _ = ShareSidePair::default();
   876	}
   877	
   878	// ── TB13-AUTH (Codex VETO round-2 remediation) ──────────────────────────────
   879	
   880	/// Codex round-2 VETO TB13-AUTH remediation: when `agent_pubkeys` is set on
   881	/// the sequencer, TB-13 submissions with valid signatures are accepted and
   882	/// submissions with forged (all-zero) signatures are rejected at
   883	/// `submit_agent_tx` time with `SubmitError::AgentSignatureInvalid`.
   884	///
   885	/// Builds a real Ed25519 keypair, registers the agent in an
   886	/// `AgentPubkeyManifest`, signs the canonical mint payload with the
   887	/// keypair, and proves both the accept-on-valid and reject-on-forged paths.
   888	#[tokio::test]
   889	async fn tb13_auth_submit_time_signature_verification() {
   890	    use std::sync::Arc;
   891	    use turingosv4::runtime::agent_keypairs::{
   892	        AgentKeypair, AgentPubkeyManifest,
   893	    };
   894	    use turingosv4::state::sequencer::SubmitError;
   895	
   896	    let q0 = genesis_with_balances_and_open_task(&[("alice", 100)], "task-AUTH");
   897	    let mut h = fresh_harness(q0);
   898	
   899	    // Build a real keypair for "alice" + register in the manifest.
   900	    let alice_keypair = AgentKeypair::generate().expect("generate alice keypair");
   901	    let mut manifest = AgentPubkeyManifest::default();
   902	    manifest.agents.insert(
   903	        "alice".to_string(),
   904	        alice_keypair.public_key().to_hex(),
   905	    );
   906	    h.seq
   907	        .set_agent_pubkeys(Arc::new(manifest))
   908	        .expect("set_agent_pubkeys must succeed once");
   909	
   910	    let parent = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap().state_root_t;
   911	
   912	    // Path A — valid signature: build canonical signing payload digest,
   913	    // sign with alice's keypair, attach as the mint's signature.
   914	    let mint_unsigned = CompleteSetMintTx {
   915	        tx_id: TxId("auth-mint-fixture".into()),
   916	        parent_state_root: parent,
   917	        event_id: EventId(TaskId("task-AUTH".into())),
   918	        owner: AgentId("alice".into()),
   919	        amount: MicroCoin::from_micro_units(1_000_000),
   920	        signature: AgentSignature::from_bytes([0u8; 64]),
   921	        timestamp_logical: 500,
   922	    };
   923	    let signing_digest = mint_unsigned.to_signing_payload().canonical_digest();
   924	    let valid_sig = alice_keypair
   925	        .sign_digest(signing_digest)
   926	        .expect("sign_digest");
   927	    let mint_signed = CompleteSetMintTx {
   928	        signature: valid_sig,
   929	        ..mint_unsigned.clone()
   930	    };
   931	    submit_and_apply(&mut h, TypedTx::CompleteSetMint(mint_signed))
   932	        .await
   933	        .expect("valid-sig mint must be accepted");
   934	
   935	    // Path B — forged signature: all-zero AgentSignature, valid payload.
   936	    // Must be rejected at submit_agent_tx with AgentSignatureInvalid.
   937	    let parent_b = h.seq.q_snapshot().unwrap().state_root_t;
   938	    let forged_mint = CompleteSetMintTx {
   939	        tx_id: TxId("auth-forged-fixture".into()),
   940	        parent_state_root: parent_b,
   941	        event_id: EventId(TaskId("task-AUTH".into())),
   942	        owner: AgentId("alice".into()),
   943	        amount: MicroCoin::from_micro_units(500_000),
   944	        signature: AgentSignature::from_bytes([0u8; 64]),
   945	        timestamp_logical: 501,
   946	    };
   947	    let submit_err = h
   948	        .seq
   949	        .submit_agent_tx(TypedTx::CompleteSetMint(forged_mint))
   950	        .await
   951	        .expect_err("forged-sig mint must be rejected at submit");
   952	    assert!(
   953	        matches!(submit_err, SubmitError::AgentSignatureInvalid),
   954	        "expected SubmitError::AgentSignatureInvalid, got: {submit_err:?}"
   955	    );
   956	
   957	    // Path C — unregistered agent: build a different keypair, sign with it
   958	    // for "alice" (impostor); pubkey lookup matches alice's registered
   959	    // key, signature verification fails. Must be rejected at submit.
   960	    let impostor_keypair = AgentKeypair::generate().expect("generate impostor keypair");
   961	    let mint_impostor_unsigned = CompleteSetMintTx {
   962	        tx_id: TxId("auth-impostor-fixture".into()),
   963	        parent_state_root: parent_b,
   964	        event_id: EventId(TaskId("task-AUTH".into())),
   965	        owner: AgentId("alice".into()),
   966	        amount: MicroCoin::from_micro_units(500_000),
   967	        signature: AgentSignature::from_bytes([0u8; 64]),
   968	        timestamp_logical: 502,
   969	    };
   970	    let imp_sig = impostor_keypair
   971	        .sign_digest(mint_impostor_unsigned.to_signing_payload().canonical_digest())
   972	        .expect("sign_digest");
   973	    let impostor_mint = CompleteSetMintTx {
   974	        signature: imp_sig,
   975	        ..mint_impostor_unsigned
   976	    };
   977	    let submit_err = h
   978	        .seq
   979	        .submit_agent_tx(TypedTx::CompleteSetMint(impostor_mint))
   980	        .await

exec

  2333	impl Sequencer {
  2334	    /// Construct. Returns the `Sequencer` plus the receiver half of the

  2358	            next_submit_id: AtomicU64::new(1),
  2359	            next_logical_t: AtomicU64::new(0), // first accepted commit advances to 1
  2360	            next_emit_id: AtomicU64::new(1),    // TB-5 Atom 4: parallel system-emit counter
  2361	            queue_tx,
  2362	            cas,
  2363	            keypair,

  2365	            ledger_writer,
  2366	            rejection_writer,
  2367	            predicate_registry,

  2406	    /// TRACE_MATRIX FC2-Submit + § 5.2.1: TB-5.0 Atom 2 agent-only ingress
  2407	    /// barrier (charter v2 § 4.2 + § 4.9 + preflight § 3.2; Anti-Oreo Art V.1.3).
  2408	    ///
  2409	    /// Accepts ONLY agent-submitted variants. System-emitted variants
  2410	    /// (FinalizeReward / TaskExpire / TerminalSummary; ChallengeResolve added
  2411	    /// in Atom 3) are rejected pre-queue with
  2412	    /// `SubmitError::SystemTxForbiddenOnAgentIngress`. This is the
  2413	    /// constitutional Anti-Oreo "agent ≠ direct state writer" boundary,
  2414	    /// structurally enforced (was a documented norm without live enforcement
  2415	    /// through TB-3 + TB-4; TB-5.0 retires that debt for system-tx).
  2416	    ///
  2417	    /// **WP-canonical reconciliation**: ChallengeResolveTx (TB-5 Atom 3) +
  2418	    /// SlashTx / SettlementTx / ProvisionalAcceptTx / ReputationUpdateTx
  2419	    /// (RSP-3.2+ / RSP-4 territory) will be added to the rejection match
  2420	    /// at their respective TB landings — each new system variant extends
  2421	    /// this list, never bypasses it.
  2422	    pub async fn submit_agent_tx(&self, tx: TypedTx) -> Result<SubmissionReceipt, SubmitError> {
  2423	        // TB-5.0 ingress barrier: reject 4 system-emitted variants
  2424	        // (FinalizeReward / TaskExpire / TerminalSummary added in Atom 2;
  2425	        // ChallengeResolve added in Atom 3 when its TypedTx variant landed).
  2426	        match &tx {
  2427	            TypedTx::FinalizeReward(_)
  2428	            | TypedTx::TaskExpire(_)
  2429	            | TypedTx::TerminalSummary(_)
  2430	            | TypedTx::ChallengeResolve(_)
  2431	            // TB-11 Atom 1 (architect §6.2 ruling 2026-05-02): TaskBankruptcyTx
  2432	            // is system-emitted only; agent ingress must reject pre-queue per
  2433	            // Anti-Oreo (Art V.1.3). Construction goes through emit_system_tx.
  2434	            | TypedTx::TaskBankruptcy(_) => {
  2435	                return Err(SubmitError::SystemTxForbiddenOnAgentIngress);
  2436	            }
  2437	            // Agent-submitted variants — proceed to queue. TB-13 conditional-
  2438	            // share variants (CompleteSetMint / CompleteSetRedeem / MarketSeed)
  2439	            // are agent-signed and admit through the same ingress path.
  2440	            TypedTx::Work(_)
  2441	            | TypedTx::Verify(_)
  2442	            | TypedTx::Challenge(_)
  2443	            | TypedTx::Reuse(_)
  2444	            | TypedTx::TaskOpen(_)
  2445	            | TypedTx::EscrowLock(_)
  2446	            | TypedTx::CompleteSetMint(_)
  2447	            | TypedTx::CompleteSetRedeem(_)
  2448	            | TypedTx::MarketSeed(_) => {}
  2449	        }
  2450	        // TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 6 round-3 (Codex VETO TB13-AUTH 2026-05-03):
  2451	        // submit-time agent-signature verification for the 3 TB-13
  2452	        // conditional-share variants. Opt-in via `set_agent_pubkeys` —
  2453	        // when the manifest is set, forged or unregistered signatures
  2454	        // are rejected pre-queue with `SubmitError::AgentSignatureInvalid`.
  2455	        // When the manifest is absent (default), this gate is bypassed
  2456	        // and replay-time `verify.rs` Gate 4 is the only line of defense
  2457	        // (see OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md).
  2458	        if let Some(manifest) = self.agent_pubkeys.get() {
  2459	            use crate::runtime::agent_keypairs::verify_agent_signature;
  2460	            match &tx {
  2461	                TypedTx::CompleteSetMint(mint) => {
  2462	                    let pubkey = manifest
  2463	                        .get(&mint.owner)
  2464	                        .ok_or(SubmitError::AgentSignatureInvalid)?;
  2465	                    let digest = mint.to_signing_payload().canonical_digest();
  2466	                    if verify_agent_signature(&mint.signature, &digest, &pubkey).is_err() {
  2467	                        return Err(SubmitError::AgentSignatureInvalid);
  2468	                    }
  2469	                }
  2470	                TypedTx::CompleteSetRedeem(redeem) => {
  2471	                    let pubkey = manifest
  2472	                        .get(&redeem.owner)
  2473	                        .ok_or(SubmitError::AgentSignatureInvalid)?;
  2474	                    let digest = redeem.to_signing_payload().canonical_digest();
  2475	                    if verify_agent_signature(&redeem.signature, &digest, &pubkey).is_err() {
  2476	                        return Err(SubmitError::AgentSignatureInvalid);
  2477	                    }
  2478	                }
  2479	                TypedTx::MarketSeed(seed) => {
  2480	                    let pubkey = manifest
  2481	                        .get(&seed.provider)
  2482	                        .ok_or(SubmitError::AgentSignatureInvalid)?;
  2483	                    let digest = seed.to_signing_payload().canonical_digest();
  2484	                    if verify_agent_signature(&seed.signature, &digest, &pubkey).is_err() {
  2485	                        return Err(SubmitError::AgentSignatureInvalid);
  2486	                    }
  2487	                }
  2488	                // Other agent variants are not gated here — codebase-wide
  2489	                // forward-dep per OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP.
  2490	                _ => {}
  2491	            }
  2492	        }
  2493	        // TB-2 P1-D r1 concurrency contract: fetch_add precedes try_send, so
  2494	        // submit_id allocation order is NOT receiver arrival order under
  2495	        // multi-producer scheduling. submit_id is always burned (never reused)
  2496	        // even when try_send fails — locked by integration test I2.
  2978	        envelope: SubmissionEnvelope,
  2979	    ) -> Result<LedgerEntry, ApplyError> {
  2980	        // TB-2 Atom 2: queue payload is SubmissionEnvelope so submit_id
  2981	        // travels with the tx through to apply_one. Atom 4: submit_id is
  2982	        // now actually used for the L4.E rejection-evidence path below.
  2983	        let SubmissionEnvelope { submit_id, tx } = envelope;
  2984	
  2985	        // Stage 1: snapshot Q_t under read lock.
  2986	        let q_snapshot = {
  2987	            let g = self.q.read().map_err(|_| ApplyError::QStateLockPoisoned)?;
  2988	            g.clone()
  2989	        };
  2990	
  2991	        // TB-5 Atom 4 (preflight § 4.5): Stage 1.5 — defense-in-depth signature
  2992	        // verification for system-emitted variants. Even though emit_system_tx
  2993	        // signs the message before queueing, apply_one re-verifies against
  2994	        // pinned_pubkeys here so that any future bypass of emit_system_tx
  2995	        // (or stale signature in a replay) is rejected at the apply boundary.
  2996	        // On verification failure, route to L4.E with InvalidSystemSignatureLive
  2997	        // exactly like a dispatch reject — no logical_t consumed, no state_root
  2998	        // advance.
  2999	        if let Some(msg) = system_message_for_verification(&tx) {
  3000	            use crate::bottom_white::ledger::system_keypair::verify_system_signature;
  3001	            let sig = system_signature_of(&tx)
  3002	                .expect("system_message_for_verification implies system_signature present");
  3003	            // TerminalSummaryTx carries no epoch field (STATE § 1.5 8-field
  3004	            // schema is digest-only); fall back to the apply-time sequencer
  3005	            // epoch. Other system variants carry epoch on the wire.
  3006	            let tx_epoch = system_epoch_of(&tx).unwrap_or(self.epoch);
  3007	            if !verify_system_signature(sig, &msg, tx_epoch, &self.pinned_pubkeys) {
  3008	                let err = TransitionError::InvalidSystemSignatureLive;
  3009	                self.record_rejection(submit_id, &tx, &q_snapshot, &err)?;
  3010	                return Err(ApplyError::Transition(err));
  3011	            }
  3012	        }
  3013	
  3014	        // Stage 2: dispatch (pure). On reject, route to L4.E rejection-evidence
  3015	        // ledger and return early. K1: no logical_t consumed; Inv 7: no
  3016	        // state_root_t / ledger_root_t advance.
  3017	        let (q_next, _signals) = match dispatch_transition(
  3018	            &q_snapshot,
  3019	            &tx,
  3020	            &self.predicate_registry,
  3021	            &self.tool_registry,
  3022	        ) {
  3023	            Ok(ok) => ok,
  3024	            Err(transition_err) => {
  3025	                self.record_rejection(submit_id, &tx, &q_snapshot, &transition_err)?;
  3026	                // No logical_t advance, no state_root advance, no ledger_root
  3027	                // advance. Caller observes ApplyError::Transition.
  3028	                return Err(ApplyError::Transition(transition_err));
  3029	            }
  3030	        };
  3031	
  3032	        // v1.1 C-2: TENTATIVE logical_t (do NOT fetch_add yet).
  3033	        let logical_t = self.next_logical_t.load(Ordering::SeqCst) + 1;
  3034	
  3035	        // Stage 3: put payload to CAS. DIV-5 5-param put signature.
  3036	        let payload_bytes = canonical_encode(&tx)
  3037	            .map_err(|e| ApplyError::PayloadEncode(e.to_string()))?;
  3038	        let payload_cid = {
  3039	            let mut cas_w = self.cas.write().map_err(|_| ApplyError::QStateLockPoisoned)?;
  3040	            cas_w.put(
  3041	                &payload_bytes,
  3042	                ObjectType::ProposalPayload,
  3043	                &format!("sequencer-epoch-{}", self.epoch.get()),
  3044	                logical_t,
  3045	                Some("TypedTx.v1".to_string()),
  3046	            )?
  3047	        };
  3048	
  3049	        // Stage 5: build LedgerEntrySigningPayload (v1.1 — stage 4 fetch_add
  3050	        // moved to AFTER stage 9 commit success).
  3051	        let signing_payload = LedgerEntrySigningPayload {
  3052	            logical_t,
  3053	            parent_state_root: q_snapshot.state_root_t,
  3054	            parent_ledger_root: q_snapshot.ledger_root_t,
  3055	            tx_kind: tx.tx_kind(),
  3056	            tx_payload_cid: payload_cid,
  3057	            resulting_state_root: q_next.state_root_t,
  3058	            timestamp_logical: logical_t,
  3059	            epoch: self.epoch,
  3060	            extensions: std::collections::BTreeMap::new(),
  3061	        };
  3062	
  3063	        // Stage 6: C3 — sign via typed CanonicalMessage::LedgerEntrySigning.
  3064	        let signing_digest = signing_payload.canonical_digest();
  3065	        let system_signature = transition_ledger_emitter::sign_ledger_entry(
  3066	            &self.keypair,
  3067	            signing_digest.0,
  3068	        )?;
  3069	
  3070	        // Stage 7: pure ledger-root fold (deterministic).
  3071	        let resulting_ledger_root = append(&q_snapshot.ledger_root_t, &signing_digest);
  3072	
  3073	        // Stage 8: build LedgerEntry (the stored record).
  3074	        let entry = LedgerEntry {
  3075	            logical_t: signing_payload.logical_t,
  3076	            parent_state_root: signing_payload.parent_state_root,
  3077	            parent_ledger_root: signing_payload.parent_ledger_root,
  3078	            tx_kind: signing_payload.tx_kind,
  3079	            tx_payload_cid: signing_payload.tx_payload_cid,
  3080	            resulting_state_root: signing_payload.resulting_state_root,
  3081	            resulting_ledger_root,
  3082	            timestamp_logical: signing_payload.timestamp_logical,
  3083	            epoch: signing_payload.epoch,
  3084	            extensions: signing_payload.extensions,
  3085	            system_signature,
  3086	        };
  3087	
  3088	        // Stage 9: commit + mutate Q_t under write lock.
  3089	        // v1.1 C-2: next_logical_t.store(logical_t) HAPPENS ONLY AFTER
  3090	        // writer.commit succeeds — preserves K1 under infra failure.

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md | sed -n '1,220p'" in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# OBS R-022 — TB-13 ResolutionRef removed (TRACE_MATRIX backlink retired)
     2	
     3	**Date**: 2026-05-03 (TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ5 remediation).
     4	**Triggered by**: pre-commit hook R-022 (TRACE_MATRIX pub-symbol-block).
     5	**Removed symbol**: `pub struct ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` in `src/state/typed_tx.rs`.
     6	**Removed backlink**: `/// TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 1 (architect §4.3): system-resolution reference embedded in CompleteSetRedeemTx`.
     7	
     8	## Why removal is correct
     9	
    10	ResolutionRef carried two dead fields:
    11	
    12	- `resolution_tx_id: TxId` — documented in round-4 as opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4 (the sequencer ignores it; `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state` is the live truth-of-resolution). A field that no code consumes is wire weight, not architecture.
    13	- `claimed_outcome: OutcomeSide` — a redundant copy of `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`. Used only by an inner-consistency gate `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome → InvalidResolutionRef`. That gate was dead defense-in-depth: the agent signature covers both fields, so any tampering that desynchronized them would break the signature first.
    14	
    15	Codex round-3 RQ5 raised this as the contract drift between doc and code. The previous session's round-4 closure was a doc-only fix that left the dead wire field locked into the on-disk encoding. This round-5 commit is the structural fix: drop the wrapper entirely, rely on `redeem.outcome` as the sole claim, preserve the state-mismatch rejection path via the existing match arm.
    16	
    17	## Why the TRACE_MATRIX entry does not need a replacement
    18	
    19	The TB-13 typed-tx surface is still flowchart-anchored — `CompleteSetRedeemTx` itself retains its `TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 1 (architect §4.3 + FR-13.4..5 + SG-13.5..6)` backlink at `src/state/typed_tx.rs`. ResolutionRef was an internal helper type without an independent flowchart role. Removing the type cleanly removes the backlink — there is no orphan code; the role it carried (claimed-outcome assertion) is now expressed directly via `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`.
    20	
    21	## Behavioral preservation evidence
    22	
    23	- `cargo test --workspace = 789 passed / 0 failed / 150 ignored` (= round-3 baseline; no regression).
    24	- SG-13.5 (`sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution`): PASS unchanged — Open / Expired states still rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`.
    25	- SG-13.6 (`sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`): PASS unchanged — including both mismatch checks (Finalized + outcome=No → `InvalidResolutionRef`; Bankrupt + outcome=Yes → `InvalidResolutionRef`). The match arm using `(market_state, redeem.outcome)` produces identical rejection behavior to the previous `(market_state, claimed_outcome)` form because all fixtures set `claimed_outcome = outcome` (the inner-consistency check could never independently fire on an honestly-signed tx).
    26	
    27	## Wire-format note
    28	
    29	This is a wire-format break. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`: no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx; no migration is needed. Future TB-13 chaintape entries will use the 8-field `CompleteSetRedeemTx` shape directly.
    30	
    31	## Cross-references
    32	
    33	- Codex audit RQ5 finding: `handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md` (round-3).
    34	- Round-4 doc-only fix (now superseded by structural fix): `handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md` §12.7 TB13-RQ5.
    35	- Fix handoff that mandated this round: `handover/ai-direct/TB-13_FIX_HANDOFF_2026-05-03.md` §3.3.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# OBS — ResolutionsIndex evolution for TB-15+ (Gemini Q12 CHALLENGE)
     2	
     3	**Date**: 2026-05-03 (TB-13 round-3 audit closure).
     4	**Status**: OBS (observation; tracked for future TB).
     5	**Triggered by**: Gemini TB-13 ship audit round-3 Q12 CHALLENGE
     6	(`handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md` §Q12).
     7	**Conflict-resolution rule**: Codex round-3 explicit "No VETO"; Gemini Q12
     8	explicitly "non-blocking, recommends OBS rather than blocks ship".
     9	
    10	## Summary
    11	
    12	TB-13's `ResolutionRef` model couples redemption logic to specific
    13	resolution-emitting transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx` for No,
    14	`FinalizeRewardTx` for Yes). The validation lives in the
    15	`CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882-1900`
    16	as a hardcoded `match (market_state, claimed_outcome)`.
    17	
    18	**Forward concern**: TB-15+ may introduce additional resolution
    19	mechanisms (ChallengeCourt rulings, oracle updates, multi-resolver
    20	quorum). Each new resolver requires modifying the `CompleteSetRedeemTx`
    21	dispatch arm to recognize the new tx type / state mapping.
    22	
    23	A more abstract long-term design — proposed by Gemini — is a canonical
    24	`ResolutionsIndex` in `QState`:
    25	
    26	```rust
    27	/// Maps event_id → resolved outcome side. Set by ANY system-emitted
    28	/// resolution transaction (TaskBankruptcy, FinalizeReward, future
    29	/// ChallengeCourt, future Oracle, etc.). Read-only after set.
    30	pub struct ResolutionsIndex(pub BTreeMap<EventId, OutcomeSide>);
    31	```
    32	
    33	The redemption logic would then be a single, decoupled lookup:
    34	```rust
    35	let outcome = q.economic_state_t.resolutions_t.0
    36	    .get(&redeem.event_id)
    37	    .ok_or(TransitionError::RedeemBeforeResolution)?;
    38	if redeem.outcome != *outcome {
    39	    return Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef);
    40	}
    41	```
    42	
    43	New resolvers add a write site to populate `resolutions_t` without
    44	touching the redemption arm.
    45	
    46	## Why this is OBS, not blocking
    47	
    48	Per Gemini R3: "The current implementation is not a bug but represents
    49	an architectural choice that incurs future refactoring debt. ...The
    50	recommended 'fix' is to add an OBS to the project backlog to track the
    51	redesign of the resolution mechanism for TB-15, rather than blocking
    52	the TB-13 ship."
    53	
    54	Per Codex R3: "No VETO: I found no live money/collateral exploit in the
    55	TB-13 dispatch arms." Codex's RQ5 challenge on `resolution_tx_id`
    56	opacity was addressed in round-4 doc remediation (2026-05-03 commit
    57	post-this-OBS).
    58	
    59	## Closure plan (TB-15 prerequisite)
    60	
    61	- Extend `EconomicState` with `resolutions_t: ResolutionsIndex` (14th
    62	  sub-field; backward-compat `#[serde(default)]`).
    63	- Refactor `TaskBankruptcyTx` and `FinalizeRewardTx` dispatch arms to
    64	  populate `resolutions_t` alongside their existing state-flip logic.
    65	- Refactor `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm to consult `resolutions_t`
    66	  instead of pattern-matching on `task_markets_t.state`.
    67	- Make `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` validation explicit (lookup in
    68	  L4, verify it's the actual tx that resolved this event) OR drop the
    69	  field as opaque metadata per Codex round-3 RQ5.
    70	
    71	## Cross-references
    72	
    73	- Gemini TB-13 R3 audit Q12 CHALLENGE: `handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md`
    74	- Codex TB-13 R3 audit RQ5 CHALLENGE: `handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md`
    75	- Recursive self-audit round-3 closure: `handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md` §12.6
    76	- TB-13 charter: `handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md`
    77	- Architect 2026-05-02 supplementary directive (TB-15 scope): `handover/directives/2026-05-02_TB11_TO_TB17_SUPPLEMENTARY_DIRECTIVE.md`
     1	# OBS — TB-13 round-3 audit residual CHALLENGES (ship-with-OBS)
     2	
     3	**Date**: 2026-05-03 (TB-13 round-3 closure).
     4	**Status**: OBS (observations; tracked for follow-up).
     5	**Triggered by**: Codex + Gemini TB-13 round-3 ship audits.
     6	**Per `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`**: VETO > CHALLENGE > PASS. Both
     7	auditors round-3 verdict = CHALLENGE-only (no VETO). Codex explicit:
     8	"No VETO: I found no live money/collateral exploit in the TB-13
     9	dispatch arms."
    10	**Per `feedback_elon_mode_policy`**: OBS-threshold-3 → ship-with-OBS
    11	allowed when CHALLENGES are not enforcement-gate failures themselves.
    12	The 5 Codex + 1 Gemini CHALLENGES are documentation drift, fence
    13	discovery edge cases, smoke evidence scope, STEP_B process artifact,
    14	and architectural future-evolution — none are enforcement-gate
    15	failures. Ship with OBS-tracking for follow-up.
    16	
    17	## Round-4 closure (this commit)
    18	
    19	- **TB13-Q5-DOC** (Codex): docs in `q_state.rs` claimed strict
    20	  `YES == NO == collateral` invariant; updated to MIN form per the
    21	  live `assert_complete_set_balanced` implementation. ✓ FIXED.
    22	- **TB13-RQ5** (Codex): `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` documented as
    23	  "L4-validated" while sequencer uses `task_markets_t.state` as the
    24	  live truth-of-resolution. Updated typed_tx.rs doc-comments to
    25	  declare `resolution_tx_id` OPAQUE traceability metadata, NOT
    26	  validated against L4. ✓ FIXED.
    27	
    28	## Carry-forward CHALLENGES (this OBS)
    29	
    30	### TB13-Q9/RQ6 — forward-fence discovery edge case
    31	
    32	**Codex finding**: `discover_tb_13_files()` walks `src/` for files
    33	containing TB-13 authoring markers. A NEW TB-13 contributing file that
    34	does NOT carry a TB-13 authoring marker AND lives outside
    35	`FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR` would bypass discovery.
    36	
    37	**Status**: by the fence's own contract, "TB-13 contributing code"
    38	requires a TB-13 authoring marker (`TRACE_MATRIX TB-13` or `// TB-13 `
    39	prefix). An unmarked file is not TB-13 by the project's own
    40	trace-matrix discipline. Codex's challenge is hypothetical:
    41	"contributor forgets to mark a TB-13 file" — caught by code review +
    42	the fact that any TB-13 contribution that *uses* legacy CPMM API
    43	would still trip the layer-1 hard-banned-import scan in any of the
    44	FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR files (typed_tx, sequencer, etc.) because it would
    45	need to import or reference legacy types.
    46	
    47	**Closure plan**: If TB-14+ adds new modules (e.g.,
    48	`src/economy/conditional_market.rs`), append them to
    49	`FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR`. The discovery walk is best-effort defense-in-
    50	depth; FENCE_SCOPE_FLOOR is the contract.
    51	
    52	### TB13-RQ3 — non-empty TB-13 replay determinism not directly evidenced
    53	
    54	**Codex finding**: the real-LLM regression smoke at
    55	`handover/evidence/tb_13_real_llm_smoke_2026-05-03/` proves
    56	EconomicState 13-sub-field schema reconstruction with EMPTY TB-13 maps
    57	(no TB-13 typed-tx submitted by the LLM-driven solver path). Direct
    58	evidence of non-empty `conditional_collateral_t` /
    59	`conditional_share_balances_t` round-trip via verify_chaintape is not
    60	present in the smoke evidence package.
    61	
    62	**Status**: TB-13 integration tests (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs`)
    63	exercise non-empty mint/redeem/seed flows + assert post-mutation
    64	state. The unit tests for canonical encode/decode round-trip
    65	(`tb_13_complete_set_mint_round_trips_canonical` etc.) verify wire-
    66	level determinism. Cross-instance verify_chaintape replay of a
    67	runtime_repo containing TB-13 typed-tx is not yet demonstrated.
    68	
    69	**Closure plan**: Phase D / TB-16 (Controlled Market Smoke Arena) per
    70	the post-TB-12 architect directive will generate non-empty TB-13
    71	chaintape under sandbox conditions; verify_chaintape replay will
    72	serve as the direct-evidence smoke. For TB-13 alone, the integration
    73	tests + canonical round-trip + fence discipline are sufficient.
    74	
    75	### TB13-RQ7 — STEP_B process artifact missing for sequencer.rs Class 3 change
    76	
    77	**Codex finding**: `src/state/sequencer.rs` is restricted per
    78	`CLAUDE.md` ("STEP_B_PROTOCOL（不直接编辑 main）"). TB-13 charter §3
    79	references STEP_B parallel-branch A/B for sequencer changes but no
    80	explicit STEP_B artifact (preflight + parallel branch + merge log)
    81	was produced for the TB-13 dispatch additions.
    82	
    83	**Status**: TB-12 precedent (`fa36eca`) extended `src/state/sequencer.rs`
    84	with NodePosition dispatch arms via direct edit on main, without a
    85	STEP_B preflight artifact. TB-11 / TB-8 / TB-5 followed the same
    86	direct-edit precedent for additive dispatch-arm changes. The STEP_B
    87	parallel-branch protocol is reserved for changes that modify EXISTING
    88	logic; pure-additive variants follow the lighter-weight direct-edit
    89	precedent.
    90	
    91	**Closure plan**: ratify the TB-12+ direct-edit precedent for
    92	additive dispatch-arm changes by amending `STEP_B_PROTOCOL.md` to
    93	document the additive-only carve-out. Tracked separately in
    94	`OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md` (existing OBS).
    95	
    96	### Gemini Q12 — ResolutionsIndex for TB-15+
    97	
    98	**Status**: tracked at `OBS_RESOLUTIONS_INDEX_TB15_2026-05-03.md`.
    99	
   100	## Cross-references
   101	
   102	- Codex TB-13 round-3 audit: `handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md`
   103	- Gemini TB-13 round-3 audit: `handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md`
   104	- Recursive self-audit round-3 closure: `handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md` §12.6
   105	- TB-13 charter: `handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md`
   106	- Existing STEP_B drift OBS: `handover/alignment/OBS_STEP_B_RESTRICTED_FILE_LIST_DRIFT_2026-04-29.md`

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:273:**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4062:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4109:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4286:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.

Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4987:genesis_payload.toml:223:# 2026-05-01 TB-6 Atom 4 — replay verifier library (NEW file). Re-opens runtime_repo + cas + pinned_pubkeys.json, replays L4 chain via replay_full_transition, emits ReplayReport. All pub symbols carry /// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N1 backlinks per R-022.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6131:    44	2. Pass `apply_one` stage 1.5 (system-only verification skips agent variants).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6537:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6540:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6577:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_5_2026-04-30.md:32:| Q3 | Defense-in-depth signature verification at apply_one stage 1.5 + emit-time | § 4.5 | sequencer.rs `verify_emitted_system_tx_signature` at emit-time + apply_one stage 1.5 (`system_message_for_verification` exhaustive helper for 4 system variants → `verify_system_signature` against pinned_pubkeys → `record_rejection` on failure) | sequencer.rs::tests stage_1_5_rejects_forged_{challenge_resolve,finalize_reward,task_expire,terminal_summary}_signature (4 tests) + stage_1_5_accepts_emit_system_tx_self_signed_challenge_resolve (U27) + stage_1_5_skipped_for_agent_variants (U28) | ✅ GREEN |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6594:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)). Replay-time detection in `verify.rs` is not an admission control for a Class 3 money/collateral mutator ([verify.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/runtime/verify.rs:442)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6596:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6610:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)).
