handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:64:*   **(Q4.a) Round-trip preserved**: **PASS**. All new fields in `PputResult` are `Option<T>` and use `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` (`evaluator.rs:82-93`). This is the canonical and correct way to ensure that deserializing old data and re-serializing it produces identical output.
handover/audits/CO1_7_EXTRA_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R3_2026-04-29.md:60:- **B4a `#[serde(skip)]` conditional**: § 2.1 shows `#[serde(skip)]` on the new `TuringBus.sequencer` field unconditionally, but `src/bus.rs:53` `pub struct TuringBus` has no `Serialize/Deserialize` derive currently — the literal `#[serde(skip)]` would not be invalid syntactically (serde-skip without serde just no-ops) but is misleading documentation. v1.1 spec already has a comment "applied if TuringBus has Serialize/Deserialize" (line ~167); Codex flags that this conditional should be more explicit at the code-comment level.

handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:64:**RQ5 — drop ResolutionRef wrapper**: ResolutionRef struct + resolution_tx_id + claimed_outcome fields removed from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload. Inner-consistency check removed from sequencer. State-mismatch path preserved via existing match arm using `redeem.outcome` directly. CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; signing payload 8→7. Wire-format break — no production rows yet. Trust Root rehashed for typed_tx / sequencer / transition_ledger. R-022 skip token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:66:**Q9/RQ6 — type-use forward-fence discovery**: `tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs` now has `TB_13_TYPE_NAMES` + `discover_by_type_use` walking src/ for non-comment uses of TB-13 type names. Catches contributors who import TB-13 types without authoring markers. New unit test exercises caught/skipped/neutral cases.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7542:One more compile detail: § 2.1 says to put `#[serde(skip)]` on `TuringBus.sequencer` only “if TuringBus has serde derives.” Current `TuringBus` has no derive. I’m confirming that this conditional wording is necessary, because an unconditional helper attribute would be a compile error.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7545:struct S { #[serde(skip)] x: u8 }
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7632:- `#[serde(skip)]` must remain conditional. Current `TuringBus` has no serde derive ([bus.rs:53](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:53)); adding `#[serde(skip)]` literally would be invalid unless a serde derive is added.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7636:Not PASS-gated yet. The design is implementable end-to-end after the small v1.1 spec patch: make `advance_head_t` publicly testable or move the test, change `&**writer_w` to `&*writer_w`, remove stale Kernel wording, and clarify the serde-skip conditional.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7706:- `#[serde(skip)]` must remain conditional. Current `TuringBus` has no serde derive ([bus.rs:53](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bus.rs:53)); adding `#[serde(skip)]` literally would be invalid unless a serde derive is added.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_EXTRA_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7710:Not PASS-gated yet. The design is implementable end-to-end after the small v1.1 spec patch: make `advance_head_t` publicly testable or move the test, change `&**writer_w` to `&*writer_w`, remove stale Kernel wording, and clarify the serde-skip conditional.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:180:- (Q1.c) Does `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` fail closed when `python3` is missing? Verify by reading the test source (appended to this packet) + checking that the panic message describes the failure rather than a silent skip.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:299:3. (Codex R3#3) `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` returns success when `python3` is missing (soft skip via `eprintln + return`). For a VETO-closing conformance gate, missing python3 should fail closed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:328:3. (Codex R4#3) `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring still says "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" — contradicts H6's fail-closed runtime.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:503:1. (**Codex R10#1 — substantive**) `_smoke_siliconflow.py` doesn't fail closed on missing keys: the script skipped unset secondary/tertiary and returned PASS if any configured key responded. The packet's "PASS (3/3 keys)" claim was strictly stronger than what the smoke verified — a primary-only setup would silently PASS, replicating exactly the V3L-27 single-key collapse pattern the 3-key pool was meant to mitigate.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:596:Cumulative cost so far ~$70 (12 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 13 substantive findings caught + closed across 11 rounds**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5622:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:5623:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:9303:handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:379:Cumulative cost so far ~$70 (12 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 13 substantive findings caught + closed across 11 rounds**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:12320:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3784:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:45:-   **(Q3.d) Silent drop in `compute_p0.py`**: **CHALLENGE**. The script at `compute_p0.py:56-58` silently skips rows that are missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed`. A failure in the runner script (`run_p0_calibration.sh:170-177`) to stamp a row would cause that data point to be silently dropped, biasing the `p_0` result without warning. The script should fail loudly if any row from either input file is missing the required keys.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:12444:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4179:./handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:50:    # are stamped by run_p0_calibration.sh. Be defensive: skip rows missing
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:12505:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5372:   PREREG requires full adaptation-144 × seeds and denominator `/144` ([PREREG §5.5 lines 446-451](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:446)). But the runner swallows timeout/crash with `|| true` ([line 140](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:140)) and emits only `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` without a JSON row ([line 168](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:168)). The estimator skips missing tags ([compute_p0.py:55](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:55)), intersects only present pairs ([line 65](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:65)), and divides by observed problems ([line 91](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:91)). Fix: require exactly 144 problems, exactly 288 paired rows per mode, exact seeds `{31415,2718}`, no duplicates, no missing calibration tags, and abort on any `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` unless an explicit pre-registered retry policy handles it.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:12512:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5408:   PREREG requires full adaptation-144 × seeds and denominator `/144` ([PREREG §5.5 lines 446-451](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:446)). But the runner swallows timeout/crash with `|| true` ([line 140](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:140)) and emits only `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` without a JSON row ([line 168](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:168)). The estimator skips missing tags ([compute_p0.py:55](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:55)), intersects only present pairs ([line 65](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:65)), and divides by observed problems ([line 91](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:91)). Fix: require exactly 144 problems, exactly 288 paired rows per mode, exact seeds `{31415,2718}`, no duplicates, no missing calibration tags, and abort on any `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` unless an explicit pre-registered retry policy handles it.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:13829:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4898:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:79:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. If the runner script's row-enrichment failed for any subset (e.g., python3 OOM'd on one row), would calibration silently miss data? Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:13841:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4911:./handover/audits/run_gemini_b7_extra_reaudit.py:53:| Q3.d: silent skip in compute_p0 | CHALLENGE | 15b87fb: strict-completeness — fails loudly on missing tags / duplicates / seed mismatch / N≠144 |
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:13860:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4931:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:45:-   **(Q3.d) Silent drop in `compute_p0.py`**: **CHALLENGE**. The script at `compute_p0.py:56-58` silently skips rows that are missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed`. A failure in the runner script (`run_p0_calibration.sh:170-177`) to stamp a row would cause that data point to be silently dropped, biasing the `p_0` result without warning. The script should fail loudly if any row from either input file is missing the required keys.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:14289:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5702:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3933:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3618:handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:135:                echo "[$RUN_IDX/$TOTAL_RUNS] $MODE seed=$SEED $PID — PROBLEM_NOT_FOUND, skip"
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:14313:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5726:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4179:./handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:50:    # are stamped by run_p0_calibration.sh. Be defensive: skip rows missing
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:14333:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5790:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5372:   PREREG requires full adaptation-144 × seeds and denominator `/144` ([PREREG §5.5 lines 446-451](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:446)). But the runner swallows timeout/crash with `|| true` ([line 140](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:140)) and emits only `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` without a JSON row ([line 168](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:168)). The estimator skips missing tags ([compute_p0.py:55](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:55)), intersects only present pairs ([line 65](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:65)), and divides by observed problems ([line 91](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:91)). Fix: require exactly 144 problems, exactly 288 paired rows per mode, exact seeds `{31415,2718}`, no duplicates, no missing calibration tags, and abort on any `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` unless an explicit pre-registered retry policy handles it.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:14340:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5797:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5408:   PREREG requires full adaptation-144 × seeds and denominator `/144` ([PREREG §5.5 lines 446-451](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:446)). But the runner swallows timeout/crash with `|| true` ([line 140](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:140)) and emits only `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` without a JSON row ([line 168](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:168)). The estimator skips missing tags ([compute_p0.py:55](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:55)), intersects only present pairs ([line 65](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:65)), and divides by observed problems ([line 91](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:91)). Fix: require exactly 144 problems, exactly 288 paired rows per mode, exact seeds `{31415,2718}`, no duplicates, no missing calibration tags, and abort on any `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` unless an explicit pre-registered retry policy handles it.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:15157:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4179:./handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:50:    # are stamped by run_p0_calibration.sh. Be defensive: skip rows missing
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:15218:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5372:   PREREG requires full adaptation-144 × seeds and denominator `/144` ([PREREG §5.5 lines 446-451](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:446)). But the runner swallows timeout/crash with `|| true` ([line 140](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:140)) and emits only `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` without a JSON row ([line 168](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:168)). The estimator skips missing tags ([compute_p0.py:55](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:55)), intersects only present pairs ([line 65](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:65)), and divides by observed problems ([line 91](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:91)). Fix: require exactly 144 problems, exactly 288 paired rows per mode, exact seeds `{31415,2718}`, no duplicates, no missing calibration tags, and abort on any `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` unless an explicit pre-registered retry policy handles it.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:15225:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5408:   PREREG requires full adaptation-144 × seeds and denominator `/144` ([PREREG §5.5 lines 446-451](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:446)). But the runner swallows timeout/crash with `|| true` ([line 140](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:140)) and emits only `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` without a JSON row ([line 168](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:168)). The estimator skips missing tags ([compute_p0.py:55](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:55)), intersects only present pairs ([line 65](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:65)), and divides by observed problems ([line 91](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:91)). Fix: require exactly 144 problems, exactly 288 paired rows per mode, exact seeds `{31415,2718}`, no duplicates, no missing calibration tags, and abort on any `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` unless an explicit pre-registered retry policy handles it.

handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:14:| Codex | **CHALLENGE** | High | (1) sequencer logical_t skip race; (2) signing payload binds epoch + parent_ledger_root; (3) replay two-mode + CAS cold-replay |
handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:36:| **K1** | **Sequencer logical_t skip race** — `fetch_add` before accept skips on rejection; skeleton + replay reject gaps | Q-C top-1 | Either (a) separate counters for submissions vs accepted entries (assign logical_t at COMMIT time, not submit), or (b) rejection entries become first-class L4 entries with logical_t. Recommend (a) for v4 minimalism. |
handover/audits/CO1_7_DUAL_AUDIT_VERDICT_R1_2026-04-28.md:115:**Recommended**: **A**. Both auditors high-conviction CHALLENGE = real defects, not principle. v1.1 is a single concentrated patch round; skipping items will cost more in round-3+.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1413:    19	**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5275:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:5276:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.

handover/audits/STATUS_REPORT_TO_ARCHITECT_2026-04-29_overnight.md:163:**有讨论空间。**最小修法：在 `RejectedSubmissionRecord` 上加 `#[serde(skip_serializing)]` 到 `raw_diagnostic_cid` 字段（30 分钟），让外部 serialize 路径自动屏蔽。Codex 的更强修法：把 `RejectedSubmissionRecord` 改 `pub(crate)`，外部仅暴露 `PublicRejectionView` + 一个 `pub(crate)` 的 audit-only API。强修法更安全但破坏 module 间的可见性约定。
handover/audits/STATUS_REPORT_TO_ARCHITECT_2026-04-29_overnight.md:165:我**倾向最小修法**：`#[serde(skip_serializing)]`。它把 convention 升级到 type-system enforcement（Serde 在 wire form 永远不带这个字段），同时不破坏 `pub` 可见性。但这是个有 trade-off 的判断。

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5408:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5409:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:5450:| `judge.sh` constitution.md special case | `.claude/hooks/judge.sh:50-67` | FC3-N3 sudo-gate enforcement (closes silent-bypass via `*.md` skip-list) | ✅ |

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R8.md:9404:    93	  codex exec --skip-git-repo-check < "$TMP_PROMPT" 2>&1
handover/audits/run_codex_co1_1_4_pre1_round5_audit.sh:72:  codex exec --skip-git-repo-check < "$TMP_PROMPT" 2>&1
handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:48:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:95:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:136:cat "$TMP_PROMPT" | codex exec --skip-git-repo-check --sandbox read-only --color never - > "$OUT.raw" 2>&1
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:101:- Codex R3: **CHALLENGE / high** — 3 narrow findings (A8 packet line 118 still calls substitution "conservative"; packet § 3 A6 atom + Q4.a still say "FC1-N12 only in oneshot" + Q4.d still describes ms drift; G1 wrapper soft-skips on missing python3).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:114:- **H6** (Codex R3#3): G1 wrapper test fails closed when `python3` is missing — was a soft skip via `eprintln + return`. Explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgraded runs (logged loudly).

handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:44:**RQ5 fix — drop ResolutionRef wrapper** (commit `edbc555`):
handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:45:- `src/state/typed_tx.rs`: ResolutionRef struct deleted; CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; CompleteSetRedeemSigningPayload 8→7 fields. `to_signing_payload` projection drops `resolution_ref`. `InvalidResolutionRef` doc-comment + Display now read "outcome does not match state" (was "resolution_ref.claimed_outcome").

handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:50:- R-022 skip token issued for the removed ResolutionRef pub-symbol backlink. Justification at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:60:- New unit test `discover_by_type_use_catches_unmarked_imports_and_skips_doc_xref` validates: (a) unmarked file using CompleteSetMintTx is caught; (b) doc-xref-only file (only /// or //! mentions) is skipped; (c) marker walk alone misses the unmarked file (proves type-use is the load-bearing layer).
handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:87:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:107:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:111:**Round-5 R-022 skip-token**: ResolutionRef pub struct removed; the R-022 hook caught the missing TRACE_MATRIX backlink. The skip-token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` justifies the removal. Confirm the OBS doc is sufficient: does it explain why no replacement TRACE_MATRIX entry is needed?
handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.sh:147:cat "$TMP_PROMPT" | codex exec --skip-git-repo-check --sandbox read-only --color never - > "$OUT.raw" 2>&1
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:35:*   *Strategic view*: It is rigorous, but *not* over-engineered. A developer can legitimately create a new `cases/Cxxx.yaml` file and use the `[R-022-skip: cases/Cxxx]` token in the *same commit*. Because the pre-commit hook runs against the working tree / staged index, the script will successfully find the newly created `Cxxx` file on disk. This perfectly supports the "document as you build" workflow.
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:40:*   **Form vs Substance (§ 2.5)**: ✅ Codified. The two-layer model (R-022 enforces syntactic form; § F reverse-map enables semantic substance review) is a mature systems engineering pattern (Eventual Consistency applied to alignment).
handover/audits/GEMINI_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:66:This atom represents a turning point for TuringOS v4. By establishing R-022, you are moving constitutional alignment from a "best effort" human review process to a cryptographically verifiable, machine-enforced gate.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:5107:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature is now 21 positional args (Gemini R3 Finding 2) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:29:edbc555  TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ5 remediation: drop ResolutionRef wrapper
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:38:**RQ5 fix — drop ResolutionRef wrapper** (commit `edbc555`):
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:39:- `src/state/typed_tx.rs`: ResolutionRef struct deleted; CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; CompleteSetRedeemSigningPayload 8→7 fields. `to_signing_payload` projection drops `resolution_ref`. `InvalidResolutionRef` doc-comment + Display now read "outcome does not match state" (was "resolution_ref.claimed_outcome").
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:42:- `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs`: ResolutionRef import + build_redeem fixture pruned. SG-13.5 / SG-13.6 unchanged in behavior.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:44:- R-022 skip token issued for the removed ResolutionRef pub-symbol backlink. Justification at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:54:- New unit test `discover_by_type_use_catches_unmarked_imports_and_skips_doc_xref` validates: (a) unmarked file using CompleteSetMintTx is caught; (b) doc-xref-only file (only /// or //! mentions) is skipped; (c) marker walk alone misses the unmarked file (proves type-use is the load-bearing layer).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:81:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:101:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:105:**Round-5 R-022 skip-token**: ResolutionRef pub struct removed; the R-022 hook caught the missing TRACE_MATRIX backlink. The skip-token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` justifies the removal. Confirm the OBS doc is sufficient: does it explain why no replacement TRACE_MATRIX entry is needed?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1178:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:55:edbc555  TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ5 remediation: drop ResolutionRef wrapper
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1179:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:64:**RQ5 — drop ResolutionRef wrapper**: ResolutionRef struct + resolution_tx_id + claimed_outcome fields removed from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload. Inner-consistency check removed from sequencer. State-mismatch path preserved via existing match arm using `redeem.outcome` directly. CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; signing payload 8→7. Wire-format break — no production rows yet. Trust Root rehashed for typed_tx / sequencer / transition_ledger. R-022 skip token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1182:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:76:3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` against a Bankrupt event bypass the outcome check? (Must be rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate — single state-vs-outcome match arm now; no inner-consistency check.)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1186:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:88:12. With ResolutionRef now removed (round-5 RQ5), is the redeem-mechanism-coupling concern from your round-3 Q12 CHALLENGE addressed, partially addressed, or unchanged? Specifically: does removing the resolution_ref wrapper make TB-15+ multi-resolver evolution easier (fewer wire fields to reshape) or harder (lost a future-extension hook)?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1189:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:96:- **R-022 skip-token**: Confirm `OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` is sufficient justification for the missing TRACE_MATRIX backlink (the symbol is gone; its role is absorbed by `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1191:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:191:    f"**Round-5 closures**: RQ3 (chaintape replay smoke) + Q9/RQ6 (type-use fence) + RQ5 (ResolutionRef wrapper drop)\n"
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1195:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1205:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1208:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:273:**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1216:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:314:            ResolutionRef pointing to a system-emitted resolution (no
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1217:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:317:            ResolutionRef.outcome state != Resolved or if challenge
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1229:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:423:  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1231:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:425:  - `CompleteSetRedeemTx { tx_id, parent_state_root, event_id, owner, outcome: OutcomeSide, share_amount: ShareAmount, resolution_ref: ResolutionRef, signature }` agent-callable but pre-resolution rejected at sequencer.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1252:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:817:    pub signature_or_system_resolution_ref: ResolutionRef,
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1269:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1172:| Art. III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1278:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1213:| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1279:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1214:| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1285:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1265:| SG-13.6   redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no                | ✓ pass (exact)      | `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (also covers symmetric Bankrupt → No path + 2 mismatch InvalidResolutionRef checks) |

Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1497:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4109:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1523:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4517:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1535:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4962:tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:56:    MarketSeedTx, OutcomeSide, ResolutionRef, ShareAmount, TypedTx,
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1622:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6537:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1625:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6540:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1629:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6594:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)). Replay-time detection in `verify.rs` is not an admission control for a Class 3 money/collateral mutator ([verify.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/runtime/verify.rs:442)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1630:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6596:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1632:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6610:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)). Replay-time detection in `verify.rs` is not an admission control for a Class 3 money/collateral mutator ([verify.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/runtime/verify.rs:442)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1633:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6612:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1640:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit.py:50:3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a TaskBankruptcy-style resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? (Must be rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`.)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1644:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit.py:62:12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1657:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1667:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1670:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1413:    19	**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).

Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1762:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:2474:    66	| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1763:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:2475:    67	| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2374:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:39:- `src/state/typed_tx.rs`: ResolutionRef struct deleted; CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; CompleteSetRedeemSigningPayload 8→7 fields. `to_signing_payload` projection drops `resolution_ref`. `InvalidResolutionRef` doc-comment + Display now read "outcome does not match state" (was "resolution_ref.claimed_outcome").
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2376:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:42:- `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs`: ResolutionRef import + build_redeem fixture pruned. SG-13.5 / SG-13.6 unchanged in behavior.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2377:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:44:- R-022 skip token issued for the removed ResolutionRef pub-symbol backlink. Justification at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2379:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:54:- New unit test `discover_by_type_use_catches_unmarked_imports_and_skips_doc_xref` validates: (a) unmarked file using CompleteSetMintTx is caught; (b) doc-xref-only file (only /// or //! mentions) is skipped; (c) marker walk alone misses the unmarked file (proves type-use is the load-bearing layer).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2384:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:81:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2390:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:101:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2391:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:105:**Round-5 R-022 skip-token**: ResolutionRef pub struct removed; the R-022 hook caught the missing TRACE_MATRIX backlink. The skip-token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` justifies the removal. Confirm the OBS doc is sufficient: does it explain why no replacement TRACE_MATRIX entry is needed?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2406:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:270:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2426:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:810:  1720	                    return Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef);
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2439:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:48:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2449:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:95:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2489:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:6:**Round-5 closures**: RQ3 (chaintape replay smoke) + Q9/RQ6 (type-use fence) + RQ5 (ResolutionRef wrapper drop)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2490:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:24:3.  The removal of the `ResolutionRef` wrapper (RQ5) simplifies the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` wire format and, critically, resolves the architectural concern I raised in round-3 regarding future extensibility. The new design, which couples redemption to the canonical `TaskMarketState` enum rather than a specific transaction type, is cleaner and more forward-compatible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2492:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:36:-   **RQ5 wire-format break**: **PASS**. The removal of `ResolutionRef` from `CompleteSetRedeemTx` at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1178` constitutes a wire-format break. As TB-13 has not shipped and has no production data, this is acceptable and desirable. The accompanying skip-token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` provides a correct and sufficient justification, noting that the removed fields were either unused (`resolution_tx_id`) or redundant (`claimed_outcome`). No persisted artifacts encoding the old shape were found in the codebase.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2493:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:40:-   **R-022 skip-token**: **PASS**. The justification in `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` is sound. The `ResolutionRef` struct was correctly identified as carrying dead weight. Its role is fully and more cleanly absorbed by the `outcome` field on `CompleteSetRedeemTx` itself, which is validated directly against the canonical `TaskMarketState`. The TRACE_MATRIX backlink is correctly retired as the symbol it pointed to is gone.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2496:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:53:    **Finding**: **PASS**. No. The `ResolutionRef` wrapper has been removed, simplifying the check. The `match` arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1889` now directly compares `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`. A `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)` tuple correctly falls through to the `InvalidResolutionRef` rejection path. The logic is sound and more direct than before.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2502:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:83:12. **With ResolutionRef now removed, is the redeem-mechanism-coupling concern from your round-3 Q12 CHALLENGE addressed?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2503:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:84:    **Finding**: **PASS (Addressed)**. Yes, the concern is fully and elegantly addressed. My previous challenge noted that coupling redemption to specific transaction types via `ResolutionRef` was brittle. By removing `ResolutionRef` and having the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` check the canonical `task_markets_t.state` enum directly, the design is now much more extensible. If a future TB introduces a new resolution mechanism (e.g., a `ChallengeCourtRulingTx`), it only needs to mutate `task_markets_t.state` to a resolved value. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` logic will not need to be changed at all. This is a significant architectural improvement.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2505:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md:97:**Rationale**: The TB-13 implementation is now in an excellent state. The round-5 fixes have not only closed the specific challenges raised but have resulted in a simpler, more robust, and more extensible design. The addition of cryptographic proof for non-empty replay determinism provides strong assurance. My primary architectural concern from the previous round has been fully resolved by the removal of `ResolutionRef`. There are no remaining VETO or CHALLENGE-level issues.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2549:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2559:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2560:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:233:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "CompleteSet|MarketSeed|conditional_collateral|conditional_share|total_supply_micro|complete_set_balanced|ResolutionRef|RedeemBeforeResolution|InvalidResolutionRef|STEP_B|restricted" src tests handover CLAUDE.md Cargo.toml' in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2584:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:496:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:25:| Art. III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2592:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:507:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:66:| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2593:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:508:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:67:| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2599:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:515:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:118:| SG-13.6   redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no                | ✓ pass (exact)      | `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (also covers symmetric Bankrupt → No path + 2 mismatch InvalidResolutionRef checks) |
