handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2608:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:526:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:202:  12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2611:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:529:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:243:Gemini also raised one non-blocking CHALLENGE on Q12 (`ResolutionRef`
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2628:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:561:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:42:**3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a TaskBankruptcy-style resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? (Must be rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`.)**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2630:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:563:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:46:*   Any other combination on a resolved market, such as `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)`, falls through to the wildcard arm which returns `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2645:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:581:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:116:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2646:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:582:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:118:*   **Scrutiny**: The `ResolutionRef` in `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1044` points to a single `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1964` checks if this `tx_id` corresponds to a `TaskBankruptcyTx` or a `FinalizeRewardTx`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2647:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:583:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:120:*   **Verdict**: **CHALLENGE**. This is not a blocker for TB-13, but the architectural roadmap for TB-15 must explicitly address how `ResolutionRef` will evolve to handle more complex resolution scenarios. The current schema correctly implements the narrow TB-13 mandate.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2678:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:1447:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2687:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:1457:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:72:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2688:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:1458:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2823:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:2096:handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md:19:**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2830:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:2108:handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md:60:            ResolutionRef pointing to a system-emitted resolution (no

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3207:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:6614:I’m treating the stale `ResolutionRef` comments as material because the audit target itself asks whether `resolution_tx_id` is security-bearing. The code path is internally conservative for money movement, but the schema and ledger docs still state a stronger L4-lookup contract than the implementation enforces.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3246:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:7399:tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:56:    MarketSeedTx, OutcomeSide, ResolutionRef, ShareAmount, TypedTx,
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3409:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:7782:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1600:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "CompleteSetMint|CompleteSetRedeem|MarketSeed|RedeemBeforeResolution|RedeemMoreThanOwned|InvalidResolutionRef|InsufficientCollateral|assert_total_ctf_conserved|assert_complete_set_balanced" src/state/sequencer.rs' in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3452:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8036:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6537:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3455:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8039:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6540:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3459:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8081:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6594:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)). Replay-time detection in `verify.rs` is not an admission control for a Class 3 money/collateral mutator ([verify.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/runtime/verify.rs:442)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3460:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8082:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6596:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3462:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8085:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6610:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)). Replay-time detection in `verify.rs` is not an admission control for a Class 3 money/collateral mutator ([verify.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/runtime/verify.rs:442)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3463:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8086:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6612:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3548:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:8778:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:233:/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n "CompleteSet|MarketSeed|conditional_collateral|conditional_share|total_supply_micro|complete_set_balanced|ResolutionRef|RedeemBeforeResolution|InvalidResolutionRef|STEP_B|restricted" src tests handover CLAUDE.md Cargo.toml' in /home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3559:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:9017:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03.md.raw:582:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:118:*   **Scrutiny**: The `ResolutionRef` in `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1044` points to a single `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1964` checks if this `tx_id` corresponds to a `TaskBankruptcyTx` or a `FinalizeRewardTx`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3562:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:9397:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:43:**Finding**: PASS. No. The dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1889-1899` contains an explicit `match` on `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`. A `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)` pair correctly falls through to the `InvalidResolutionRef` rejection path. This is confirmed by integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3565:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:9401:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:74:**Finding**: CHALLENGE. The current `ResolutionRef` model at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1120` and its validation logic in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` are tightly coupled to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). If TB-15+ introduces new resolution mechanisms (e.g., `ChallengeCourtRulingTx`, `OracleUpdateTx`), the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm will require modification to recognize these new transaction types. A more abstract and robust long-term design might involve a canonical `ResolutionsIndex` in `QState` that maps `EventId` to a resolved `OutcomeSide`, decoupling the redemption logic from the specific mechanism of resolution. The current implementation is not a bug but represents an architectural choice that incurs future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3570:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:10026:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3573:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:10029:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3587:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11008:handover/tracer_bullets/TB-13_charter_2026-05-03.md:169:  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3594:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11092:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3599:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11120:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:423:  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).

The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3658:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11549:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6594:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)). Replay-time detection in `verify.rs` is not an admission control for a Class 3 money/collateral mutator ([verify.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/runtime/verify.rs:442)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3659:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11550:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6596:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3661:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11553:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6610:Blocking issue remains: TB-13 is still not live agent-authenticated. `submit_agent_tx` admits `CompleteSetMint`, `CompleteSetRedeem`, and `MarketSeed` without signature verification ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2328)); `apply_one` only verifies system signatures and skips agent variants ([sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:2886)); the repo’s own OBS says forged TB-13 signatures mutate state before replay detects them ([OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/alignment/OBS_AGENT_SIG_REPLAY_GAP_2026-05-03.md:41)). Replay-time detection in `verify.rs` is not an admission control for a Class 3 money/collateral mutator ([verify.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/runtime/verify.rs:442)).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3677:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:11965:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1563:   169	  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3766:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:12343:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:95:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3776:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:12746:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:120:*   **Verdict**: **CHALLENGE**. This is not a blocker for TB-13, but the architectural roadmap for TB-15 must explicitly address how `ResolutionRef` will evolve to handle more complex resolution scenarios. The current schema correctly implements the narrow TB-13 mandate.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3780:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:13078:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:27:However, the audit identifies one non-blocking but significant architectural risk concerning the forward-compatibility of the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12), which warrants a **CHALLENGE**. The current model tightly couples redemption to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`), which may prove brittle in TB-15+ multi-resolver scenarios. While not a halting trigger for TB-13, this design choice introduces future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3782:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:13082:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:87:**Rationale**: The implementation of TB-13 is sound and passes all critical safety checks (Q1-Q9) in its remediated form. However, the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12) introduces a point of future architectural friction. While not a VETO-level flaw for TB-13, it is a significant enough design choice to warrant a CHALLENGE. The architect should acknowledge this future refactoring cost. The recommended "fix" is to add an OBS (observation) to the project backlog to track the redesign of the resolution mechanism for TB-15, rather than blocking the TB-13 ship. With this acknowledgment, the project can proceed.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3806:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:25:| Art. III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3815:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:66:| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3816:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:67:| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:3822:handover/audits/RECURSIVE_AUDIT_TB_13_2026-05-03.md:118:| SG-13.6   redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no                | ✓ pass (exact)      | `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (also covers symmetric Bankrupt → No path + 2 mismatch InvalidResolutionRef checks) |

While not a halting trigger for TB-13, this design choice introduces future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4005:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:43:**Finding**: PASS. No. The dispatch arm at `src/state/sequencer.rs:1889-1899` contains an explicit `match` on `(market_state, redeem.outcome)`. A `(TaskMarketState::Bankrupt, OutcomeSide::Yes)` pair correctly falls through to the `InvalidResolutionRef` rejection path. This is confirmed by integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4013:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:73:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4014:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:74:**Finding**: CHALLENGE. The current `ResolutionRef` model at `src/state/typed_tx.rs:1120` and its validation logic in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1882` are tightly coupled to specific transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). If TB-15+ introduces new resolution mechanisms (e.g., `ChallengeCourtRulingTx`, `OracleUpdateTx`), the `CompleteSetRedeemTx` dispatch arm will require modification to recognize these new transaction types. A more abstract and robust long-term design might involve a canonical `ResolutionsIndex` in `QState` that maps `EventId` to a resolved `OutcomeSide`, decoupling the redemption logic from the specific mechanism of resolution. The current implementation is not a bug but represents an architectural choice that incurs future refactoring debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4017:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R3.md:87:**Rationale**: The implementation of TB-13 is sound and passes all critical safety checks (Q1-Q9) in its remediated form. However, the `ResolutionRef` model (Q12) introduces a point of future architectural friction. While not a VETO-level flaw for TB-13, it is a significant enough design choice to warrant a CHALLENGE. The architect should acknowledge this future refactoring cost. The recommended "fix" is to add an OBS (observation) to the project backlog to track the redesign of the resolution mechanism for TB-15, rather than blocking the TB-13 ship. With this acknowledgment, the project can proceed.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4028:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4037:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:72:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4038:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4070:handover/alignment/OBS_RESOLUTIONS_INDEX_TB15_2026-05-03.md:12:TB-13's `ResolutionRef` model couples redemption logic to specific

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4078:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:1:# OBS R-022 — TB-13 ResolutionRef removed (TRACE_MATRIX backlink retired)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4079:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:5:**Removed symbol**: `pub struct ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` in `src/state/typed_tx.rs`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4081:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:10:ResolutionRef carried two dead fields:
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4082:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:13:- `claimed_outcome: OutcomeSide` — a redundant copy of `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`. Used only by an inner-consistency gate `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome → InvalidResolutionRef`. That gate was dead defense-in-depth: the agent signature covers both fields, so any tampering that desynchronized them would break the signature first.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4083:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:19:The TB-13 typed-tx surface is still flowchart-anchored — `CompleteSetRedeemTx` itself retains its `TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 1 (architect §4.3 + FR-13.4..5 + SG-13.5..6)` backlink at `src/state/typed_tx.rs`. ResolutionRef was an internal helper type without an independent flowchart role. Removing the type cleanly removes the backlink — there is no orphan code; the role it carried (claimed-outcome assertion) is now expressed directly via `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:4084:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:25:- SG-13.6 (`sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`): PASS unchanged — including both mismatch checks (Finalized + outcome=No → `InvalidResolutionRef`; Bankrupt + outcome=Yes → `InvalidResolutionRef`). The match arm using `(market_state, redeem.outcome)` produces identical rejection behavior to the previous `(market_state, claimed_outcome)` form because all fixtures set `claimed_outcome = outcome` (the inner-consistency check could never independently fire on an honestly-signed tx).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6631:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:13:- `claimed_outcome: OutcomeSide` — a redundant copy of `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`. Used only by an inner-consistency gate `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome → InvalidResolutionRef`. That gate was dead defense-in-depth: the agent signature covers both fields, so any tampering that desynchronized them would break the signature first.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6632:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:19:The TB-13 typed-tx surface is still flowchart-anchored — `CompleteSetRedeemTx` itself retains its `TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 1 (architect §4.3 + FR-13.4..5 + SG-13.5..6)` backlink at `src/state/typed_tx.rs`. ResolutionRef was an internal helper type without an independent flowchart role. Removing the type cleanly removes the backlink — there is no orphan code; the role it carried (claimed-outcome assertion) is now expressed directly via `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6633:handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:25:- SG-13.6 (`sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`): PASS unchanged — including both mismatch checks (Finalized + outcome=No → `InvalidResolutionRef`; Bankrupt + outcome=Yes → `InvalidResolutionRef`). The match arm using `(market_state, redeem.outcome)` produces identical rejection behavior to the previous `(market_state, claimed_outcome)` form because all fixtures set `claimed_outcome = outcome` (the inner-consistency check could never independently fire on an honestly-signed tx).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6634:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6636:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6637:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:273:**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6638:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:314:            ResolutionRef pointing to a system-emitted resolution (no

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6640:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:423:  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6641:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:425:  - `CompleteSetRedeemTx { tx_id, parent_state_root, event_id, owner, outcome: OutcomeSide, share_amount: ShareAmount, resolution_ref: ResolutionRef, signature }` agent-callable but pre-resolution rejected at sequencer.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6643:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:817:    pub signature_or_system_resolution_ref: ResolutionRef,
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6644:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1172:| Art. III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6645:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1213:| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6646:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1214:| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6647:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1265:| SG-13.6   redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no                | ✓ pass (exact)      | `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (also covers symmetric Bankrupt → No path + 2 mismatch InvalidResolutionRef checks) |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6682:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:3636:   439	    assert!(err.contains("InvalidResolutionRef"));
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6683:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4062:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6685:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4109:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6686:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4286:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6688:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4333:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?

Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6691:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4517:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6692:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4962:tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:56:    MarketSeedTx, OutcomeSide, ResolutionRef, ShareAmount, TypedTx,
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6693:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6537:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6694:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6540:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6695:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6596:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6696:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6612:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6697:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:55:edbc555  TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ5 remediation: drop ResolutionRef wrapper
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6698:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:64:**RQ5 — drop ResolutionRef wrapper**: ResolutionRef struct + resolution_tx_id + claimed_outcome fields removed from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload. Inner-consistency check removed from sequencer. State-mismatch path preserved via existing match arm using `redeem.outcome` directly. CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; signing payload 8→7. Wire-format break — no production rows yet. Trust Root rehashed for typed_tx / sequencer / transition_ledger. R-022 skip token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6699:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:76:3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` against a Bankrupt event bypass the outcome check? (Must be rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate — single state-vs-outcome match arm now; no inner-consistency check.)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6700:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:88:12. With ResolutionRef now removed (round-5 RQ5), is the redeem-mechanism-coupling concern from your round-3 Q12 CHALLENGE addressed, partially addressed, or unchanged? Specifically: does removing the resolution_ref wrapper make TB-15+ multi-resolver evolution easier (fewer wire fields to reshape) or harder (lost a future-extension hook)?

Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6707:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1413:    19	**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).

III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6727:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:2474:    66	| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6728:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:2475:    67	| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6785:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:6927:   439	    assert!(err.contains("InvalidResolutionRef"));
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6786:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:48:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6788:handover/audits/run_codex_tb_13_ship_audit.sh:95:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6789:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:29:edbc555  TB-13 Atom 6 round-5 — Codex RQ5 remediation: drop ResolutionRef wrapper
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6790:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:38:**RQ5 fix — drop ResolutionRef wrapper** (commit `edbc555`):
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6791:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:39:- `src/state/typed_tx.rs`: ResolutionRef struct deleted; CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; CompleteSetRedeemSigningPayload 8→7 fields. `to_signing_payload` projection drops `resolution_ref`. `InvalidResolutionRef` doc-comment + Display now read "outcome does not match state" (was "resolution_ref.claimed_outcome").
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6794:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:42:- `tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs`: ResolutionRef import + build_redeem fixture pruned. SG-13.5 / SG-13.6 unchanged in behavior.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6795:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:44:- R-022 skip token issued for the removed ResolutionRef pub-symbol backlink. Justification at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6797:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:81:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6798:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:101:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6799:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:105:**Round-5 R-022 skip-token**: ResolutionRef pub struct removed; the R-022 hook caught the missing TRACE_MATRIX backlink. The skip-token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md` justifies the removal. Confirm the OBS doc is sufficient: does it explain why no replacement TRACE_MATRIX entry is needed?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6817:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1179:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:64:**RQ5 — drop ResolutionRef wrapper**: ResolutionRef struct + resolution_tx_id + claimed_outcome fields removed from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload. Inner-consistency check removed from sequencer. State-mismatch path preserved via existing match arm using `redeem.outcome` directly. CompleteSetRedeemTx 9→8 fields; signing payload 8→7. Wire-format break — no production rows yet. Trust Root rehashed for typed_tx / sequencer / transition_ledger. R-022 skip token at `handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md`.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6818:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1182:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:76:3. Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` against a Bankrupt event bypass the outcome check? (Must be rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate — single state-vs-outcome match arm now; no inner-consistency check.)
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6819:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1186:handover/audits/run_gemini_tb_13_ship_audit_r4.py:88:12. With ResolutionRef now removed (round-5 RQ5), is the redeem-mechanism-coupling concern from your round-3 Q12 CHALLENGE addressed, partially addressed, or unchanged? Specifically: does removing the resolution_ref wrapper make TB-15+ multi-resolver evolution easier (fewer wire fields to reshape) or harder (lost a future-extension hook)?

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6821:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1195:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:46:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6823:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1205:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:93:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6824:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1208:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:273:**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6825:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1216:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:314:            ResolutionRef pointing to a system-emitted resolution (no
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6826:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1217:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:317:            ResolutionRef.outcome state != Resolved or if challenge
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6827:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1229:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:423:  - `ResolutionRef` newtype referencing the system-emitted resolution (TB-13 reuses `TaskBankruptcyTx.tx_id` for NO outcome + reuses `FinalizeRewardTx.tx_id` for YES outcome — both already system-emitted; this is the "system resolution reference" architect §4.3 mentions).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6828:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1231:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:425:  - `CompleteSetRedeemTx { tx_id, parent_state_root, event_id, owner, outcome: OutcomeSide, share_amount: ShareAmount, resolution_ref: ResolutionRef, signature }` agent-callable but pre-resolution rejected at sequencer.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6830:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1252:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:817:    pub signature_or_system_resolution_ref: ResolutionRef,
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6831:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1269:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1172:| Art. III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6832:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1278:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1213:| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6833:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1279:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1214:| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6834:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1285:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1265:| SG-13.6   redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no                | ✓ pass (exact)      | `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (also covers symmetric Bankrupt → No path + 2 mismatch InvalidResolutionRef checks) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6835:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1288:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:1283:| G9 TransitionError variants: 5 NEW additive                       | ✓ pass     | InsufficientBalanceForMint / RedeemBeforeResolution / RedeemMoreThanOwned / InsufficientCollateral / InvalidResolutionRef. |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6855:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1471:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:3636:   439	    assert!(err.contains("InvalidResolutionRef"));
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6856:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1487:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4062:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6858:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1497:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4109:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6859:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1500:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4286:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Sequencer must reject with `InvalidResolutionRef` when state-vs-outcome mismatch. Test: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6861:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1510:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4333:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6862:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1513:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4470:**Q3 (outcome match)**: Can Redeem with `outcome=Yes` and a Bankrupt-task resolution_ref bypass the outcome check? Also: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` is a separate gate that fires before state lookup — defense-in-depth.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6864:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1523:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4517:**RQ5 — ResolutionRef integrity**: TB-13 uses `ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` but the sequencer ONLY validates the task_market state (Finalized/Bankrupt) + the claimed_outcome match. The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? Sequencer ignores resolution_tx_id at validation — only the task_market state matters. Is this the right design or a security gap?
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6865:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1535:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:4962:tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:56:    MarketSeedTx, OutcomeSide, ResolutionRef, ShareAmount, TypedTx,
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6866:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1622:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6537:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6867:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1625:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6540:handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6868:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1630:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6596:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6869:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1633:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2.md:6612:Non-blocking challenges: `assert_complete_set_balanced` is test-only, not called by TB-13 dispatch arms ([monetary_invariant.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/economy/monetary_invariant.rs:457); no `src/` callers). `ResolutionRef.resolution_tx_id` is documented as L4-validated, but the sequencer ignores it and uses only `task_markets_t` state ([typed_tx.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/typed_tx.rs:1128), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:1683)). The forward fence catches the prompt’s exact top-level import example inside fixed scope, but fixed `FENCE_SCOPE` still misses new unlisted TB-13 files ([tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:51)).

The `resolution_tx_id` is opaque traceability metadata, NOT validated against L4. Is this safe? Argument: task_markets_t.state IS the on-chain state-of-truth for resolution; resolution_tx_id is for L4-audit replay. Charge: a malicious agent could submit a CompleteSetRedeemTx with resolution_tx_id pointing to a non-existent or wrong tx. Is this a concern? handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6875:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1670:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:1413:    19	**FC-trace**: `Art. 0.2` (Tape Canonical — `CompleteSetMintTx` / `CompleteSetRedeemTx` / `MarketSeedTx` canonical-encoded; replay-deterministic) + `Art. I.1` (5-step compile loop — conditional collateral and share accounting are part of the proposal-pricing substrate, but DO NOT affect predicate outcome; CR-13.6 explicit) + `Art. III.4` (no fake accepted preserved — Redeem requires system-resolved outcome reference; agent-callable Redeem rejected pre-resolution) + `Art. V.1.3` (Anti-Oreo — `MarketSeedTx` requires explicit provider funds, NO automatic seed; `CompleteSetMintTx` is agent-driven balance debit, no money created) + `WP-§14.1` (typed_tx variant additive bumps; 3 NEW typed-tx variants; CompleteSetRedeem additionally accepts a `ResolutionRef` from system-emitted resolution; not a unilateral agent variant since pre-resolution rejection is sequencer-side gate) + `WP-§19` (RSP-M collateral substrate — conditional collateral is the YES_E + NO_E claim foundation that TB-14 PriceIndex will derive prices from).

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6887:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1753:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:2433:    25	| Art. III.4 no fake accepted              | CompleteSetRedeemTx requires sequencer-side validation of `task_markets_t[event_id.0].state ∈ {Finalized, Bankrupt}` AND `resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` matches the state. Pre-resolution rejected with `RedeemBeforeResolution`; wrong-outcome-for-state rejected with `InvalidResolutionRef`. Owner cannot self-resolve. | `sg_13_5_redeem_unavailable_before_outcome_resolution` (Open + Expired states both reject); `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (mismatch outcome rejected with InvalidResolutionRef on Finalized event; symmetric Bankrupt check) |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6888:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1762:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:2474:    66	| 4.2 Resolution-outcome match enforced      | Finalized state → only outcome=Yes accepted; Bankrupt state → only outcome=No accepted; mismatch → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Verified: `sg_13_6` includes 2 mismatch checks (Finalized+No rejected; Bankrupt+Yes rejected). |
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6889:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:1763:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R1.md:2475:    67	| 4.3 ResolutionRef inner-consistency        | Pre-state-lookup gate: `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome` → `InvalidResolutionRef`. Catches malformed wire payloads where the redeem's outcome field disagrees with the resolution_ref's claimed_outcome (defense-in-depth before task_markets_t lookup). |

handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6936:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2384:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:81:**Q2 (resolution gate)**: Round-5 RQ5 simplified the gate (removed inner-consistency check, kept state-vs-outcome match). Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:6937:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:2390:handover/audits/CODEX_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R4.md.raw:101:**Round-5 RQ5 wire-format break**: ResolutionRef is gone from CompleteSetRedeemTx + signing payload + canonical encoding. Old chain entries written with the old shape would not decode under the new struct. Per `feedback_no_retroactive_evidence_rewrite`, no production rows exist for TB-13 typed-tx, so no migration is needed. Confirm there are no CAS payloads or persisted artifacts that encode the old shape.

Walk `src/state/sequencer.rs` redeem dispatch (around line 1701..1760) and confirm: (a) Open / Expired → RedeemBeforeResolution; (b) Finalized + Yes → ok; Bankrupt + No → ok; (c) (Finalized, _) | (Bankrupt, _) → InvalidResolutionRef. The redeem.outcome is the only claim source.
