handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:175:### 1.2 CO1.13.2 — R-022 commit-time hook (v1.1: ARCHITECTURALLY CORRECTED per Codex r1 P0-1+P0-2 + Gemini r1 P0-G1)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:177:**v1 architectural mismatch** (round-1 P0): the v1 plan piped through `engine.py --rule R-022`, but `engine.py` only supports `grep` / `grep_inverse` / `compound` check types and is per-file (not diff-aware) per pre-edit `judge.sh` invocation. R-022 needs cross-file diff awareness which engine.py doesn't provide. v1 also placed the hook at `.git/hooks/pre-commit` which is untracked local state — doesn't protect fresh clones, CI, or merges (Codex P0-2).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:179:**v1.1 corrected architecture**: pre-commit shim calls `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` DIRECTLY (engine.py BYPASSED for R-022). YAML rule becomes a declarative tombstone for the 30-rule cap + documentation. Tracked shim under `scripts/hooks/pre-commit.r022` + `scripts/install_hooks.sh` symlink installer + CI command for fresh-clone / merge protection.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:184:rules/active/R-022_trace_matrix_pub_symbol_block.yaml    # declarative tombstone (engine.py bypassed)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:193:id: "R-022"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:199:fc_trace: "CLAUDE.md Alignment Standard — every NEW src/ pub symbol must have TRACE_MATRIX backlink AT COMMIT TIME (not retroactive)"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:205:  invocation_note: "engine.py BYPASSED for this rule because R-022 requires cross-file diff awareness which engine.py per-file architecture does not provide"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:208:message: "BLOCK (R-022 / Alignment Standard): NEW pub symbol(s) added under src/ without TRACE_MATRIX backlink (or REMOVAL of an existing backlink). See script output for specific locations. Either (a) add /// TRACE_MATRIX <FC-id>: <role> doc-comment in the immediately preceding doc/attribute block of each new pub symbol, (b) file in handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3.md § G with orphan justification (cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md required), or (c) include `[R-022-skip: <reason; cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md REQUIRED>]` token in the commit message."
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:215:- Mode `--mode commit`: reads `git diff --cached`, identifies NEW pub items added in this commit vs base; for each, walks **immediately preceding contiguous doc/attribute/comment/blank-line block** (NOT raw 5-line window per Codex P0-4 empirical finding 86%/107 vs 92%/107 = semantic block walk wins) for `/// TRACE_MATRIX `; ALSO detects removals of existing TRACE_MATRIX lines (per Codex § C: R-022 should block removal too); falls back to TRACE_MATRIX_v3 § G orphan section lookup; falls back to commit-message `[R-022-skip: ...]` token (REQUIRES `cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*` reference per Codex P0-5 — silent skip rejected); structured logs every block/skip event to `rules/enforcement.log` with symbol/file/line/reason/staged-tree-hash/timestamp; exits 2 on any unjustified violation.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:225:### 1.3 R-022 Scope Table (NEW v1.1; per Codex r1 P0-6 boundary cases)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:229:| Pub style | R-022 enforcement | Rationale |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:237:| Macro-generated pub items | **EXEMPT** if trace at macro-invocation site OR via `[R-022-skip: macro-generated]` commit-msg token | No stable source line for expansion; trace at the invocation. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:239:| **Removal of existing TRACE_MATRIX line** | **BLOCK** unless commit-msg has `[R-022-skip: ...]` with justification | Codex § C: "R-022 should block ... removal of existing TRACE_MATRIX backlinks". |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:251:### 2.1 R-022 enforcement boundary (v1.1: semantic block walk per Codex r1 P0-4 empirical finding)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:253:R-022 fires on `pre_commit` when `git diff --cached` shows NEW pub-style declarations under `src/` (per § 1.3 R-022 Scope Table: `pub fn`/`struct`/`enum`/`trait`/`const`/`mod`/`type`/`static`, `pub(crate)` variants; exempts `pub use`, `#[cfg(test)]`, macro-generated). For each new pub line, `check_trace_matrix.py --mode commit`:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:258:4. **If still not found**: search the staged commit message for `[R-022-skip: <reason; cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md REQUIRED>]` token (per § 2.2 v1.1); if found AND reason has a valid justification reference, PASS-with-LOG.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:261:**Removal detection** (NEW v1.1; per Codex r1 § C "R-022 should block ... removal of existing TRACE_MATRIX backlinks"): script also detects `git diff --cached` deletions of `/// TRACE_MATRIX ` lines under `src/`. Each removal is BLOCKED unless commit-message has `[R-022-skip: ...]` token with valid justification. This prevents silent backlink decay.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:263:### 2.2 R-022 escape hatch (v1.1: COMMIT-MESSAGE TOKEN + STRUCTURED LOG per Gemini r1 P0-G2 + Codex r1 P0-5)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:265:**v1 silent-bypass risk**: `// R-022-skip: <reason>` (Rust code comment) was permissive (no required justification reference; quarterly audit only). Codex r1 P0-5: "A hard gate needs structured logging at minimum: symbol, file, line, reason, staged tree hash, timestamp. I would also require cases/Cxxx, PREREG-§, or OBS_R022_*."
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:267:**v1.1 escape hatch**: `[R-022-skip: <reason; cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md REQUIRED>]` token in the COMMIT MESSAGE (NOT a Rust code comment, per Gemini P0-G2: "prevents polluting Rust source code with temporary bypass comments that will inevitably be forgotten"). Structured logging mandatory:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:270:2026-04-29T12:34:56Z R-022-SKIP commit=<staged_tree_hash> file=src/foo.rs line=42 symbol=pub_fn_foo reason="cases/C-xxx <description>"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:274:- No `[R-022-skip: ...]` token in commit message
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:278:Quarterly audit reviews accumulated R-022-SKIP log entries; flags suspicious patterns.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:282:R-022 is invoked at `git commit` pre-commit time, before the commit object is created. If BLOCK fires, the commit is aborted (exit 2) and the working tree state is preserved (developer can amend). No atomicity concerns since git's commit-or-abort is atomic.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:288:| **I-FORWARD** | R-022 triggers on NEW pub symbols only; legacy untouched pub symbols exempt; legacy MODIFIED pub symbols WARN (not block) until CO1.13-extra | `tests/r_022_no_legacy_block.sh` (NEW v1.1: shell test, not Rust) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:289:| **I-REMOVAL** | R-022 also blocks REMOVAL of existing TRACE_MATRIX backlinks unless escape-hatch justified | `tests/r_022_blocks_backlink_removal.sh` (NEW v1.1) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:290:| **I-SCOPE** | R-022 scope table (§ 1.3) honored: pub use exempt, cfg(test) exempt, macro-generated exempt-with-token | `tests/r_022_scope_table.sh` (NEW v1.1) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:292:| **I-LIST** | Active rules count ≤ 30 (docs/rules.md cap); CO1.13.2 lands R-022 as 16th rule (within cap) | `ls rules/active/*.yaml \| wc -l` |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:293:| **I-ENFORCE** | R-022 enforcement actually blocks; integration test demonstrates a real temp-repo commit with missing backlink fails pre-commit | `tests/r_022_blocks_missing_backlink.sh` (NEW v1.1: shell integration test, not Rust per Codex P0-7) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:294:| **I-LOG** | Every R-022 event (block, skip, pass) appends structured log entry to rules/enforcement.log | `tests/r_022_structured_log.sh` (NEW v1.1) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:299:R-022 enforces **FORM**: every new pub symbol has a `/// TRACE_MATRIX <id>: <role>` doc-comment in the preceding block. R-022 is a SYNTACTIC check; it cannot verify whether the backlink correctly assigns the symbol to its true FC layer (e.g., a top-white symbol incorrectly tagged FC3 would pass R-022).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:301:TRACE_MATRIX_v3 § F (reverse-map, auto-generated by CO1.13.3) provides **SUBSTANCE** review: every shipped symbol → its claimed TRACE_MATRIX row → human/AI reviewer can verify alignment correctness. § F is the "eventual consistency" loop — R-022 enforces form at commit time; § F enables substance review post-merge.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:303:This two-layer model is acknowledged as a known limitation: R-022 alone cannot prevent constitutionally misaligned backlinks. Mitigations:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:325:### 3.4 `tests/integration/co1_13/r_022_skip_token_with_justification.sh`
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:326:Same setup; commit message contains `[R-022-skip: cases/C-test refactor cleanup]`; asserts commit succeeds + structured log entry shows SKIP with justification.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:328:### 3.5 `tests/integration/co1_13/r_022_skip_token_without_justification_blocks.sh`
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:329:Same setup; commit message contains `[R-022-skip: <empty>]` or skip token without `cases/* | PREREG-§ | OBS_R022_*` reference; asserts BLOCK + log entry.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:332:Setup: existing file with `/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-Nx: foo` + `pub fn foo`; commit removes the TRACE_MATRIX line; asserts BLOCK unless skip-token justified.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:335:Multi-test bundle covering scope-table policy: cfg(test) pub items don't block, pub use re-exports don't block, macro-generated marked via skip-token passes, pub(crate) DOES block, pub type/pub static DO block.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:338:Pre-stages files with legacy untraced pub symbols at HEAD; subsequent commit modifies an unrelated file; asserts no spurious R-022 block from legacy.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:367:3. **R-015 stats-not-propagating bug**: filed as separate OBS; orthogonal to R-022.

Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard". No STEP_B-restricted files touched (kernel.rs / bus.rs / wallet.rs UNTOUCHED).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:416:1. **Scope correction from Elon-mode framing**: the user's "factory tooling" framing in conversation suggested broader scope (scaffold scripts + Trust Root rehash); the canonical CO1.13 sprint-graph scope is narrower (3 sub-atoms: TRACE_MATRIX impl + R-022 + reverse-map). v1 honors the narrower scope; broader devtools land separately as non-spec follow-up commits per § 0.4.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:417:2. **R-015 retention**: R-015 (existing pre-edit warn) is NOT retired by this atom. R-022 adds defense-in-depth at commit time; R-015 catches issues earlier in the editing flow. Both coexist within the 30-rule cap.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:418:3. **Forward-only enforcement**: R-022 blocks NEW untraced pub symbols only. The ~250 legacy untraced symbols (pre-CO1.13.2) are handled in a separate CO1.13-extra atom. v1 does NOT close the legacy gap.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:419:4. **Escape hatch permissive in v1**: `// R-022-skip: <reason>` allows bypass without `cases/Cxxx` reference. Quarterly audit catches abuse. Tightening to require justification is a CO1.13-extra concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:420:5. **Test coverage**: 5 tests cover R-022 enforcement boundary + idempotency + doc coverage. Does NOT test the script's robustness against edge cases (e.g., pub symbol inside a comment block — should be ignored). v1 ships best-effort regex; refinement is out of v1 scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:422:7. **FC-trace requirements for CO1.13 implementation**: `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` + `scripts/update_trace_matrix_reverse_map.sh` are tooling, not src/ pub symbols; they don't need TRACE_MATRIX backlinks. The R-022 YAML rule itself is documented via `fc_trace:` field (already a YAML schema convention).

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:652:| **CO1.13.2** | R-022 commit-time hook: `rules/active/R-022_trace_matrix_pub_symbol_block.yaml` (block-enforcement variant of R-015) + `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` (multi-line context grep tool the YAML check delegates to) + git pre-commit hook installation | ~120 LoC (script) + ~30 LoC (yaml) + ~15 LoC (pre-commit shim) = ~165 LoC | 1 day |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:668:1. **Does NOT replace R-015**: R-015 (pre-edit warn) remains active; R-022 (commit-time block) is defense-in-depth. R-015 stats-not-propagating is a separate bug filed as `OBS_R_015_STATS_TIMES_TRIGGERED_DRIFT` follow-up.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:669:2. **Does NOT enforce backlinks on legacy pub symbols** (the existing 75% gap): R-022 is a forward-only enforcer (blocks NEW untraced pub symbols). Legacy gap closure is a separate cleanup arc (CO1.13-extra; targets ~250 missing backlinks; ~10-15 hr work).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:684:### 1.2 CO1.13.2 — R-022 commit-time hook
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:689:rules/active/R-022_trace_matrix_pub_symbol_block.yaml
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:696:id: "R-022"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:701:fc_trace: "CLAUDE.md Alignment Standard — every NEW src/ pub symbol must have TRACE_MATRIX backlink AT COMMIT TIME (not retroactive)"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:710:message: "BLOCK (R-022 / Alignment Standard): NEW pub symbol(s) added under src/ without TRACE_MATRIX backlink. See script output for specific locations. Either (a) add /// TRACE_MATRIX <FC-id>: <role> doc-comment within 5 lines preceding each new pub symbol, (b) file in handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3.md § 3 with orphan justification, or (c) explicit `// R-022-skip: <reason>` on the same commit (audited at quarterly review)."
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:718:Pre-commit shim (~15 LoC) — installs in `.git/hooks/pre-commit`; reads staged diff; pipes to engine.py with `--rule R-022`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:730:### 2.1 R-022 enforcement boundary
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:732:R-022 fires on `pre_commit` when `git diff --cached` shows NEW `pub fn|struct|enum|trait|const|mod` lines under `src/`. For each new pub line, `check_trace_matrix.py`:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:739:### 2.2 R-022 escape hatch
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:741:Per spec § 1.2 message: `// R-022-skip: <reason>` comment on the same commit allows bypass. Audited at quarterly review (manual). Use cases: experimental atoms; refactor cleanup that lands backlinks in a follow-up commit (within 1 week deadline).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:747:| **I-FORWARD** | R-022 triggers on NEW pub symbols only; legacy pub symbols (already shipped pre-CO1.13.2) are exempt | `tests/r_022_no_legacy_block.rs` |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:749:| **I-LIST** | Active rules count ≤ 30 (docs/rules.md cap); CO1.13.2 lands R-022 as 16th rule (within cap) | `ls rules/active/*.yaml \| wc -l` |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:750:| **I-ENFORCE** | R-022 enforcement actually blocks; cargo test demonstrates a test commit with missing backlink fails pre-commit | `tests/r_022_blocks_missing_backlink.rs` (uses `git -c hooks.pre-commit=enabled commit --dry-run`) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:762:Verifies that already-shipped pub symbols (without backlink) do NOT trigger R-022 on subsequent commits that don't modify them.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:779:3. **R-015 stats-not-propagating bug**: filed as separate OBS; orthogonal to R-022.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:789:| Q1 | Should R-022 fire on edits to EXISTING pub symbols (e.g., signature change) or only NEW ones? | NEW only (`I-FORWARD`). Edits are out of scope to keep enforcement tractable; existing R-015 (warn) covers edits. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:790:| Q2 | Is the 5-line preceding context window correct for backlink detection, or should it be flexible (e.g., entire doc-comment block above)? | 5 lines is a heuristic; works for ~95% of conventions in current src/. Edge case: multi-paragraph doc-comments where TRACE_MATRIX line is >5 lines above. Mitigation: convention enforced via R-022 message — keep TRACE_MATRIX line within 5 lines OR escape hatch. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:791:| Q3 | Should `// R-022-skip: <reason>` escape hatch be more rigorous (e.g., require `cases/Cxxx` reference) to prevent abuse? | v1 ships permissive; quarterly audit catches abuse. Tightening to require `cases/Cxxx` is a CO1.13-extra concern. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:792:| Q4 | Should reverse-map § F be the source-of-truth for backlinks, or are doc-comments authoritative? | Doc-comments authoritative (single source). § F is auto-derived view. This matches R-022 enforcement (blocks at doc-comment level). |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:793:| Q5 | Should this atom also retire R-015 (downgrade to deprecated)? | NO. R-015 (pre-edit warn) catches issues earlier; R-022 (commit-time block) is the hard gate. Defense in depth. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:805:**Pre-implementation gate**: spec must reach PASS/PASS (or PASS-with-OBS) before any code in `rules/active/R-022*` / `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` / `scripts/update_trace_matrix_reverse_map.sh` / `.git/hooks/pre-commit` is written. Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard". No STEP_B-restricted files touched (kernel.rs / bus.rs / wallet.rs UNTOUCHED).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:823:1. **Scope correction from Elon-mode framing**: the user's "factory tooling" framing in conversation suggested broader scope (scaffold scripts + Trust Root rehash); the canonical CO1.13 sprint-graph scope is narrower (3 sub-atoms: TRACE_MATRIX impl + R-022 + reverse-map). v1 honors the narrower scope; broader devtools land separately as non-spec follow-up commits per § 0.4.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:824:2. **R-015 retention**: R-015 (existing pre-edit warn) is NOT retired by this atom. R-022 adds defense-in-depth at commit time; R-015 catches issues earlier in the editing flow. Both coexist within the 30-rule cap.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:825:3. **Forward-only enforcement**: R-022 blocks NEW untraced pub symbols only. The ~250 legacy untraced symbols (pre-CO1.13.2) are handled in a separate CO1.13-extra atom. v1 does NOT close the legacy gap.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:826:4. **Escape hatch permissive in v1**: `// R-022-skip: <reason>` allows bypass without `cases/Cxxx` reference. Quarterly audit catches abuse. Tightening to require justification is a CO1.13-extra concern.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:827:5. **Test coverage**: 5 tests cover R-022 enforcement boundary + idempotency + doc coverage. Does NOT test the script's robustness against edge cases (e.g., pub symbol inside a comment block — should be ignored). v1 ships best-effort regex; refinement is out of v1 scope.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:829:7. **FC-trace requirements for CO1.13 implementation**: `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` + `scripts/update_trace_matrix_reverse_map.sh` are tooling, not src/ pub symbols; they don't need TRACE_MATRIX backlinks. The R-022 YAML rule itself is documented via `fc_trace:` field (already a YAML schema convention).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:841:| S2 | Rule engine + 15 active rules | `ls rules/active/*.yaml \| wc -l` | 15 (R-022 not yet present) |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:863:| S3 | R-022 absent | "No such file or directory" | ✅ PASS (greenfield confirmed) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:866:| S6 | 30-rule cap | 15/30 (R-022 lands as 16th = within cap) | ✅ PASS |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2731:**CHALLENGE / HIGH conviction.** Not VETO because the shape is salvageable, but v1 cannot proceed to implementation as written. The R-022 path is currently non-functional, the hook surface is not reproducible, the orphan fallback points to a non-existent section, and the 5-line heuristic is empirically false for this repo.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2735:1. **R-022 cannot run through the current engine.** Spec §1.2 lines 85-99 introduces `check.type: external_script`, but `rules/engine.py` only supports `grep`, `grep_inverse`, and `compound` at lines 50-64. It also has no `--rule R-022` CLI despite spec line 109 saying the shim pipes to engine.py with `--rule R-022`. `rules/SCHEMA.yaml` lines 9-11 also excludes `external_script`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2737:2. **`.git/hooks/pre-commit` is not a shippable artifact.** Spec lines 79-83, 109, and 196 put the hook in `.git/hooks/pre-commit`; that is untracked local state. It will not protect fresh clones, CI, or merges. If R-022 is meant to prevent untraced merges, add a tracked hook template plus installer and a CI/merge check.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2743:5. **Escape hatch is a silent bypass.** Spec lines 101, 132, 182, and 217 allow `// R-022-skip: <reason>` with quarterly manual audit only. A hard gate needs structured logging at minimum: symbol, file, line, reason, staged tree hash, timestamp. I would also require `cases/Cxxx`, `PREREG-§`, or `OBS_R022_*`.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2751:- R-022 should block **new production pub definitions** and **removal of existing TRACE_MATRIX backlinks**. It should not block untouched legacy symbols. Modified legacy pub signatures without backlinks should warn/list debt, not block, until the legacy cleanup atom lands.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2753:- `pub use` should be explicitly out of R-022 v1 unless you want re-export surfaces traced separately. If excluded, say canonical definitions only.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2754:- Macro-generated pub API needs a rule: trace the macro invocation or require a logged skip/orphan. Expanded symbols have no stable source line.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2755:- `external_script` is architecturally cleaner than hard-coding `R-022` in engine.py, but only if SCHEMA, docs, parser, CLI, exit-code handling, and tests are updated. R-022-specific dispatch is brittle.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2762:- Add an explicit “R-022 Scope Table”: include/exclude `pub(crate)`, `pub use`, `#[cfg(test)]`, macro invocations, `pub type`, `pub static`, and backlink removals.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2764:- Rewrite §3 tests as integration tests: block missing backlink, pass backlink, pass orphan, pass/log skip, exempt cfg(test), ignore/handle pub use, block backlink removal, legacy modified warning, reverse-map idempotency.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2771:CO1.13 is infrastructure, not direct progress on the frozen 5-step compile loop. It is justified only if the gate is real. If round 2 still has non-enforcing R-022, do not ship-with-OBS; that would convert a hard alignment gate into theater. Ship-with-OBS is acceptable only for bounded edge cases with detection commands and expiry.

The R-022 path is currently non-functional, the hook surface is not reproducible, the orphan fallback points to a non-existent section, and the 5-line heuristic is empirically false for this repo.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2817:CO1.13 is infrastructure, not direct progress on the frozen 5-step compile loop. It is justified only if the gate is real. If round 2 still has non-enforcing R-022, do not ship-with-OBS; that would convert a hard alignment gate into theater. Ship-with-OBS is acceptable only for bounded edge cases with detection commands and expiry.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2840:The strategic intent of this atom is excellent, and the "Eventual Consistency" approach to alignment (syntactic check at commit + semantic review via auto-generated § F) is a highly scalable architectural pattern. However, the mechanical integration of R-022 into the existing `engine.py` rule evaluator is structurally broken and will cause immediate pipeline failures if implemented as specified. 
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2846:**1. Architectural mismatch between `engine.py` and R-022 diff-evaluation (Spec § 1.2)**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2847:*   **The Flaw**: Spec § 1.2 states the pre-commit shim "pipes to engine.py with `--rule R-022`" and uses a "new `check.type: external_script` extension". 
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2851:    *   Update Spec § 1.2 so the `.git/hooks/pre-commit` shim calls `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` *directly*, bypassing `engine.py` entirely. The `R-022*.yaml` file should serve purely as a declarative manifest/tombstone for the 30-rule cap and documentation.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2854:*   **The Flaw**: Spec § 2.2 defines the escape hatch as `// R-022-skip: <reason> on the same commit`. It is highly ambiguous whether this is a Rust code comment (and if so, where it must be placed relative to the `pub` symbol) or a commit message token.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2855:*   **Required Fix**: Since `check_trace_matrix.py` is already operating at the commit level, the escape hatch should be a commit message token (e.g., `[R-022-skip: <reason>]` in the commit message). This prevents polluting the Rust source code with temporary bypass comments that will inevitably be forgotten and become permanent technical debt.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2865:*   **Q5 (Anti-Oreo Compliance)**: R-022 is a syntactic check; it cannot verify if a `top_white` symbol correctly claims an `FC3` role. However, the auto-generated § F reverse-map solves this. It creates an "Eventual Consistency" loop where the script enforces the *form*, and human/AI reviewers reading § F enforce the *substance*. This is a sound architectural compromise.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2877:    *   Change *"pipes to engine.py with `--rule R-022`"* to *"executes `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py --mode commit` directly. `engine.py` is bypassed for this specific rule because it requires cross-file diff awareness."*
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2879:    *   Change *"explicit `// R-022-skip: <reason>` on the same commit"* to *"explicit `[R-022-skip: <reason>]` token in the commit message"*.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2883:By decoupling the commit-time diff checks from the per-file `engine.py`, you preserve the simplicity of the original rule engine while allowing R-022 the multi-line, cross-file context it needs. 
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:2885:Once CO1.13 ships, the immediate priority should be monitoring the cycle time. If the 2-day wall-clock target is hit, the Elon-mode hypothesis is validated. If developers begin abusing the `[R-022-skip]` commit message token to maintain velocity, the quarterly audit will catch it, but we may need to elevate the escape hatch to require a valid `cases/Cxxx` reference sooner than anticipated.

Pre-commit hook R-022 enforces "every `pub` symbol in src/{top_white,middle_black,bottom_white,economy,state,transition,governance}/*.rs MUST have a `/// TRACE_MATRIX <id>: <role>` doc-comment". Build fails if missing.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3297:    43	| **CO1.13.2** | R-022 commit-time hook (v1.1: SHIPPABLE TRACKED FORM per Codex r1 P0-2): `rules/active/R-022_trace_matrix_pub_symbol_block.yaml` (declarative tombstone — engine.py BYPASSED for this rule per Gemini P0-G1) + `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` (multi-line context grep + diff parser) + **NEW v1.1: `scripts/hooks/pre-commit.r022` tracked shim** (NOT `.git/hooks/pre-commit` which is local-only per Codex P0-2) + **NEW v1.1: `scripts/install_hooks.sh`** (installer that symlinks tracked shim → `.git/hooks/pre-commit`) + **NEW v1.1: CI command `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py --mode ci`** (catches PRs where install_hooks.sh wasn't run; protects fresh clones + merges) + **NEW v1.1: engine.py 5-line patch** to gracefully ignore rules where `trigger == pre_commit` | ~250 LoC (script) + ~30 LoC (yaml) + ~30 LoC (tracked shim + installer + engine.py patch) = ~310 LoC | 1.5 day |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3339:    85	### 1.2 CO1.13.2 — R-022 commit-time hook (v1.1: ARCHITECTURALLY CORRECTED per Codex r1 P0-1+P0-2 + Gemini r1 P0-G1)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3341:    87	**v1 architectural mismatch** (round-1 P0): the v1 plan piped through `engine.py --rule R-022`, but `engine.py` only supports `grep` / `grep_inverse` / `compound` check types and is per-file (not diff-aware) per pre-edit `judge.sh` invocation. R-022 needs cross-file diff awareness which engine.py doesn't provide. v1 also placed the hook at `.git/hooks/pre-commit` which is untracked local state — doesn't protect fresh clones, CI, or merges (Codex P0-2).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3343:    89	**v1.1 corrected architecture**: pre-commit shim calls `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` DIRECTLY (engine.py BYPASSED for R-022). YAML rule becomes a declarative tombstone for the 30-rule cap + documentation. Tracked shim under `scripts/hooks/pre-commit.r022` + `scripts/install_hooks.sh` symlink installer + CI command for fresh-clone / merge protection.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3348:    94	rules/active/R-022_trace_matrix_pub_symbol_block.yaml    # declarative tombstone (engine.py bypassed)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3357:   103	id: "R-022"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3363:   109	fc_trace: "CLAUDE.md Alignment Standard — every NEW src/ pub symbol must have TRACE_MATRIX backlink AT COMMIT TIME (not retroactive)"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3369:   115	  invocation_note: "engine.py BYPASSED for this rule because R-022 requires cross-file diff awareness which engine.py per-file architecture does not provide"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3372:   118	message: "BLOCK (R-022 / Alignment Standard): NEW pub symbol(s) added under src/ without TRACE_MATRIX backlink (or REMOVAL of an existing backlink). See script output for specific locations. Either (a) add /// TRACE_MATRIX <FC-id>: <role> doc-comment in the immediately preceding doc/attribute block of each new pub symbol, (b) file in handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3.md § G with orphan justification (cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md required), or (c) include `[R-022-skip: <reason; cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md REQUIRED>]` token in the commit message."
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3379:   125	- Mode `--mode commit`: reads `git diff --cached`, identifies NEW pub items added in this commit vs base; for each, walks **immediately preceding contiguous doc/attribute/comment/blank-line block** (NOT raw 5-line window per Codex P0-4 empirical finding 86%/107 vs 92%/107 = semantic block walk wins) for `/// TRACE_MATRIX `; ALSO detects removals of existing TRACE_MATRIX lines (per Codex § C: R-022 should block removal too); falls back to TRACE_MATRIX_v3 § G orphan section lookup; falls back to commit-message `[R-022-skip: ...]` token (REQUIRES `cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*` reference per Codex P0-5 — silent skip rejected); structured logs every block/skip event to `rules/enforcement.log` with symbol/file/line/reason/staged-tree-hash/timestamp; exits 2 on any unjustified violation.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3389:   135	### 1.3 R-022 Scope Table (NEW v1.1; per Codex r1 P0-6 boundary cases)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3393:   139	| Pub style | R-022 enforcement | Rationale |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3401:   147	| Macro-generated pub items | **EXEMPT** if trace at macro-invocation site OR via `[R-022-skip: macro-generated]` commit-msg token | No stable source line for expansion; trace at the invocation. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3403:   149	| **Removal of existing TRACE_MATRIX line** | **BLOCK** unless commit-msg has `[R-022-skip: ...]` with justification | Codex § C: "R-022 should block ... removal of existing TRACE_MATRIX backlinks". |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3415:   161	### 2.1 R-022 enforcement boundary (v1.1: semantic block walk per Codex r1 P0-4 empirical finding)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3417:   163	R-022 fires on `pre_commit` when `git diff --cached` shows NEW pub-style declarations under `src/` (per § 1.3 R-022 Scope Table: `pub fn`/`struct`/`enum`/`trait`/`const`/`mod`/`type`/`static`, `pub(crate)` variants; exempts `pub use`, `#[cfg(test)]`, macro-generated). For each new pub line, `check_trace_matrix.py --mode commit`:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3422:   168	4. **If still not found**: search the staged commit message for `[R-022-skip: <reason; cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md REQUIRED>]` token (per § 2.2 v1.1); if found AND reason has a valid justification reference, PASS-with-LOG.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3425:   171	**Removal detection** (NEW v1.1; per Codex r1 § C "R-022 should block ... removal of existing TRACE_MATRIX backlinks"): script also detects `git diff --cached` deletions of `/// TRACE_MATRIX ` lines under `src/`. Each removal is BLOCKED unless commit-message has `[R-022-skip: ...]` token with valid justification. This prevents silent backlink decay.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3427:   173	### 2.2 R-022 escape hatch (v1.1: COMMIT-MESSAGE TOKEN + STRUCTURED LOG per Gemini r1 P0-G2 + Codex r1 P0-5)
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3429:   175	**v1 silent-bypass risk**: `// R-022-skip: <reason>` (Rust code comment) was permissive (no required justification reference; quarterly audit only). Codex r1 P0-5: "A hard gate needs structured logging at minimum: symbol, file, line, reason, staged tree hash, timestamp. I would also require cases/Cxxx, PREREG-§, or OBS_R022_*."
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3431:   177	**v1.1 escape hatch**: `[R-022-skip: <reason; cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md REQUIRED>]` token in the COMMIT MESSAGE (NOT a Rust code comment, per Gemini P0-G2: "prevents polluting Rust source code with temporary bypass comments that will inevitably be forgotten"). Structured logging mandatory:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3434:   180	2026-04-29T12:34:56Z R-022-SKIP commit=<staged_tree_hash> file=src/foo.rs line=42 symbol=pub_fn_foo reason="cases/C-xxx <description>"
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3438:   184	- No `[R-022-skip: ...]` token in commit message
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3442:   188	Quarterly audit reviews accumulated R-022-SKIP log entries; flags suspicious patterns.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3446:   192	R-022 is invoked at `git commit` pre-commit time, before the commit object is created. If BLOCK fires, the commit is aborted (exit 2) and the working tree state is preserved (developer can amend). No atomicity concerns since git's commit-or-abort is atomic.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3452:   198	| **I-FORWARD** | R-022 triggers on NEW pub symbols only; legacy untouched pub symbols exempt; legacy MODIFIED pub symbols WARN (not block) until CO1.13-extra | `tests/r_022_no_legacy_block.sh` (NEW v1.1: shell test, not Rust) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3453:   199	| **I-REMOVAL** | R-022 also blocks REMOVAL of existing TRACE_MATRIX backlinks unless escape-hatch justified | `tests/r_022_blocks_backlink_removal.sh` (NEW v1.1) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3454:   200	| **I-SCOPE** | R-022 scope table (§ 1.3) honored: pub use exempt, cfg(test) exempt, macro-generated exempt-with-token | `tests/r_022_scope_table.sh` (NEW v1.1) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3456:   202	| **I-LIST** | Active rules count ≤ 30 (docs/rules.md cap); CO1.13.2 lands R-022 as 16th rule (within cap) | `ls rules/active/*.yaml \| wc -l` |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3457:   203	| **I-ENFORCE** | R-022 enforcement actually blocks; integration test demonstrates a real temp-repo commit with missing backlink fails pre-commit | `tests/r_022_blocks_missing_backlink.sh` (NEW v1.1: shell integration test, not Rust per Codex P0-7) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3458:   204	| **I-LOG** | Every R-022 event (block, skip, pass) appends structured log entry to rules/enforcement.log | `tests/r_022_structured_log.sh` (NEW v1.1) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3463:   209	R-022 enforces **FORM**: every new pub symbol has a `/// TRACE_MATRIX <id>: <role>` doc-comment in the preceding block. R-022 is a SYNTACTIC check; it cannot verify whether the backlink correctly assigns the symbol to its true FC layer (e.g., a top-white symbol incorrectly tagged FC3 would pass R-022).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3465:   211	TRACE_MATRIX_v3 § F (reverse-map, auto-generated by CO1.13.3) provides **SUBSTANCE** review: every shipped symbol → its claimed TRACE_MATRIX row → human/AI reviewer can verify alignment correctness. § F is the "eventual consistency" loop — R-022 enforces form at commit time; § F enables substance review post-merge.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3467:   213	This two-layer model is acknowledged as a known limitation: R-022 alone cannot prevent constitutionally misaligned backlinks. Mitigations:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:3489:   235	### 3.4 `tests/integration/co1_13/r_022_skip_token_with_justification.sh`

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5473:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R13.md:596:Cumulative cost so far ~$70 (12 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 13 substantive findings caught + closed across 11 rounds**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5903:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11097:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3781:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5918:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11280:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3537:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:5930:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11307:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:4309:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3781:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.

A truncated/tampered WAL line cannot be detected; it just gets skipped. |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7449:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:3:**Status**: v1.1 (2026-04-29; round-1 = **CHALLENGE / CHALLENGE** per `CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md` + `GEMINI_CO1_13_ROUND1_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md`. 9 patches applied per merged verdict. Per Elon-mode 2-round-cap: this is the FINAL patch round before r2 ship-or-ship-with-OBS decision; per Codex r1 § E warning: "If round 2 still has non-enforcing R-022, do not ship-with-OBS; that would convert a hard alignment gate into theater" — R-022 itself MUST be enforcing at r2 or escalate to user). Wave 6 #2 PRE-CO1.8 per Elon-mode ROI analysis. User auto-execute mode authorization 2026-04-29.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7450:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:13:**Single sentence**: ship the 3-sub-atom factory bundle that closes the existing TRACE_MATRIX_v3 doc (currently § F empty + ~80 conformance test rows stub) + adds R-022 commit-time hook (blocks pub symbols without TRACE_MATRIX backlink) + boots the reverse-map population workflow — leaving generic scaffold scripts + Trust Root rehash automation to non-spec devtools (no audit gate; supporting `scripts/` deliverables landed alongside this atom).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7454:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:36:| 15 active rules R-001..R-020 | conventions for YAML schema; R-015 specifically as the pre-edit warn variant (CO1.13 keeps R-015 active; R-022 adds defense in depth at commit time) |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7456:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:43:| **CO1.13.2** | R-022 commit-time hook (v1.1: SHIPPABLE TRACKED FORM per Codex r1 P0-2): `rules/active/R-022_trace_matrix_pub_symbol_block.yaml` (declarative tombstone — engine.py BYPASSED for this rule per Gemini P0-G1) + `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` (multi-line context grep + diff parser) + **NEW v1.1: `scripts/hooks/pre-commit.r022` tracked shim** (NOT `.git/hooks/pre-commit` which is local-only per Codex P0-2) + **NEW v1.1: `scripts/install_hooks.sh`** (installer that symlinks tracked shim → `.git/hooks/pre-commit`) + **NEW v1.1: CI command `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py --mode ci`** (catches PRs where install_hooks.sh wasn't run; protects fresh clones + merges) + **NEW v1.1: engine.py 5-line patch** to gracefully ignore rules where `trigger == pre_commit` | ~250 LoC (script) + ~30 LoC (yaml) + ~30 LoC (tracked shim + installer + engine.py patch) = ~310 LoC | 1.5 day |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7460:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:63:2. **Ship-with-OBS NOT applicable to R-022 gate itself** (Codex r1 § E): "If round 2 still has non-enforcing R-022, do not ship-with-OBS; that would convert a hard alignment gate into theater. Ship-with-OBS is acceptable only for bounded edge cases with detection commands and expiry." → If R-022 doesn't actually enforce by r2, escalate to user — do NOT auto-ship-with-OBS.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7462:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:87:**v1 architectural mismatch** (round-1 P0): the v1 plan piped through `engine.py --rule R-022`, but `engine.py` only supports `grep` / `grep_inverse` / `compound` check types and is per-file (not diff-aware) per pre-edit `judge.sh` invocation. R-022 needs cross-file diff awareness which engine.py doesn't provide. v1 also placed the hook at `.git/hooks/pre-commit` which is untracked local state — doesn't protect fresh clones, CI, or merges (Codex P0-2).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7463:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:89:**v1.1 corrected architecture**: pre-commit shim calls `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` DIRECTLY (engine.py BYPASSED for R-022). YAML rule becomes a declarative tombstone for the 30-rule cap + documentation. Tracked shim under `scripts/hooks/pre-commit.r022` + `scripts/install_hooks.sh` symlink installer + CI command for fresh-clone / merge protection.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7468:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:118:message: "BLOCK (R-022 / Alignment Standard): NEW pub symbol(s) added under src/ without TRACE_MATRIX backlink (or REMOVAL of an existing backlink). See script output for specific locations. Either (a) add /// TRACE_MATRIX <FC-id>: <role> doc-comment in the immediately preceding doc/attribute block of each new pub symbol, (b) file in handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3.md § G with orphan justification (cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md required), or (c) include `[R-022-skip: <reason; cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md REQUIRED>]` token in the commit message."
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7474:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:163:R-022 fires on `pre_commit` when `git diff --cached` shows NEW pub-style declarations under `src/` (per § 1.3 R-022 Scope Table: `pub fn`/`struct`/`enum`/`trait`/`const`/`mod`/`type`/`static`, `pub(crate)` variants; exempts `pub use`, `#[cfg(test)]`, macro-generated). For each new pub line, `check_trace_matrix.py --mode commit`:
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7476:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:188:Quarterly audit reviews accumulated R-022-SKIP log entries; flags suspicious patterns.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7478:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:192:R-022 is invoked at `git commit` pre-commit time, before the commit object is created. If BLOCK fires, the commit is aborted (exit 2) and the working tree state is preserved (developer can amend). No atomicity concerns since git's commit-or-abort is atomic.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7487:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:308:**Pre-implementation gate**: spec must reach PASS/PASS (or PASS-with-OBS) before any code in `rules/active/R-022*` / `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` / `scripts/update_trace_matrix_reverse_map.sh` / `.git/hooks/pre-commit` is written. Per CLAUDE.md "Audit Standard". No STEP_B-restricted files touched (kernel.rs / bus.rs / wallet.rs UNTOUCHED).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7488:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:332:7. **FC-trace requirements for CO1.13 implementation**: `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py` + `scripts/update_trace_matrix_reverse_map.sh` are tooling, not src/ pub symbols; they don't need TRACE_MATRIX backlinks. The R-022 YAML rule itself is documented via `fc_trace:` field (already a YAML schema convention).
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7492:./handover/specs/CO1_13_TRACE_MATRIX_IMPL_v1_2026-04-29.md:386:- **P6** (Codex P0-6): boundary cases unspecified → NEW § 1.3 "R-022 Scope Table" codifies policy for `pub fn/struct/enum/trait/const/mod/type/static`, `pub(crate)`, `pub use`, `#[cfg(test)]`, macro-generated, signature-modified, backlink-removal.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:7698:./handover/specs/CO1_3_1_GIX_SPIKE_PREFLIGHT_v1_2026-04-27.md:25:| C8 | Hooks compatibility: pre-commit hooks work or are bypassable | R-018 + R-022 pre-commit hooks operate on user-facing repo; runtime_repo is separate; verify no interference | run a runtime_repo commit while user-repo has hook configured |

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8013:./handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:149:This reverse map is auto-generated by `scripts/check_trace_matrix_updated.sh` per Plan v3.2 CO1.13.2 atom. Pre-commit hook R-022 enforces "every `pub` symbol in src/{top_white,middle_black,bottom_white,economy,state,transition,governance}/*.rs MUST have a `/// TRACE_MATRIX <id>: <role>` doc-comment". Build fails if missing.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8020:./handover/alignment/WHITEPAPER_v2_TACTICAL_ALIGNMENT_2026-04-27.md:30:User explicitly chose to skip these heavy gates ("先不合并"). v2 instead acts as **the highest校准 mirror** for derivative documents, without forcing the alignment chain to be re-derived.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8088:**CHALLENGE-on-R-022-ENFORCEMENT-ESCALATE / HIGH conviction.**
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8090:v1.1 fixes the local-hook architecture, but not the fresh-clone / PR bypass. The spec adds `--mode ci`, but does not add a tracked CI workflow or required merge-gate invocation. In this repo there is no `.github/workflows/*`. So the concrete case fails: developer skips `install_hooks.sh`, pushes a missing-backlink pub symbol, and nothing in the tracked repo forces `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py --mode ci` to run on PR.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8101:- **P0-6 boundary cases: closed with one inconsistency.** Scope table lines 141-149 covers the cases. But macro row line 147 says `[R-022-skip: macro-generated]`, which conflicts with §2.2 lines 183-186 requiring a real reference.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8108:1. **R-022 CI enforcement is still not actually wired.** A CI mode is not a CI gate. Add a tracked workflow or equivalent required merge command as a deliverable.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8109:2. **TRACE_MATRIX §G collision.** Use `§ J — Orphan Extensions` or `§ G.1`, and update R-022 lookup accordingly. Do not overwrite existing Deferred Items §G.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8117:Minimum user decision: authorize a narrow cap exception for v1.1.1 that adds tracked CI enforcement and resolves the §G collision, or explicitly accept that R-022 is local-only unless hooks are installed.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8125:- fix the macro skip-token wording to require the same valid reference as §2.2
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_13_ROUND2_AUDIT_2026-04-29.md:8131:**CHALLENGE-on-R-022-ENFORCEMENT-ESCALATE / HIGH conviction.**

The spec adds `--mode ci`, but does not add a tracked CI workflow or required merge-gate invocation. In this repo there is no `.github/workflows/*`. So the concrete case fails: developer skips `install_hooks.sh`, pushes a missing-backlink pub symbol, and nothing in the tracked repo forces `scripts/check_trace_matrix.py --mode ci` to run on PR.

handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10491:The biggest bug: `fetch_add` happens before transition acceptance at lines 158-173. If `step_transition` rejects, no entry is committed, but the next accepted entry gets a skipped `logical_t`; skeleton writer and replay reject gaps ([transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:211), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:278)). Spec needs separate counters for submissions/rejections vs accepted ledger entries, or rejection entries must be first-class L4 entries.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10540:1. Fix sequencer logical time: no skipped accepted-entry `logical_t`, clear submit-vs-accept ordering.
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10706:| Codex | **CHALLENGE** | High | (1) sequencer logical_t skip race; (2) signing payload binds epoch + parent_ledger_root; (3) replay two-mode + CAS cold-replay |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10728:| **K1** | **Sequencer logical_t skip race** — `fetch_add` before accept skips on rejection; skeleton + replay reject gaps | Q-C top-1 | Either (a) separate counters for submissions vs accepted entries (assign logical_t at COMMIT time, not submit), or (b) rejection entries become first-class L4 entries with logical_t. Recommend (a) for v4 minimalism. |
handover/audits/CODEX_CO1_7_ROUND3_AUDIT_2026-04-28.md:10807:**Recommended**: **A**. Both auditors high-conviction CHALLENGE = real defects, not principle. v1.1 is a single concentrated patch round; skipping items will cost more in round-3+.

handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:82:3. (Codex R3#3) `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` returns success when `python3` is missing (soft skip via `eprintln + return`). For a VETO-closing conformance gate, missing python3 should fail closed.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:111:3. (Codex R4#3) `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring still says "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" — contradicts H6's fail-closed runtime.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:286:1. (**Codex R10#1 — substantive**) `_smoke_siliconflow.py` doesn't fail closed on missing keys: the script skipped unset secondary/tertiary and returned PASS if any configured key responded. The packet's "PASS (3/3 keys)" claim was strictly stronger than what the smoke verified — a primary-only setup would silently PASS, replicating exactly the V3L-27 single-key collapse pattern the 3-key pool was meant to mitigate.
handover/audits/A8_AUDIT_HISTORY_2026-04-26.md:379:Cumulative cost so far ~$80 (13 rounds × ~$5–7 — note A8e12 was a no-audit harness amplifier between R10 and R11); within memory `feedback_dual_audit` Phase A reservation. **Real-bug yield: 14 substantive findings caught + closed across 12 audited rounds + 1 PASS-only round (R13)**: 5 routing/correctness in R1 (run_id ms drift / FC1-N12 swarm gap / Qwen-HF misroute / TR count off-by-1 / "strictest" wording reversed) + 1 fail-closed-gate in R3 (H6 wrapper soft-skip) + 1 routing collision in R6 (deepseek-ai/* misroute) + 1 PREREG § 2 logic in R7 (M4) + 1 PREREG § 8 logic in R8 (N1 — parallel-text miss) + 2 in R10 (P1 smoke false-PASS / P2 runners not in TR) + 2 in R11 (Q1 boot.rs proxy not enforced / Q2 make_pput Option<...> divergence) + 1 in R12 (R2 search_cache stale after SEARCH_CAP) + 0 in R13 (documentary only — Codex CHALLENGE on Q3.c stale "37 entries" + this paragraph's stale "13/11" enumeration; Gemini PASS). Plus 1 false-closure caught at R9 (N3 claimed runner default fix that wasn't shipped) — counted separately as a delivery-quality finding, not a system bug. The recurring documentary CHALLENGE class persisted longer than expected because each round's fix touched documentation in ways that left adjacent staleness; the A8e7 structural rewrite addressed the root cause (category error) but its implementation needed two more cycles (A8e8 + A8e9) to fully complete the lineage strip + cross-section consistency. **A8e12** added case C-076 + rule R-020 (commit-claim diff parity) as a Living Harness amplifier specifically targeting the false-closure / parallel-miss class — pre-commit WARN reminds the committer to grep-verify every claim against the actual diff. **R13 is the asymptote signal**: zero substantive findings, only narrow documentary cascade items A8e14 R1 missed (this paragraph's enumeration + Q3.c). Two consecutive Gemini PASS rounds (R10 R13) over the 13-round span — the audit gate has reached the point where additional rounds yield mostly procedural drift, not new system bugs.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:34:**Finding**: No. The `CompleteSetRedeemTx` accept arm in `src/state/sequencer.rs:1852-1866` contains a nested `match` that strictly enforces consistency between the on-chain `market_state` and the transaction's `redeem.outcome`. A `TaskMarketState::Bankrupt` (NO outcome) paired with an `outcome=Yes` redeem attempt will fall through to the `(_, _)` arm and be rejected with `Err(TransitionError::InvalidResolutionRef)`. The integration test `sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no` (`tests/tb_13_complete_set.rs:199`) includes symmetric checks for this mismatch.
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:72:**12. Is the `ResolutionRef` model robust to multi-resolver scenarios in TB-15+?**
handover/audits/GEMINI_TB_13_SHIP_AUDIT_2026-05-03_R2_PRE_R3.md:74:**Finding**: Yes. The `ResolutionRef` struct (`src/state/typed_tx.rs:1019`) points to a `resolution_tx_id`. The sequencer logic (`src/state/sequencer.rs:1852`) currently hardcodes the accepted resolution transaction types (`TaskBankruptcyTx`, `FinalizeRewardTx`). To support a new resolver (e.g., a ChallengeCourt emitting a `CourtRulingTx`), the `match` statement can be extended. The model of referencing an on-chain, system-emitted fact is fundamentally robust and extensible.
handover/audits/EXT_CODEX_PHASE_8_R1ALPHA_2026-04-22.md:49:**Fix plan**: either (a) don't emit `PPUT_RESULT` at all on WAL failure (just stderr + early return), or (b) add `measurement_error: bool` field to PputResult and have make_pput skip emission.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:86:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. If the runner script's row-enrichment failed for any subset (e.g., python3 OOM'd on one row), would calibration silently miss data? Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:123:- (Q7.b) If any individual run hits the 40-min timeout, the runner skips with MEASUREMENT_ERROR. Does this artificially raise p_0 (by counting timeouts as treatment-UNSOLVED while control might have SOLVED)?

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3593:The main path confirms a few important facts: the treatment exits before the first prompt of tx 50, `synthetic_short_circuit` is only set at that return site, and the estimator currently skips missing calibration tags. I’m checking whether those facts cross the line from documented limitation into pre-batch blocker.

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3763:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:79:- (Q3.d) `compute_p0.py` skips rows missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed` — silent drop. If the runner script's row-enrichment failed for any subset (e.g., python3 OOM'd on one row), would calibration silently miss data? Should we fail loudly?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3769:./handover/audits/run_codex_b7_extra_prebatch_audit.sh:116:- (Q7.b) If any individual run hits the 40-min timeout, the runner skips with MEASUREMENT_ERROR. Does this artificially raise p_0 (by counting timeouts as treatment-UNSOLVED while control might have SOLVED)?
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3781:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3784:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:45:-   **(Q3.d) Silent drop in `compute_p0.py`**: **CHALLENGE**. The script at `compute_p0.py:56-58` silently skips rows that are missing `calibration_problem_id` or `calibration_seed`. A failure in the runner script (`run_p0_calibration.sh:170-177`) to stamp a row would cause that data point to be silently dropped, biasing the `p_0` result without warning. The script should fail loudly if any row from either input file is missing the required keys.

handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5372:   PREREG requires full adaptation-144 × seeds and denominator `/144` ([PREREG §5.5 lines 446-451](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:446)). But the runner swallows timeout/crash with `|| true` ([line 140](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:140)) and emits only `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` without a JSON row ([line 168](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:168)). The estimator skips missing tags ([compute_p0.py:55](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:55)), intersects only present pairs ([line 65](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:65)), and divides by observed problems ([line 91](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:91)). Fix: require exactly 144 problems, exactly 288 paired rows per mode, exact seeds `{31415,2718}`, no duplicates, no missing calibration tags, and abort on any `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` unless an explicit pre-registered retry policy handles it.
handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5408:   PREREG requires full adaptation-144 × seeds and denominator `/144` ([PREREG §5.5 lines 446-451](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/PREREG_PPUT_CCL_2026-04-26.md:446)). But the runner swallows timeout/crash with `|| true` ([line 140](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:140)) and emits only `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` without a JSON row ([line 168](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/run_p0_calibration.sh:168)). The estimator skips missing tags ([compute_p0.py:55](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:55)), intersects only present pairs ([line 65](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:65)), and divides by observed problems ([line 91](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/preregistration/scripts/compute_p0.py:91)). Fix: require exactly 144 problems, exactly 288 paired rows per mode, exact seeds `{31415,2718}`, no duplicates, no missing calibration tags, and abort on any `MEASUREMENT_ERROR` unless an explicit pre-registered retry policy handles it.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7633:.claude/worktrees/agent-a89c20c4/experiments/minif2f_v4/history.py:10:  - No GP    → PPUT = 0 (problem too hard for current harness, skip in early stage)
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:7747:.claude/worktrees/agent-a89c20c4/handover/ai-direct/CHECKPOINT_PHASE_2_2026-04-20.md:98:(a) is the cheapest test; (b) is the most constitutional. My recommendation is to **skip to Phase 4 next**. Phase 4 makes `append` rewards compound across problems, so the first agent to earn from a successful tape-path solve discovers it by balance growth on the NEXT problem. That's the real Hayek loop.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:8624:1. **R-018 is not airtight.** The hook handles `Bash` first and exits before any `constitution.md` file guard, so `python/perl/tee/sed -i constitution.md` style mutations are not blocked by R-018 ([judge.sh:19](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:19), [judge.sh:57](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:57)). The Edit/Write guard also requires nonempty `CONTENT`, so an empty replacement/delete edit can bypass the special case and fall through to the `*.md` skip-list ([judge.sh:16](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:16), [judge.sh:66](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:66), [judge.sh:87](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:87)). This contradicts C-071’s “不可绕过” claim ([C-071:63](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/cases/C-071_constitution_amendment_process.yaml:63)).
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:8655:1. **R-018 is not airtight.** The hook handles `Bash` first and exits before any `constitution.md` file guard, so `python/perl/tee/sed -i constitution.md` style mutations are not blocked by R-018 ([judge.sh:19](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:19), [judge.sh:57](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:57)). The Edit/Write guard also requires nonempty `CONTENT`, so an empty replacement/delete edit can bypass the special case and fall through to the `*.md` skip-list ([judge.sh:16](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:16), [judge.sh:66](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:66), [judge.sh:87](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/.claude/hooks/judge.sh:87)). This contradicts C-071’s “不可绕过” claim ([C-071:63](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/cases/C-071_constitution_amendment_process.yaml:63)).
handover/audits/CODEX_PPUT_CCL_AUDIT_ROUND2_2026-04-26.md:3023:  mid-batch, after which the "skip fee if insufficient balance" path kicks in and
handover/audits/GEMINI_PHASE_A0_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:42:    **CHALLENGE (High Severity)**. The logic in `.claude/hooks/judge.sh:58` (`[ "$(basename "$FILE_PATH")" = "constitution.md" ]`) is **NOT airtight**. It is vulnerable to a symlink bypass. An agent could create a symlink (`ln -s constitution.md my_other_doc.md`) and then edit `my_other_doc.md`. The `basename` would be `my_other_doc.md`, which would then match the `*.md` skip-list pattern at `judge.sh:75`, silently bypassing the R-018 sudo gate. This is a critical vulnerability that directly contradicts the mandate of C-071 and Art. V.1.1. The script must resolve the file's real path before performing the check.
handover/audits/run_gemini_phase_a0_exit_audit.py:30:- 4 smoke tests on judge.sh confirm: R-018 BLOCKS constitution.md edit / R-016 WARNS on git commit without FC-trace / silent pass on FC-traced commit / non-constitution .md still skipped
handover/audits/run_gemini_phase_a0_exit_audit.py:41:- (Q1.c) R-018 BLOCK depends on judge.sh special-case BEFORE the *.md skip-list. Path-routing logic (lines 50-67) airtight, or could relative path / symlink bypass?

Verify by reading the test source (appended to this packet) + checking that the panic message describes the failure rather than a silent skip.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5435:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:5436:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7448:handover/audits/GEMINI_PPUT_CCL_B2_B4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:64:*   **(Q4.a) Round-trip preserved**: **PASS**. All new fields in `PputResult` are `Option<T>` and use `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` (`evaluator.rs:82-93`). handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7464:handover/audits/run_codex_pput_ccl_b2_b4_audit.sh:147:  codex exec --skip-git-repo-check < "$TMP_PROMPT" 2>&1
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:7471:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8722:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6379:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:17:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8934:handover/audits/run_codex_phase_a8_exit_audit.sh:93:  codex exec --skip-git-repo-check < "$TMP_PROMPT" 2>&1
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8950:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:11097:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:3781:./handover/audits/GEMINI_B7_EXTRA_PREBATCH_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:23:-   **(Q1.c) Serialization of `synthetic_short_circuit`**: **PASS**. The implementation is correct. The field `synthetic_short_circuit` in `PputResult` (`evaluator.rs:142`) is an `Option<bool>` with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`. The default construction path in `make_pput` (`evaluator.rs:1252`) sets it to `None`, ensuring it is omitted for control runs. The treatment short-circuit path (`evaluator.rs:517`) explicitly sets it to `Some(true)`. This matches the smoke test verification.
