rules/active/R-015_trace_matrix_pub_symbol.yaml:4:  - "F-2026-04-25-04"  # B7 alignment retroactive fix — pub symbols shipped without TRACE_MATRIX backlink
rules/engine.py:120:        # trigger: "pre_commit" (e.g., R-022) are evaluated by their own
rules/active/R-022_trace_matrix_pub_symbol_block.yaml:1:id: "R-022"
rules/active/R-022_trace_matrix_pub_symbol_block.yaml:8:fc_trace: "CLAUDE.md Alignment Standard — every NEW src/ pub symbol must have TRACE_MATRIX backlink AT COMMIT TIME (not retroactive)"
rules/active/R-022_trace_matrix_pub_symbol_block.yaml:14:  invocation_note: "engine.py BYPASSED for this rule because R-022 requires cross-file diff awareness which engine.py per-file architecture does not provide. The pre-commit shim at scripts/hooks/pre-commit.r022 invokes the script directly. Engine.py treats this rule as a declarative tombstone (early-continue on trigger==pre_commit per CO1.13.2 5-line patch)."
rules/active/R-022_trace_matrix_pub_symbol_block.yaml:17:message: "BLOCK (R-022 / Alignment Standard): NEW pub symbol(s) added under src/ without TRACE_MATRIX backlink (or REMOVAL of an existing backlink). See script output for specific locations. Either (a) add /// TRACE_MATRIX <FC-id>: <role> doc-comment in the immediately preceding doc/attribute block of each new pub symbol, (b) file in handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md § J with orphan justification (cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md required), or (c) include `[R-022-skip: <reason; cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md REQUIRED>]` token in the commit message."
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:180:- (Q1.c) Does `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` fail closed when `python3` is missing? Verify by reading the test source (appended to this packet) + checking that the panic message describes the failure rather than a silent skip.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:299:3. (Codex R3#3) `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` returns success when `python3` is missing (soft skip via `eprintln + return`). For a VETO-closing conformance gate, missing python3 should fail closed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:328:3. (Codex R4#3) `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring still says "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" — contradicts H6's fail-closed runtime.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5459:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5460:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:5501:| `judge.sh` constitution.md special case | `.claude/hooks/judge.sh:50-67` | FC3-N3 sudo-gate enforcement (closes silent-bypass via `*.md` skip-list) | ✅ |

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:6103:   140	- (Q1.c) Does `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` fail closed when `python3` is missing? Verify by reading the test source (appended to this packet) + checking that the panic message describes the failure rather than a silent skip.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:6220:    82	3. (Codex R3#3) `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` returns success when `python3` is missing (soft skip via `eprintln + return`). For a VETO-closing conformance gate, missing python3 should fail closed.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:6249:   111	3. (Codex R4#3) `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring still says "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" — contradicts H6's fail-closed runtime.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:6601:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R5.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:7990:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8721:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R7.md:6378:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:16:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:8185:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R9.md:8950:handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R4.md:88:- **G1** (Codex R2#1 + Gemini R2#1, hardened by A8e3 H6): `experiments/minif2f_v4/tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` — Rust integration test that shells to `python3 scripts/test_llm_proxy.py` + asserts exit 0 + checks for the unittest "OK" trailer. Now exercised on every `cargo test --workspace` (PASS in A8e2/A8e3 verification). The Python suite is no longer "manual only" — it runs whenever the Rust tests run, which is every commit that touches Rust + every CI pipeline that already exercises Rust tests. **A8e3 H6 fail-closed**: missing `python3` is now a hard test failure (not a soft skip — Codex R3#3 caught the silent-pass risk). Explicit opt-out only via `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` and the bypass is logged loudly. Closes the round-2 "recurring conformance gate" finding.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9385:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:5922:handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5808:./handover/ai-direct/AUTO_RESEARCH_NOTEPAD.md:84:**TL;DR**: B7 commit shipped 4 new pub symbols (`verify_trust_root`, `parse_trust_root_section`, `TrustRootError`, panic site in main) without TRACE_MATRIX backlinks — violation of CLAUDE.md "Alignment Standard". User flagged. Fixed in commit `0cc48bc`: doc comments added with `/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34: ...` etc; TRACE_MATRIX_v1 written (FC3-N34 ⚠️→✅ promoted, 15 readonly-extension orphan rows with constitutional justification); OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT records that TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panic happens before kernel/bus init exists, so it's not a FC2-N22 HALT (no QState to mark Halted) — closer to FC3-E14 immediate-abort variant. **Lesson**: every src/ pub symbol MUST get TRACE_MATRIX backlink in same commit it's introduced. Treating alignment as "follow-up cleanup" leads to drift.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9386:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R2.md:7165:handover/ai-direct/AUTO_RESEARCH_NOTEPAD.md:84:**TL;DR**: B7 commit shipped 4 new pub symbols (`verify_trust_root`, `parse_trust_root_section`, `TrustRootError`, panic site in main) without TRACE_MATRIX backlinks — violation of CLAUDE.md "Alignment Standard". User flagged. Fixed in commit `0cc48bc`: doc comments added with `/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34: ...` etc; TRACE_MATRIX_v1 written (FC3-N34 ⚠️→✅ promoted, 15 readonly-extension orphan rows with constitutional justification); OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT records that TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panic happens before kernel/bus init exists, so it's not a FC2-N22 HALT (no QState to mark Halted) — closer to FC3-E14 immediate-abort variant. **Lesson**: every src/ pub symbol MUST get TRACE_MATRIX backlink in same commit it's introduced. Treating alignment as "follow-up cleanup" leads to drift.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9457:./handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R3.md:6027:The Rust wrapper exists and is in TRACE_MATRIX, but it soft-skips the gate if `python3` is missing. That may or may not be acceptable for a conformance gate; I’m checking the actual test result now and then I’ll decide whether that rises above residual risk.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9512:./handover/audits/CODEX_B7_EXTRA_ROUND4_AUDIT_2026-04-25.md:5808:./handover/ai-direct/AUTO_RESEARCH_NOTEPAD.md:84:**TL;DR**: B7 commit shipped 4 new pub symbols (`verify_trust_root`, `parse_trust_root_section`, `TrustRootError`, panic site in main) without TRACE_MATRIX backlinks — violation of CLAUDE.md "Alignment Standard". User flagged. Fixed in commit `0cc48bc`: doc comments added with `/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34: ...` etc; TRACE_MATRIX_v1 written (FC3-N34 ⚠️→✅ promoted, 15 readonly-extension orphan rows with constitutional justification); OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT records that TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panic happens before kernel/bus init exists, so it's not a FC2-N22 HALT (no QState to mark Halted) — closer to FC3-E14 immediate-abort variant. **Lesson**: every src/ pub symbol MUST get TRACE_MATRIX backlink in same commit it's introduced. Treating alignment as "follow-up cleanup" leads to drift.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9816:./handover/ai-direct/TAPE_ECONOMY_v1_2026-04-20.md:12:exists on "did you use tape?", so agents rationally skip it.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9838:./handover/ai-direct/EXT_AUDIT_PHASE_8_R1ALPHA/brief.md:72:If you see any regression from R1-α/R2/R3 changes on R4-R8 areas (e.g., halt_and_settle still working, reputation still wired, serde still deserializes), note it. Otherwise skip.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:9843:./handover/ai-direct/AUTO_RESEARCH_NOTEPAD.md:84:**TL;DR**: B7 commit shipped 4 new pub symbols (`verify_trust_root`, `parse_trust_root_section`, `TrustRootError`, panic site in main) without TRACE_MATRIX backlinks — violation of CLAUDE.md "Alignment Standard". User flagged. Fixed in commit `0cc48bc`: doc comments added with `/// TRACE_MATRIX FC3-N34: ...` etc; TRACE_MATRIX_v1 written (FC3-N34 ⚠️→✅ promoted, 15 readonly-extension orphan rows with constitutional justification); OBS_BOOT_FAIL_NOT_HALT records that TRUST_ROOT_TAMPERED panic happens before kernel/bus init exists, so it's not a FC2-N22 HALT (no QState to mark Halted) — closer to FC3-E14 immediate-abort variant. **Lesson**: every src/ pub symbol MUST get TRACE_MATRIX backlink in same commit it's introduced. Treating alignment as "follow-up cleanup" leads to drift.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12824:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:20:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12857:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:19:- A8e4 (post-A8e3, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-4 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini PASS — split verdict, conservative merge wins per memory `feedback_dual_audit_conflict`): three narrow documentary defects Codex found in the packet itself. **I1** packet title + date: "round 2" → "running through rounds 1–N" (cumulative, with reader pointer to most-recent round section). **I2** per-atom Trust Root deltas in § 3: A5 25→26 → 26→27, A6 26→27 → 27→28, A7 27→30 → 28→31 (all corrected to match the chain in `genesis_payload.toml` header — round-1 packet undercounted because A3's `agent_models.rs` had already raised the count to 26 before A5 landed); TRACE_MATRIX § 1 line 11 anchor-site location: "Six anchor sites wired in `run_swarm`" → "Six anchor sites wired (5 in `run_swarm` + 1 in `run_oneshot`)" — same in § 5 item 7 closure note. **I3** `tests/llm_proxy_python_conformance.rs` file header docstring: "if not, it skips with a clear diagnostic" → "if not, it FAILS CLOSED with a clear diagnostic" (A8e3 H6 fixed the runtime but left the docstring stale; Codex R4#3 caught the contradiction). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hash of the wrapper). No source-code defects, no architectural changes; this round is purely documentary alignment.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:12858:handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v2_2026-04-25.md:20:- A8e3 (post-A8e2, FIX-THEN-PROCEED after A8 round-3 audit Codex CHALLENGE / Gemini CHALLENGE — both convergent on packet staleness; no VETO): six narrow cleanup fixes. **H1** A8 packet § 3 A1 description: "Mathematically conservative" → "least-strict admissible value" with explicit Type-I implications. **H2** packet § 3 A6 description: 6 → 9 anchor sites (added F4 swarm sites explicitly). **H3** packet § 6 Q2.a / Q4.a / Q4.d marked CLOSED with closure rationale. **H4** `genesis_payload.toml` TR header phrasing about A1: "conservative ceiling" → "max-tolerated ceiling". **H5** § 5 item 7 here in TRACE_MATRIX: "(commit pending) + 6 wired" → "CLOSED + 9 wired". **H6** G1 wrapper test fail-closed on missing `python3` (was soft skip; Codex R3 caught the silent-pass risk); explicit opt-out `SKIP_LLM_PROXY_PYTHON_CONFORMANCE=1` for deliberate downgrades (logged loudly). Trust Root manifest unchanged at 34 (only re-hashes; no new files). Non-blocking observation deferred to Phase B+: `make_pput` signature was 21 positional args at round-3; A8e fix F1 added the `run_id` parameter, bringing the post-A8e count to **24** (Gemini R3 Finding 2 + Gemini R5/Codex R6 spot-checks; A8e6 K3 corrected here) — refactor to a builder pattern is recommended but not gating.

handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:14668:   The packet’s acceptance evidence is explicitly “PASS (3/3 keys)” and “3 keys × 1 probe” ([A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:49), [line 110](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:110)). But `smoke_siliconflow.sh` only requires the primary key ([scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh:35)), and `_smoke_siliconflow.py` skips unset secondary/tertiary keys then returns PASS if any configured key succeeds ([scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py:73), [line 84](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py:84), [line 90](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py:90)). I verified primary-only mode returns PASS. This undermines the A7 3-key V3L-27 mitigation gate.
handover/audits/CODEX_PHASE_A8_EXIT_AUDIT_2026-04-26_R10.md:14689:   The packet’s acceptance evidence is explicitly “PASS (3/3 keys)” and “3 keys × 1 probe” ([A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:49), [line 110](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/audits/A8_EXIT_PACKET_2026-04-26.md:110)). But `smoke_siliconflow.sh` only requires the primary key ([scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/scripts/smoke_siliconflow.sh:35)), and `_smoke_siliconflow.py` skips unset secondary/tertiary keys then returns PASS if any configured key succeeds ([scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py:73), [line 84](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py:84), [line 90](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/scripts/_smoke_siliconflow.py:90)). I verified primary-only mode returns PASS. This undermines the A7 3-key V3L-27 mitigation gate.

handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB-8_ATOM_1_CLAIMENTRY_TRACE_MATRIX_TEXT_EXTENSION_2026-05-02.md:14:src/state/q_state.rs — removed TRACE_MATRIX backlink: /// TRACE_MATRIX WP § 2 — claim entry shape (stub). Full fields land CO P2.6.
handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB-8_ATOM_1_CLAIMENTRY_TRACE_MATRIX_TEXT_EXTENSION_2026-05-02.md:19:R-022 expects every `pub` symbol in `src/` to carry a `/// TRACE_MATRIX <FC-id>: <role>` doc-comment. The hook detects 1 backlink "removed" — but in fact the line was **extended with TB-8 Atom 1 context** rather than removed. After the edit:
handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB-8_ATOM_1_CLAIMENTRY_TRACE_MATRIX_TEXT_EXTENSION_2026-05-02.md:36:This OBS doc serves as the auditable explanation for the R-022-skip token in the TB-8 ship commit message. No constitutional debt is created; no orphan symbol remains. Same WP § 2 anchor; richer doc-body reflecting the schema's TB-8 expansion.

handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:1:# OBS R-022 — TB-13 ResolutionRef removed (TRACE_MATRIX backlink retired)
handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:4:**Triggered by**: pre-commit hook R-022 (TRACE_MATRIX pub-symbol-block).
handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:5:**Removed symbol**: `pub struct ResolutionRef { resolution_tx_id, claimed_outcome }` in `src/state/typed_tx.rs`.
handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:10:ResolutionRef carried two dead fields:
handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:13:- `claimed_outcome: OutcomeSide` — a redundant copy of `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`. Used only by an inner-consistency gate `redeem.outcome != redeem.resolution_ref.claimed_outcome → InvalidResolutionRef`. That gate was dead defense-in-depth: the agent signature covers both fields, so any tampering that desynchronized them would break the signature first.
handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:19:The TB-13 typed-tx surface is still flowchart-anchored — `CompleteSetRedeemTx` itself retains its `TRACE_MATRIX TB-13 Atom 1 (architect §4.3 + FR-13.4..5 + SG-13.5..6)` backlink at `src/state/typed_tx.rs`. ResolutionRef was an internal helper type without an independent flowchart role. Removing the type cleanly removes the backlink — there is no orphan code; the role it carried (claimed-outcome assertion) is now expressed directly via `CompleteSetRedeemTx.outcome`.
handover/alignment/OBS_R022_TB13_RESOLUTIONREF_REMOVED_2026-05-03.md:25:- SG-13.6 (`sg_13_6_redeem_after_yes_outcome_pays_yes_not_no`): PASS unchanged — including both mismatch checks (Finalized + outcome=No → `InvalidResolutionRef`; Bankrupt + outcome=Yes → `InvalidResolutionRef`). The match arm using `(market_state, redeem.outcome)` produces identical rejection behavior to the previous `(market_state, claimed_outcome)` form because all fixtures set `claimed_outcome = outcome` (the inner-consistency check could never independently fire on an honestly-signed tx).
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:144:| `pub` items with TRACE_MATRIX backlink (immediately preceding doc-block) | 87 | per CO1.13 spec § 9 S7 baseline (semantic block walk per Codex r1 P0-4 measurement at 86%) |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:161:**Forbidden state**: any Normative row with empty "code symbol" or empty "conformance test" column. Pre-commit hook R-022 (added per Plan v3.2 **CO1.13.2** — corrects stale "CO P0.8" attribution) enforces.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:167:This section is the reverse-map: every `/// TRACE_MATRIX <id>: <role>` doc-comment in `src/` paired with the symbol (or variant/field) immediately following. It enables substance-side review (the form-vs-substance two-layer model per CO1.13 spec § 2.5): **R-022** at commit-time enforces *form* (every NEW `pub` symbol carries a backlink); § F enables eventual-consistency *substance* review (every shipped backlink → its claimed alignment row → human/AI verifier checks layer correctness).
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:172:- **Pre-commit hook R-022** (lands in **CO1.13.2**, NOT in legacy CO P0.8 attribution that v3 doc previously claimed) enforces: every NEW `pub fn / struct / enum / trait / const / mod / type / static` (and `pub(crate)` variants) under `src/` MUST have a `/// TRACE_MATRIX <id>: <role>` doc-comment in the **immediately preceding contiguous doc/attribute/comment/blank-line block** per CO1.13 spec § 2.1 (semantic block walk; Codex r1 P0-4 empirical 86 % accuracy vs 69.4 % under raw 5-line heuristic), OR be filed in § J orphan extensions, OR cite a `[R-022-skip: …]` token in the commit message with `cases/Cxxx | PREREG-§n.m | OBS_R022_*.md` justification per CO1.13 spec § 2.2. Commit aborts (exit 2) if missing.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:179:2. **Variant/field rows present but R-022 enforcement is at enclosing-symbol granularity** per CO1.13 spec § 1.3 R-022 Scope Table. The variant/field rows below carry their own backlinks (legacy practice; preserved); R-022 itself fires on the parent `pub enum` / `pub struct`. Variant/field backlinks pass form check via the parent's preceding doc-block.

handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:550:> **Purpose**: register `pub`-style symbols in `src/` whose constitutional alignment is real but does not fit the canonical § A/§ B/§ C/§ D row schema (e.g., a constitutional concept lives across multiple WP sections, or a symbol implements scaffolding/glue that supports the matrix but has no single canonical row). § J is the **non-row-bound fallback target** that R-022 falls through to in step 3 of its check (per spec § 2.1).
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:552:> **Authority**: § J is a constitutional-surface extension. Each row MUST cite a justification reference (one or more of `cases/Cxxx`, `PREREG-§n.m`, or a sedimented `OBS_R022_*.md`). The justification reference protects against the silent-bypass risk: any symbol that lands here without a justification reference is a violation; R-022 enforces this at commit time per spec § 2.1 step 3.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:554:> **Lifecycle**: a row is **opened** when an atom ships a symbol that R-022 needs to skip (e.g., a meta-tooling helper that supports the alignment regime itself), and **closed** by either (a) graduating into a canonical § A/§ B/§ C/§ D row in a subsequent doc revision when the atom matures, or (b) being deleted alongside the symbol.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:556:> **Audit cadence**: § J is reviewed during the quarterly R-022 enforcement-log audit (per spec § 2.2 "Quarterly audit reviews accumulated R-022-SKIP log entries"). Suspicious accumulation (≥10 entries new since prior quarter, or any row missing a valid justification reference) triggers a factory halt per Elon-mode OBS-threshold policy (CO1.13 spec § 0.5).
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:565:| **Justification ref** | yes | `cases/C-xxx`, `PREREG-§n.m`, or `OBS_R022_<topic>_<date>.md`. Must exist in repo at commit time; R-022 will reject if reference missing |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:574:| _(none registered)_ | — | — | — | — | — | § J is greenfield as of CO1.13.1 (2026-04-29). The shipped atoms covered by § F all map to canonical § A/§ B/§ C/§ D rows; no genuine orphan has been needed yet. R-022 will populate § J on first commit-time skip-token use that requires a non-row fallback target. |
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:582:### § J.4 R-022 fallback semantics (cross-reference)
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:590:If any of (1)/(2)/(3) fails, R-022 falls through to step 4 (commit-message `[R-022-skip: …]` token check). If both fail, R-022 BLOCKS the commit and appends a structured log entry per spec § 2.2.
handover/alignment/TRACE_MATRIX_v3_2026-04-27.md:592:— end of § J Orphan Extensions (CO1.13.1 initial schema; rows populated commit-by-commit via R-022 skip-token use).

## VERDICT: CHALLENGE

- TB13-Q9/RQ6 CHALLENGE: type-use discovery adds unmarked TB-13 files to effective scope, but the f64 / AMM / CPMM checks still scan only marker-derived `tb_13_spans()`. An unmarked file importing `CompleteSetMintTx` and using `f64` would be discovered but not fail SG-13.0.2. Refs: [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:126), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:224), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:281), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:340), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:377).
- TB13-RQ3 CHALLENGE: the smoke overclaims `final_state_root_hex == live state_root_t` as cryptographic proof of full non-empty TB-13 map equality. TB-13 accept roots hash `domain || previous_state_root || canonical_tx`, not full `QState`; this proves deterministic tx-chain replay, not byte-equal `conditional_collateral_t` / `conditional_share_balances_t`. Refs: [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:261), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:277), [tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:293), [README.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/evidence/tb_13_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-03/README.md:21), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:507).
- conviction: high
- recommendation: FIX-THEN-PROCEED

## VERDICT: CHALLENGE

- TB13-Q9/RQ6 CHALLENGE: type-use discovery adds unmarked TB-13 files to effective scope, but the f64 / AMM / CPMM checks still scan only marker-derived `tb_13_spans()`. An unmarked file importing `CompleteSetMintTx` and using `f64` would be discovered but not fail SG-13.0.2. Refs: [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:126), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:224), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:281), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:340), [tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_legacy_cpmm_forward_fence.rs:377).
- TB13-RQ3 CHALLENGE: the smoke overclaims `final_state_root_hex == live state_root_t` as cryptographic proof of full non-empty TB-13 map equality. TB-13 accept roots hash `domain || previous_state_root || canonical_tx`, not full `QState`; this proves deterministic tx-chain replay, not byte-equal `conditional_collateral_t` / `conditional_share_balances_t`. Refs: [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:261), [sequencer.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/state/sequencer.rs:277), [tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/tests/tb_13_chaintape_smoke.rs:293), [README.md](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/handover/evidence/tb_13_chaintape_smoke_2026-05-03/README.md:21), [transition_ledger.rs](/home/zephryj/projects/turingosv4/src/bottom_white/ledger/transition_ledger.rs:507).
- conviction: high
- recommendation: FIX-THEN-PROCEED
